Lichtenstein deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image instead of narrative.
ja.translated:
〈車の中で〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと太い黒の輪郭線が適用されています。
赤、青、黄の純粋な原色だけが漫画のひとコマのように画面を満たしています。

〈車の中で〉はリキテンスタインが漫画《ガールズ・ロマンス》のひとコマをカンヴァスに拡大した作品です。
原作にあった吹き出しを意図的に削除し、物語ではなくイメージの力だけを残しました。
ja.back:
The bendy dots and thick black outlines seen in "In the Car" are applied.
Only the pure primary colors of red, blue, and yellow fill the screen, as in a comic strip.

In the Car" is Lichtenstein's expansion on canvas of a scene from the comic strip "Girls' Romance.
The speech bubbles in the original were intentionally removed, leaving only the power of the image, not the story.

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narrative of speech bubbles in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flatly within thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, which sold for $30,000—five times the going rate.
It is now displayed as a centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.
ja.translated:
〈たぶん〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと吹き出しの漫画的な語りが適用されています。
太い輪郭線の中に原色が平面的に塗られ、印刷物の質感を再現しています。

〈たぶん〉はリキテンスタインが個人所蔵していた作品で、当時の相場の5倍にあたる3万ドルで売却されました。
現在はドイツ・ケルンのルートヴィヒ美術館でポップアート・コレクションの代表作として展示されています。
ja.back:
The cartoonish narrative of venday dots and speech bubbles found in <I think> is applied.
Primary colors are applied flat within thick outlines to reproduce the texture of printed matter.

Probably" was in Lichtenstein's private collection and sold for $30,000, five times the market price at the time.
It is currently on display at the Ludwig Museum in Cologne, Germany, as a representative work of the Pop Art collection.

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterwork created just after Lichtenstein's first solo show in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: "Is He the Worst Artist in the U.S.?"—about him.
ja.translated:
〈忘れて！〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと強烈な原色のコントラストが適用されています。
太い黒の輪郭線が形を漫画のように固定し、感情を劇的に圧縮しています。

〈忘れて！〉はリキテンスタインの1962年の初個展直後に制作された初期の代表作です。
同年、ライフ誌は「アメリカ最悪の画家か？」という見出しで彼を取り上げました。
ja.back:
Forget [Joon]! The venday dots and intense primary color contrasts seen in
Thick black outlines anchor the shapes in a cartoon-like manner, dramatically compressing the emotion.

Forget [Joon! is one of Lichtenstein's earliest major works, created shortly after his first solo exhibition in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine featured him under the headline "America's Worst Painter?" in the same year Life magazine featured him under the headline "America's Worst Painter?

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow separate cleanly within black outlines, as crisp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work Lichtenstein borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, overturning the value of what he once called "used canvas."
ja.translated:
〈ああ、わかった〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと平面的な色面構成が適用されています。
赤と青と黄色が黒の輪郭線の中で印刷物のように鮮明に分離しています。

〈ああ、わかった〉は漫画《シークレット・ハーツ》88号から借用した1964年の作品です。
2010年のクリスティーズのオークションで4,260万ドルで落札され、彼が「使い古したカンヴァス」と呼んだものの価値を覆しました。
ja.back:
The bendy dots and flat color plane structure seen in "Oh, I get it.
Red, blue, and yellow are separated by black outlines as clearly as in a printed matter.

Oh, I get it" is a 1964 work borrowed from the "Secret Hearts" comic strip #88.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, reversing the value of what he called a "worn canvas.

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in a comic-like manner, recreating the texture of print.

〈Still Life〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre as homage to Picasso.
"There's no mood in my still lifes—it's just lemons and grapefruits," he said.
ja.translated:
〈静物〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと太い輪郭線の平面的表現が適用されています。
原色の色面が対象を漫画的に単純化し、印刷物の質感を再現しています。

〈静物〉はリキテンスタインが1970年代にピカソへのオマージュを込めて伝統的ジャンルを再解釈した連作です。
「私の静物画にはムードなんてない。レモンとグレープフルーツがあるだけだ」と語りました。
ja.back:
The flat expression of Benday dots and thick outlines seen in Still Life is applied.
The primary color surfaces cartoonishly simplify the subject and reproduce the texture of the printed matter.

Still Life is a series of works in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted the traditional genre in homage to Picasso in the 1970s.
There is no mood in my still lifes," Lichtenstein said. There are just lemons and grapefruit.

ja.translated: ファン・ゴッホ画風

ja.original:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

Van Gogh left over 40 self-portraits, confronting his inner self most honestly through them.
The eyes gazing directly forward show unwavering will even amid suffering.
ja.translated:
ファン・ゴッホの絵の具を厚く塗り重ねるインパスト技法と渦巻く筆致が適用されています。
黄色と青の強烈なコントラストで内面の感情を表しています。

40点を超える自画像で、自らの内面を最も率直に見つめ続けました。
正面を見据える眼差しは、苦悩の中でも揺るがない意志を示しています。
ja.back:
Van Gogh's impasto technique of applying thick layers of paint and swirling brushstrokes is applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue expresses inner emotions.

In over 40 self-portraits, he continued to look most candidly at his own inner world.
The gaze looking straight ahead shows an unwavering will even in the midst of suffering.

ja.translated: クリムト画風

ja.original:
Klimt's real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on men's clothing contrast with curves and circles on women's, expressing both opposition and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decoration wrap the figure in a mystical golden world.
Within this decoration, sensuality and divinity coexist.
ja.translated:
クリムトの実際の金箔とビザンティン・モザイクから霊感を得た幾何学的パターンが適用されています。
男性の衣に直線の矩形、女性の衣に曲線と円で、対比と調和を表現しています。

クリムトの螺旋形の曲線と華麗な装飾が、人物を神秘的な黄金の世界に包み込んでいます。
その装飾の中で、官能と神聖は同時に存在しています。
ja.back:
Geometric patterns inspired by Klimt's actual gold leaf and Byzantine mosaics are applied.
The straight rectangles on the men's robe and the curves and circles on the women's robe express contrast and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decorations envelop the figures in a mysterious golden world.
Within the ornamentation, the sensual and the sacred exist simultaneously.

ja.translated: ムンク画風

ja.original:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors have been applied.
Blood-red skies and wavering landscapes visualize inner emotions.

Munch wrote in his diary that while walking by the Oslo fjord, he "heard an enormous scream pass through nature."
Before his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.
ja.translated:
ムンクの渦巻く曲線と歪んだ形態、強烈な色彩が適用されています。
血の色に燃える空と揺らめく風景が、内面の感情を視覚的に具現化しています。

オスロのフィヨルドを散歩中に「自然全体を貫く巨大な叫びを聞いた」と日記に記しました。
彼の絵の前に立つと、色彩そのものが感情の振動となって伝わってきます。
ja.back:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors are applied.
The blood-red sky and shimmering landscape visually embody inner emotions.

During a walk in the Oslo fjord, he wrote in his diary, "I heard a huge cry that penetrated the whole of nature.
Standing in front of his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.

ja.translated: マティス画風

ja.original:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors have been applied.
Rather than anatomical accuracy, focus is on the energy of movement, with color directly conveying emotion.

Just as Matisse erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room, he the boundary between table and wall, he chose harmony of color planes over perspective.
Before his canvas, colors sing out.
ja.translated:
マティスの単純化された形態と大胆な原色が適用されています。
解剖学的な正確さよりも動きのエネルギーに集中し、色彩が感情を直接伝えています。

〈赤い部屋〉でテーブルと壁の境界を消し去ったように、遠近法よりも色面の調和を選びました。
彼の画面の前に立つと、色彩が歌うように響いてきます。
ja.back:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors are applied.
Concentrating on the energy of movement rather than anatomical precision, the colors convey emotion directly.

He chooses harmony of color surfaces over perspective, just as he erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room.
Standing in front of his paintings, the colors seem to sing.

ja.translated: シャガール画風

ja.original:
Chagall's dreamlike atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel-like colors has been applied.
Bouquets, lovers, and hometown scenes blend together like memories, lifting the weight of reality.

Chagall composed his canvases with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space halt before love, and the world fills with dreamlike light.
ja.translated:
シャガールのピンクとコバルトブルー、宝石のような色彩で織りなされた夢幻的な雰囲気が適用されています。
花束と恋人、故郷の風景が記憶のようにおぼろげに溶け合い、現実の重さを取り去っています。

遠近法や解剖学ではなく、感情の論理で画面を構成しました。
彼の絵の中では、時間も空間も愛の前に立ち止まり、世界は夢のような光に染まります。
ja.back:
A dreamy atmosphere is applied, woven with Chagall's pinks and cobalt blues and jewel-like colors.
Bouquets of flowers, lovers, and hometown scenes meld into each other dimly, like memories, taking away the weight of reality.

He composed his paintings not with perspective or anatomy, but with emotional logic.
In his paintings, both time and space stand still before love, and the world is tinged with a dreamy light.

ja.translated: ピカソ画風

ja.original:
Picasso's Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Like Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple viewpoints coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, Picasso conveyed the terror of war immediately. This destructive deconstruction was itself creation.
Five centuries of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.
ja.translated:
ピカソの対象を幾何学的な面に分解し、正面と側面を同時に見せるキュビスムの技法が適用されています。
〈ドラ・マールの肖像〉のように、一つの顔に複数の視点が共存しています。

〈ゲルニカ〉では白と黒だけで戦争の恐怖を即座に伝え、破壊的な解体がそのまま創造でした。
500年続いた遠近法の規則は、彼のカンヴァスの上で粉々に砕け散りました。
ja.back:
Picasso's cubist technique of breaking his subject down into geometric planes and showing both the front and side simultaneously is applied.
As in "Portrait of Dora Maar," multiple perspectives coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, black and white alone immediately conveys the horrors of war, and the destructive deconstruction of the work is its creation.
The rules of perspective, which had been in place for 500 years, were shattered on his canvas.

ja.translated: フリーダ・カーロ画風

ja.original:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and communion with animals have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird, thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys as family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not mere backgrounds but companions offering comfort amid suffering.
ja.translated:
フリーダのメキシコの伝統色彩と熱帯の葉、動物との交感が適用されています。
〈荊の首飾りと蜂鳥の自画像〉で、荊は苦痛を、死んだ蜂鳥は失った愛を象徴しています。

子どもを持てなかったフリーダにとって、オウムや猿は家族のような存在でした。
彼女の自画像の中で、動物たちは単なる背景ではなく、苦痛の中の慰めを伝える伴侶です。
ja.back:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and animal communion are applied.
In "Self-Portrait with Briar Necklace and Bee Bird," the briars symbolize pain and the dead bee bird symbolizes lost love.

For Frida, who was unable to have children, parrots and monkeys were like family.
In her self-portraits, the animals are not mere backgrounds, but companions who convey comfort in the midst of pain.

ja.translated: リキテンスタイン画風

ja.original:
Lichtenstein's Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines, and primary colors have been applied.
Like the glamorous couple In the Car, every image becomes a comic book panel.

Lichtenstein's dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of comic heroines, everyday moments transform into dramatic scenes.
Emotion exploding within mechanical dots and lines—that is Lichtenstein's paradox.
ja.translated:
リキテンスタインの印刷漫画のベンデイ・ドットと太い黒の輪郭線、原色のみを使ったポップアートの技法が適用されています。
〈車の中で〉のグラマラスなカップルのように、あらゆるイメージが漫画のひとコマになります。

リキテンスタインの漫画のヒロインのドラマチックな表情と吹き出しのように、日常が劇的な瞬間に変わります。
機械的なドットと線の中で感情が爆発する、それがリキテンスタインのパラドックスです。
ja.back:
Lichtenstein's print cartoon technique of pop art, using only bendy dots, thick black outlines, and primary colors, is applied.
Every image becomes a comic strip, like the glamorous couple in "In the Car.

Everyday life is transformed into a dramatic moment, like the dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of Lichtenstein's cartoon heroines.
Emotions explode amidst the mechanical dots and lines, which is Lichtenstein's paradox.

ja.translated: フィンセント・ファン・ゴッホ（Vincent van Gogh, 1853〜1890）
後期印象派 · オランダ
苦悩の中に咲いた不滅
ja.back: Immortality blooming in the midst of suffering

ja.translated: フィンセント・ファン・ゴッホ（Vincent van Gogh, 1853〜1890）
星月夜 · ひまわり · 自画像
渦巻く筆先の情熱
ja.back: The passion of a swirling brush tip

ja.translated: 星月夜（The Starry Night）

ja.original: The night sky painted in swirls
ja.translated: 夜空を渦巻きに染め上げる
ja.back: The night sky is tinged with whirlpools

ja.translated: 夜のカフェテラス（Café Terrace at Night）

ja.original: A warm glow of yellow under starlight
ja.translated: 星明かりの下に輝く黄色い夜
ja.back: Yellow night shining under the starlight

ja.translated: ひまわり（Sunflowers）
太陽に似た花、情熱の色彩
ja.back: Flowers that resemble the sun, colors of passion

ja.translated: 自画像（Self-Portrait, 1889）

ja.original: Gazing into the mirror of the soul
ja.translated: 鏡の中の魂を見つめる
ja.back: Gaze into the soul in the mirror

ja.translated: 糸杉のある麦畑（Wheat Field with Cypresses）

ja.original: Golden fields dancing in the wind
ja.translated: 風にうねる金色の大地
ja.back: The golden earth undulating in the wind

ja.translated: グスタフ・クリムト（Gustav Klimt, 1862〜1918）
アール・ヌーヴォー · オーストリア
世紀末ウィーンの黄金の夢
グスタフ・クリムト（Gustav Klimt, 1862〜1918）

ja.original: The Kiss · Judith · The Tree of Life
ja.translated: 接吻 · ユディト · 生命の樹
黄金色の官能の世界
接吻（The Kiss）

ja.original: An eternal kiss dissolved in gold
ja.translated: 黄金に溶け込んだ永遠の口づけ
生命の樹（The Tree of Life）

ja.original: Golden branches singing the song of life
ja.translated: 黄金の枝が伸びゆく生命の歌
ja.back: A song of life with golden branches stretching out

ja.translated: ユディトI（Judith I）

ja.original: Between the sacred and sensual, golden allure
ja.translated: 神聖と官能の狭間、黄金の誘惑
ja.back: Between the sacred and the sensual, golden temptation

ja.translated: エドヴァルド・ムンク（Edvard Munch, 1863〜1944）
表現主義 · ノルウェー

ja.original: An artist of the soul, born from loss
ja.translated: 喪失が生んだ魂の画家
ja.back: Painter of souls born of loss

ja.translated: エドヴァルド・ムンク（Edvard Munch, 1863〜1944）

ja.original: The Scream · Madonna · The Dance of Life
ja.translated: 叫び · マドンナ · 生の踊り
内面の叫びを描く
叫び（The Scream）

ja.original: A soul's cry beneath a blood-red sky
ja.translated: 血の色の空に響き渡る魂の叫び
ja.back: A soul's cry echoing in the blood-colored sky

ja.translated: マドンナ（Madonna）

ja.original: A mysterious figure between life and death
ja.translated: 生と死の境に立つ神秘の女性
ja.back: Mysterious woman standing on the border between life and death

ja.translated: 生の踊り（The Dance of Life）

ja.original: A waltz of love and loss
ja.translated: 愛と喪失が紡ぐ人生の舞踏会
ja.back: A ball of life spun by love and loss

ja.translated: アンリ・マティス（Henri Matisse, 1869〜1954）
フォーヴィスム · フランス
色彩で喜びを歌う
アンリ・マティス（Henri Matisse, 1869〜1954）

ja.original: The Dance · The Red Room · The Green Stripe
ja.translated: ダンス · 赤い部屋 · 緑のすじ
色彩の魔術師
緑のすじ（The Green Stripe）

ja.original: A single green line that declared a color revolution
ja.translated: 緑の一本線で色彩革命を宣言する
ja.back: A single green line declares a color revolution.

ja.translated: 紫のコートの女（Woman in a Purple Coat）
鮮やかな色彩に包まれた優雅な佇まい
ja.back: Elegant appearance surrounded by vivid colors

ja.translated: 赤い部屋（The Red Room）

ja.original: A feast of decoration ruled by red
ja.translated: 赤が支配する装飾の饗宴
ja.back: A decorative feast where red reigns

ja.translated: ドランの肖像（Portrait of André Derain）

ja.original: A Fauvist colleague painted in the colors of the wild
ja.translated: フォーヴの色彩で描いた盟友の肖像
ja.back: Portrait of an ally painted in the colors of the Fauves

ja.translated: マルク・シャガール（Marc Chagall, 1887〜1985）
シュルレアリスム · ロシア／フランス

ja.original: A painter of 100 years of love
ja.translated: 百年の愛を描いた画家
マルク・シャガール（Marc Chagall, 1887〜1985）

ja.original: Lovers with Flowers · I and the Village · La Mariée
ja.translated: 花と恋人たち · 私と村 · 花嫁
ja.back: Flowers and Lovers - Me and the Village - The Bride

ja.translated: 愛と夢の詩人
花と恋人たち（Lovers with Flowers）

ja.original: Love glowing warmly within a bouquet
ja.translated: 花束の中に愛を込めた恋人たち
ja.back: Lovers with love in a bouquet

ja.translated: 花嫁（La Mariée）

ja.original: A bride floating between dream and reality
ja.translated: 夢と現実の間を漂う赤い花嫁
ja.back: Red Bride drifting between dream and reality

ja.translated: 私と村（I and the Village）

ja.original: Memories of home drifting like a dream
ja.translated: 故郷の記憶が夢のように流れゆく
ja.back: Memories of home flow like a dream

ja.translated: パブロ・ピカソ（Pablo Picasso, 1881〜1973）
キュビスム · スペイン
果てしなく変身した天才
パブロ・ピカソ（Pablo Picasso, 1881〜1973）

ja.original: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon · Guernica · Portrait of Dora Maar
ja.translated: アヴィニョンの娘たち · ゲルニカ · ドラ・マールの肖像
ja.back: Daughters of Avignon - Guernica - Portrait of Dora Maar

ja.translated: 視線を解体した革命家
ドラ・マールの肖像（Portrait of Dora Maar）

ja.original: A deconstructed portrait where front meets side
ja.translated: 正面と側面が共存する解体された肖像
ja.back: A deconstructed portrait in which the front and side coexist

ja.translated: フリーダ・カーロ（Frida Kahlo, 1907〜1954）
シュルレアリスム · メキシコ
壊れた体、折れない筆
フリーダ・カーロ（Frida Kahlo, 1907〜1954）

ja.original: Me and My Parrots · The Broken Column · Self-Portrait with Monkeys
ja.translated: 私とオウムたち · 折れた背骨 · 猿と自画像
ja.back: Me and my parrots - broken spine - monkeys and self-portraits

ja.translated: 痛みを見つめた自画像
私とオウムたち（Me and My Parrots）

ja.original: A self-portrait in solitude, with parrots
ja.translated: オウムとともに、孤独の中の自画像
猿と自画像（Self-Portrait with Monkeys）

ja.original: A portrait of pain, embraced by monkeys
ja.translated: 猿たちに包まれた苦痛の肖像
ja.back: A Portrait of Pain Wrapped in Monkeys

ja.translated: ロイ・リキテンスタイン（Roy Lichtenstein, 1923〜1997）
ポップアート · アメリカ

ja.original: Revolution that started with Mickey Mouse
ja.translated: ミッキーマウスから始まった革命
ja.back: The Revolution Began with Mickey Mouse

ja.translated: ロイ・リキテンスタイン（Roy Lichtenstein, 1923〜1997）

ja.original: In the Car · M-Maybe · Forget It!
ja.translated: 車の中で · たぶん · 忘れて
ja.back: In the car - maybe - forget it.

ja.translated: 漫画を芸術に変えた男
ja.back: The Man Who Turned Comics into Art

ja.translated: 車の中で（In the Car）
吹き出しのない沈黙、疾走する感情
たぶん（M-Maybe）

ja.original: A single word of hesitation, a Pop Art drama
ja.translated: ためらいの一言に詰まったポップアートのドラマ
ja.back: The drama of Pop Art packed into one word: hesitation.

ja.translated: 忘れて！（Forget It!）

ja.original: A farewell cry captured in a comic panel
ja.translated: 漫画のひとコマに込められた別れの叫び
ja.back: A Farewell Cry in a Single Frame of a Cartoon

ja.translated: ああ、わかった（Ohhh...Alright...）

ja.original: Resignation and acceptance in one breath
ja.translated: 諦めと受容のひと言
ja.back: A word of resignation and acceptance

ja.translated: 静物（Still Life with Crystal Bowl）

ja.original: Still life reborn through Ben-Day dots
ja.translated: ベンデイ・ドットで蘇る静物の世界
ja.back: Still Life World Revived by Benday Dot

ja.translated: ファン・ゴッホ

ja.original:
Van Gogh is a defining figure of Post-Impressionism.
He began painting at 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, yet only one sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces like The Starry Night, Sunflowers, and Café Terrace at Night were all born in poverty and mental anguish.

Van Gogh died at 37, but his art profoundly influenced Expressionism and Fauvism.
"I put my heart and soul into my work." — A painter who forged immortal beauty from suffering.
ja.translated:
ファン・ゴッホは後期印象派を代表する画家です。
27歳で絵を描き始め、わずか10年で900点の油彩画を残しましたが、生前に売れた作品はたった1点でした。
〈星月夜〉〈ひまわり〉〈夜のカフェテラス〉をはじめとする傑作は、精神病院と貧困の中で生まれました。

ファン・ゴッホは37歳で生涯を閉じましたが、その芸術はその後の表現主義とフォーヴィスムに多大な影響を与えました。
「私は作品に心と魂を注ぎ込む」 — 苦悩の中から不滅の美を創造した画家です。
ja.back:
Van Gogh is one of the leading painters of the Late Impressionists.
He began painting at the age of 27 and left 900 oil paintings in just 10 years, but only one of them sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces such as "Starry Moon Night," "Sunflowers," and "Café Terrace at Night" were created in a mental hospital and in poverty.

Van Gogh died at the age of 37, but his art had a profound influence on later Expressionism and Fauvism.
I pour my heart and soul into my work" - a painter who created immortal beauty out of suffering.

ja.translated: クリムト

ja.original:
Klimt was the leading Art Nouveau painter of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
Born the son of a goldsmith, he applied real gold leaf to canvas, creating an opulent decorative world inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
He left behind golden masterpieces where sensuality and divinity coexist, including The Kiss, Judith, and The Tree of Life.

Klimt founded the Vienna Secession, challenging the conservative art establishment.
"To every age its art, to every art its freedom." — A painter who captured fin-de-siècle beauty in gold.
ja.translated:
クリムトは世紀末ウィーンでアール・ヌーヴォーを代表した画家です。
金細工師の息子として生まれ、実際に金箔をカンヴァスに貼り、ビザンティン・モザイクから霊感を得た華麗な装飾世界を完成させました。
〈接吻〉〈ユディト〉〈生命の樹〉など、官能と神聖が共存する黄金色の傑作を残しました。

クリムトはウィーン分離派を創設し、保守的な美術界に挑みました。
「時代にはその芸術を、芸術にはその自由を」 — 黄金で世紀末の美を描き出した画家です。
ja.back:
Klimt was a leading Art Nouveau painter in fin-de-siècle Vienna.
The son of a goldsmith, Klimt actually applied gold leaf to his canvases, creating a world of decorative splendor inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
He left behind golden masterpieces such as "The Kiss," "Judith," and "The Tree of Life," in which sensuality and the sacred coexist.

Klimt founded the Vienna Secession and challenged the conservative art world.
Klimt was a painter who used gold to depict the beauty of the fin de siècle: "Let the age have its art, and art have its freedom.

ja.translated: ムンク

ja.original:
Munch is the defining Expressionist painter from Norway.
He lost his mother at five and his sister at fourteen, and the memory of these losses dominated his canvases for life.
His works, including The Scream, Madonna, and The Dance of Life, form "The Frieze of Life" — a cycle of love, death, and anxiety.

Munch declared, "I paint not what I see, but what I saw,", exploring the deepest human emotions.
"Illness, madness, and death were the dark angels that watched over my cradle." — A painter who transformed inner darkness into art.
ja.translated:
ムンクは表現主義を代表するノルウェーの画家です。
5歳で母を、14歳で姉を失い、この喪失の記憶が生涯にわたり彼のカンヴァスを支配しました。
〈叫び〉〈マドンナ〉〈生の踊り〉をはじめとする作品は、愛と死と不安を描いた「生命のフリーズ」連作です。

ムンクは「私は見えるものではなく、かつて見たものを描く」と語り、人間の根源的な感情を探求しました。
「病と狂気と死は、私の揺りかごを守った黒い天使たちだった」 — 内面の闇を芸術に昇華させた画家です。
ja.back:
Munch was a leading Norwegian Expressionist painter.
He lost his mother at the age of 5 and his sister at 14, and the memory of these losses dominated his canvases for the rest of his life.
His works, including "The Scream," "Madonna," and "Dance of Life," are a series of "life friezes" depicting love, death, and anxiety.

Munch explored fundamental human emotions, saying, "I paint not what I see, but what I once saw.
Disease, madness, and death were the black angels that guarded my cradle" - a painter who sublimated inner darkness into art.

ja.translated: マティス

ja.original:
Matisse is the leading Fauvist painter of France.
At 21, he began painting with a box of colors his mother gave him while bedridden, later recalling it felt like "discovering paradise."
The 1905 cry of "Wild beasts!" became the birth of Fauvism, and he left behind color-revolutionary masterpieces like The Dance, The Green Stripe, and The Red Room.

Matisse conveyed emotion through pure color alone, pushing the decorative potential of painting to its limit.
"The purpose of color is not to describe form but to express emotion." — A painter who sang joy through color.
ja.translated:
マティスはフォーヴィスムを代表するフランスの画家です。
21歳の時、病床で母から渡された絵の具箱で絵を描き始め、「天国を見つけたようだった」と回想しました。
1905年、「野獣だ！」という非難がそのままフォーヴィスムの始まりとなり、〈ダンス〉〈緑のすじ〉〈赤い部屋〉など色彩革命の傑作を残しました。

マティスは純粋な色彩だけで感情を伝え、絵画の装飾的可能性を最後まで追い続けました。
「色彩の目的は形ではなく、感情を表現することだ」 — 色彩で喜びを歌った画家です。
ja.back:
Matisse was a leading French painter of the Fauvisme.
At the age of 21, on his sickbed, he began painting with a box of paints given to him by his mother and recalled, "It was as if I had found heaven."
In 1905, "A beast!" was the beginning of Fauvism, and he left behind such masterpieces of the color revolution as "Dance," "The Green Streak," and "The Red Room.

Matisse used only pure colors to convey emotion, and he continued to pursue the decorative potential of painting until the end of his career.
The purpose of color is not to express form, but to express emotion" - Matisse sang of joy through color.

ja.translated: シャガール

ja.original:
Chagall was a painter of love and dreams.
Born in a Russian Jewish village, he moved to Paris and never put down his brush through two World Wars and exile.
His love for his wife Bella was a lifelong source of his art, leaving behind masterpieces like The Birthday, La Mariée, and I and the Village — canvases where bouquets and lovers float in midair.

Chagall worked until 97, enchanting the world with the Paris Opéra ceiling and stained glass windows.
"In our life there is a single color, as on an artist's palette, which provides the meaning of life and art — the color of love." — A poet who erased the boundary between dream and reality.
ja.translated:
シャガールは愛と夢を画布に描いた画家です。
ロシアのユダヤ人の村に生まれパリへ渡り、二度の世界大戦と亡命の中でも筆を置きませんでした。
妻ベラとの愛が生涯にわたり芸術の源泉であり、〈恋人たち〉〈花嫁〉〈私と村〉など花束と恋人が宙に浮かぶ傑作を残しました。

シャガールは97歳まで制作を続け、パリ・オペラ座の天井画やステンドグラスでも世界を魅了しました。
「人生にはたった一つの色がある。それは愛の色だ」 — 現実と夢の境界を消した夢幻の詩人です。
ja.back:
Chagall was a painter of love and dreams on canvas.
Born in a Russian Jewish village, Chagall moved to Paris, and despite two world wars and exile, he never put down his brush.
The love he shared with his wife Bella remained the source of his art throughout his life, and he left behind masterpieces such as "The Lovers," "The Bride," and "Me and My Village," in which bouquets of flowers and lovers float in the air.

Chagall continued to work until he was 97 years old, and his ceiling paintings and stained glass windows at the Paris Opera fascinated the world.
There is only one color in life, and that is the color of love. He was a dreamy poet who erased the boundary between reality and dreams.

ja.translated: ピカソ

ja.original:
Picasso co-founded Cubism and changed the direction of modern art.
In his 91-year life, he left approximately 50,000 works. Legend has it that at 13, his art teacher father saw his talent and broke his own brushes.
He opened Cubism with Les Demoiselles d'Avignon, denounced war with Guernica, and reached the pinnacle of deconstruction with Portrait of Dora Maar.

Through Blue Period, Rose Period, Cubism, and Surrealism, Picasso constantly reinvented himself.
"Good artists copy, great artists steal." — A painter who broke all rules and created new ones.
ja.translated:
ピカソはキュビスムを創始し、現代美術の方向を変えた革命家です。
91年の生涯で約5万点の作品を残し、13歳の時に美術教師だった父がその才能を見て自ら筆を折ったという逸話はその天才性を物語っています。
〈アヴィニョンの娘たち〉でキュビスムを開き、〈ゲルニカ〉で戦争の惨状を告発し、〈ドラ・マールの肖像〉で解体と再構成の頂点を示しました。

ピカソは青の時代、薔薇色の時代、キュビスム、シュルレアリスムを経て、絶えず自らを再創造しました。
「優れた芸術家は模倣し、偉大な芸術家は盗む」 — すべての規則を壊し新たな規則を作り上げた画家です。
ja.back:
Picasso was a revolutionary who founded cubism and changed the direction of contemporary art.
He left approximately 50,000 works of art during his 91-year life, and the anecdote that his father, an art teacher, broke his own brush when he saw his talent at the age of 13 is testimony to his genius.
In "Daughters of Avignon," he opened up cubism, in "Guernica," he denounced the horrors of war, and in "Portrait of Dora Maar," he showed the pinnacle of deconstruction and reassembly.

Picasso continually reinvented himself through the Blue Period, the Rose Period, Cubism, and Surrealism.
A good artist imitates, a great artist steals" - a painter who broke all the rules and created new ones.

ja.translated: フリーダ・カーロ

ja.original:
Frida Kahlo is Mexico's most original painter and a feminist icon.
She contracted polio at 6 and at 18 suffered a bus accident that shattered her spine and pelvis, enduring over 30 surgeries throughout her life.
She began painting while bedridden, looking at a mirror on the ceiling — and much of her work, including Me and My Parrots, The Broken Column, and Self-Portrait with Monkeys, are self-portraits.

Frida's art, with its honest portrayal of pain, love, and identity, continues to resonate deeply today.
"Feet, what do I need them for if I have wings to fly?" — A painter who transformed pain into art.
ja.translated:
フリーダ・カーロはメキシコが生んだ最も独創的な画家であり、フェミニズムのアイコンです。
6歳で小児麻痺を患い、18歳でバス事故により脊椎と骨盤が砕け、生涯で30回以上の手術に耐えなければなりませんでした。
ベッドに横たわったまま天井の鏡を見て自分を描き始め、〈私とオウムたち〉〈折れた背骨〉〈猿と自画像〉など彼女の作品の多くが自画像です。

フリーダは苦痛と愛、アイデンティティをカンヴァスに率直に描き出した彼女の作品世界は、今日もなお深い共感を呼んでいます。
「足があって何になるの、翼があるのに」 — 苦痛を芸術に昇華させた不屈の画家です。
ja.back:
Frida Kahlo is one of the most original painters and feminist icons to come out of Mexico.
She suffered from infantile paralysis at the age of six, had her spine and pelvis shattered in a bus accident at the age of 18, and had to endure more than 30 surgeries during her lifetime.
Lying in bed, she began drawing herself in the mirror on the ceiling, and many of her works, including "Me and My Parrots," "Broken Spine," and "Monkey and Self-Portrait," are self-portraits.

Frida's candid depictions of pain, love, and identity on canvas remain deeply resonant today.
What's the use of having legs when you have wings?

ja.translated: リキテンスタイン

ja.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who broke the boundaries of fine art with comic book panels.
When his son pointed to a Mickey Mouse comic and asked, "Can you draw as well as this, Dad?", he actually began transferring comics to canvas.
With In the Car, M-Maybe, and Forget It!, he shook the art world by blowing up comic panels onto massive canvases.

Lichtenstein, with Ben-Day dots and bold outlines, overturned the hierarchy between popular culture and high art.
"I don't draw comics, I paint pictures about comics." — A painter who erased the line between pop culture and fine art.
ja.translated:
リキテンスタインは漫画のひとコマで純粋芸術の境界を壊したポップアートの巨匠です。
息子が読んでいたミッキーマウスの漫画を指さして「パパもこんなに上手に描ける？」と聞いたことがきっかけで、本当に漫画をカンヴァスに移し始めました。
〈車の中で〉〈たぶん〉〈忘れて！〉など漫画の一場面を巨大なカンヴァスに移し、美術界を揺るがしました。

リキテンスタインはベンデイ・ドットと太い輪郭線で大衆文化と芸術のヒエラルキーを覆しました。
「私は漫画を描いているのではない。漫画を題材に絵を描いているのだ」 — 大衆文化と純粋芸術の境界を消した画家です。
ja.back:
Lichtenstein was a master of Pop Art who broke the boundaries of pure art with a single comic strip.
Pointing to a Mickey Mouse cartoon his son was reading, Lichtenstein asked, "Can you draw this well too, Daddy?" That's when he really started transferring his cartoons to canvas.
He started to transfer cartoon scenes such as "In the Car," "Maybe," and "Forget It! and other cartoon scenes onto giant canvases, shaking up the art world.

Lichtenstein subverted the hierarchy of popular culture and art with his Benday dots and bold outlines.
I don't draw cartoons," Lichtenstein said. Lichtenstein was a painter who erased the boundaries between popular culture and pure art.

ja.original:
The swirling impasto brushstrokes of 〈The Starry Night〉 have been applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow unleashes the energy of the night.

〈The Starry Night〉 was Van Gogh's reimagined vision of the landscape beyond his asylum window at Saint-Rémy.
Rather than painting the actual night sky, he projected his inner turmoil onto the stars.
ja.translated:
〈星月夜〉に見られる渦巻くインパストの筆致が適用されています。
コバルトブルーとクロムイエローの強烈なコントラストが、夜のエネルギーを爆発させています。

〈星月夜〉はファン・ゴッホがサン＝レミの精神病院の窓越しの風景を想像で再構成した作品です。
実際の夜空を描いたのではなく、内面の渦巻きを星に投影したものです。
ja.back:
The swirling impasto brushstrokes seen in <Night of Stars and Moon> are applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow explodes with the energy of the night.

In "Starry Moonlit Night," van Gogh imaginatively reconstructed the scene through the window of the asylum in Saint-Rémy.
It is not a depiction of the actual night sky, but a projection of the inner whirlpools onto the stars.

ja.original:
The complementary color nightscape technique of 〈Café Terrace at Night〉—without using black—has been applied.
Yellow gaslights and deep blue night sky express darkness through color contrast alone.

〈Café Terrace at Night〉 is the first outdoor night scene Van Gogh painted under starlight at Arles' Place du Forum.
The empty chairs on the terrace and passing figures hold both the stillness and warmth of the night.
ja.translated:
〈夜のカフェテラス〉に見られる黒を使わない補色対比の夜景技法が適用されています。
黄色いガス灯と深い青の夜空が、色の対比だけで闇を表現しています。

〈夜のカフェテラス〉はファン・ゴッホがアルルで描いた、黒を一切使わない夜景画です。
カフェの温かな黄色と空の深い青が、補色だけで夜の世界を完成させています。
ja.back:
The night scene technique of contrasting complementary colors without using black, as seen in "Cafe Terrace at Night," is applied.
The yellow gas lamps and the deep blue night sky express darkness through color contrast alone.

Café Terrace at Night is a night scene painted in Arles by van Gogh that uses no black at all.
The warm yellow of the café and the deep blue of the sky complete the world of night with only complementary colors.

ja.original:
The subtle tonal variations of a single chrome yellow in 〈Sunflowers〉 have been applied.
Thickly layered paint captures the texture of the petals as they are.

〈Sunflowers〉 is a series Van Gogh painted to decorate the Yellow House in Arles for Gauguin's arrival.
Full blooms, wilted flowers, and seeds together reveal the cycle of life.
ja.translated:
〈ひまわり〉に見られる厚塗りインパストと黄色の色調変化が適用されています。
クロムイエローからオークルまで、黄色だけで画面全体を満たし生命力を表現しています。

〈ひまわり〉はファン・ゴッホがアルルの黄色い家を飾るために描いた連作です。
花瓶のひまわりは南仏の太陽そのものであり、友人ゴーギャンを迎えるための歓迎の花でした。
ja.back:
The thick impasto and yellow tonal shifts seen in "Sunflowers" are applied.
From chrome yellow to ochre, yellow alone fills the entire image and expresses vitality.

Sunflowers" is a series of paintings by van Gogh to decorate the Yellow House in Arles.
The sunflowers in the vase are the very sun of the south of France, and were the welcoming flowers for his friend Gauguin.

ja.original:
The swirling background and thick impasto brushstrokes of 〈Self-Portrait〉 have been applied.
Cool tones of blue and green convey both solitude and resolve.

〈Self-Portrait〉 was painted when Van Gogh, too poor to afford models, used himself as subject.
The eyes gazing directly forward and tightly closed lips show unwavering will amid suffering.
ja.translated:
〈自画像〉に見られる方向性のある筆致と補色対比の心理的表現が適用されています。
背景の青い渦巻きと顔の暖色系の色彩が互いにぶつかり、内面の葛藤を浮かび上がらせています。

〈自画像〉はファン・ゴッホがモデルを雇えない貧困の中で自らを対象として描いた作品です。
正面を見据える眼差しと固く結んだ口が、苦悩の中でも揺るがない意志を示しています。
ja.back:
The directional brushstrokes and the psychological expression of complementary color contrasts seen in "Self-Portrait" are applied.
The blue swirls in the background and the warm colors of the face clash with each other, revealing the artist's inner conflict.

Self-Portrait" is a work in which van Gogh painted himself as a subject in the midst of poverty, unable to hire a model.
The eyes looking straight ahead and the tightly knit mouth show an unwavering will in the midst of suffering.

ja.original:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of 〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 have been applied.
Wind-blown wheat and flame-like cypresses convey nature's vital energy.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 is a landscape Van Gogh repeatedly painted near the asylum at Saint-Rémy.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the vertical cypress connect earth and sky.
ja.translated:
〈糸杉のある麦畑〉に見られる躍動的な曲線の筆致と補色対比が適用されています。
風にうねる麦と炎のようにそびえる糸杉が、自然の生命力を伝えています。

〈糸杉のある麦畑〉はファン・ゴッホがサン＝レミの精神病院近くで繰り返し描いた風景です。
黄金色の麦畑の水平線と垂直にそびえる糸杉が、大地と空を結んでいます。
ja.back:
The dynamic curving strokes and complementary color contrasts seen in "Wheat Field with Cypresses" are applied.
The wheat undulating in the wind and the cypresses soaring like flames convey the vitality of nature.

Wheat Field with Cedars" is a scene that van Gogh repeatedly painted near the mental hospital in Saint-Rémy.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the perpendicular cypresses connect the earth to the sky.

ja.original:
The gold leaf background and geometric decorative patterns of 〈The Kiss〉 have been applied.
Rectangles on the man's robe contrast with curves and circles on the woman's, creating opposition and harmony.

〈The Kiss〉 is Klimt's masterpiece from his "Golden Phase," inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The lovers' embrace wrapped in gold holds both the ecstasy of love and the anxiety of its passing.
ja.translated:
〈接吻〉に見られる金箔の背景と幾何学的な装飾パターンが適用されています。
男性の衣に直線の矩形、女性の衣に曲線と円形が、対比と調和を生み出しています。

〈接吻〉はクリムトがビザンティン・モザイクに霊感を得て「黄金時代」に描いた代表作です。
金箔に包まれた恋人たちの抱擁が、愛の恍惚と消滅の不安を同時にたたえています。
ja.back:
The gilded background and geometric decorative patterns seen in "The Kiss" are applied.
The straight rectangles on the man's robe and the curved lines and circles on the woman's robe create contrast and harmony.

The Kiss is one of Klimt's masterpieces from the "Golden Age," inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The embrace of the lovers, wrapped in gold leaf, simultaneously celebrates the ecstasy of love and the anxiety of annihilation.

ja.original:
The spiral curves and golden decorative patterns of 〈The Tree of Life〉 have been applied.
Triangles, circles, and spirals repeat, creating a rhythmic flow of life's energy.

〈The Tree of Life〉 was created as a dining room mural for the Stoclet Palace in Brussels.
The endlessly branching spiral limbs are a metaphor for birth, death, and the cycle of life.
ja.translated:
〈生命の樹〉に見られる螺旋形の曲線と黄金色の装飾パターンが適用されています。
三角形、円形、渦巻きが繰り返され、生命のエネルギーがリズミカルに流れています。

〈生命の樹〉はクリムトがブリュッセルのストクレー邸の食堂壁画として制作した装飾画です。
果てしなく伸びゆく螺旋形の枝が、誕生と消滅、生の循環を暗示しています。
ja.back:
The spiral curves and golden ornamentation patterns found in the Tree of Life are applied.
Triangles, circles, and spirals are repeated, and the energy of life flows rhythmically.

The Tree of Life is a decorative mural that Klimt created for the dining room of the Stoclet residence in Brussels.
The endless spiraling branches suggest birth and extinction, the cycle of life.

ja.original:
The gold leaf decoration and jewel-like sensual colors of 〈Judith〉 have been applied.
Transparent veils and gold necklaces create tension between the sacred and the sensual.

〈Judith〉 is Klimt's reinterpretation of the biblical heroine as a sensual woman.
Half-closed eyes and a provocative smile completely overturn the piety of traditional religious painting.
ja.translated:
〈ユディト〉に見られる金箔装飾と宝石のような官能的な色彩が適用されています。
透明なヴェールと金の首飾りが、神聖さと官能の間の緊張感を生み出しています。

〈ユディト〉はクリムトが聖書の英雄を官能的な女性として再解釈した作品です。
半ば閉じた目と挑発的な微笑みが、従来の宗教画の厳粛さを完全に覆しています。
ja.back:
The gilded decoration and jewel-like sensual colors of Judith are applied.
The transparent veil and gold necklace create a tension between the sacred and the sensual.

Judith is Klimt's reinterpretation of the biblical hero as a voluptuous woman.
Her half-closed eyes and provocative smile completely subvert the solemnity of traditional religious painting.

ja.original:
The undulating curves and distorted forms of 〈The Scream〉 have been applied.
A blood-red sky and rippling background contrast with the straight railing, creating extreme anxiety.

〈The Scream〉 captures the terror Munch felt when the sky turned blood-red during a walk.
The open-mouthed figure's cry is expressed not as sound but as a tremor passing through all existence.
ja.translated:
〈叫び〉に見られるうねる曲線と歪んだ形態が適用されています。
血の色の空と揺らめく背景が、直線の欄干と対比されて極度の不安感を生み出しています。

〈叫び〉はムンクが散歩中に空が血の色に染まるのを見て感じた恐怖を画布に移した作品です。
口を開けた人物の叫びは音ではなく、存在全体を貫く震えとして表現されています。
ja.back:
The undulating curves and distorted forms of The Scream are applied.
The blood-colored sky and shimmering background contrast with the straight parapet, creating a sense of extreme unease.

The Scream is Munch's rendering on canvas of the horror he felt when he saw the sky turn the color of blood while out for a walk.
The scream of the open-mouthed figure is expressed not as a sound but as a tremor that pervades the entire being.

ja.original:
The red halo and undulating sensual curves of 〈Madonna〉 have been applied.
At the boundary between open and closed eyes, life and death merge into one.

〈Madonna〉 is Munch's reinterpretation of the Virgin Mary as a moment where sensuality and death intersect.
Closed eyes, parted lips, and a red halo reveal human desire rather than holiness.
ja.translated:
〈マドンナ〉に見られる赤い後光とうねる曲線の官能的表現が適用されています。
開いた目と閉じた目の境界で、生と死が一つに重なっています。

〈マドンナ〉はムンクが聖母を官能と死が交差する瞬間として再解釈した作品です。
閉じた目と開いた唇、赤い後光が、聖なるものではなく人間の欲望を露わにしています。
ja.back:
The sensuality of the red halo and undulating curves seen in "Madonna" is applied.
At the border between open and closed eyes, life and death merge into one.

The Madonna is Munch's reinterpretation of the Madonna as a moment of intersection between sensuality and death.
The closed eyes, open lips, and red halo reveal not the sacred but human desire.

ja.original:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark colors in 〈The Dance of Life〉 has been applied.
With the central woman in a red dress as axis, innocence and despair are placed on either side.

〈The Dance of Life〉 is Munch's autobiographical work compressing memories of love and heartbreak into one canvas.
Light and dark colors alternate on a single canvas, capturing both the beginning and end of love.
ja.translated:
〈生の踊り〉に見られる明暗の象徴的な対比が適用されています。
中央の赤いドレスの女性を軸に、純粋さと絶望が両側に配されています。

〈生の踊り〉はムンクが愛と失恋の記憶を一つの画面に凝縮した自伝的作品です。
明るい色と暗い色が一つの画面で交差し、愛の始まりと終わりを同時に映し出しています。
ja.back:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark seen in "Dance of the Living" is applied.
The woman in the red dress in the center is the central figure, with purity and despair on either side.

Dance of the Living" is an autobiographical work in which Munch condensed his memories of love and heartbreak into a single painting.
Light and dark colors intersect on one screen, simultaneously reflecting the beginning and the end of love.

ja.original:
The bold color plane division that bisects the face in 〈The Green Stripe〉 has been applied.
Pink, yellow, and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring natural color.

〈The Green Stripe〉 is a portrait Matisse painted of his wife Amélie, drawing cries of "Wild beasts!" at the 1905 Salon.
A single green stripe down the center of the face divides light and shadow through color alone.
ja.translated:
〈緑のすじ〉に見られる顔を横切る大胆な色面分割が適用されています。
肌にピンク、黄色、緑が共存し、現実の色を完全に無視しています。

〈緑のすじ〉はマティスが妻アメリを描いた肖像画で、1905年のサロンで「野獣だ！」と非難されました。
顔の真ん中の緑の一本線が、光と影を色彩だけで分けています。
ja.back:
The bold color plane splitting across the face seen in "Green Stripes" is applied.
Pink, yellow, and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring the colors of reality.

Green Stripes is a portrait by Matisse of his wife, Amélie, which was condemned at the 1905 Salon as "A beast! The green line in the middle of her face is a line of green.
A line of green in the center of the face separates light and shadow using only color.

ja.original:
The intense color planes and decorative flat composition of 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 have been applied.
The boundary between figure and background dissolves, with color itself creating space.

〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 is a work where Matisse conveyed emotion through simplified forms and pure color.
The vast color plane of the purple coat dominates the canvas, merging with the background patterns as one.
ja.translated:
〈紫のコートの女〉に見られる強烈な色面と装飾的な平面構成が適用されています。
人物と背景の境界が曖昧になり、色彩そのものが空間を作り出しています。

〈紫のコートの女〉はマティスが単純化された形と純粋な色で感情を伝えた作品です。
紫のコートの巨大な色面が画面を支配し、背景のパターンと一つに溶け合っています。
ja.back:
The intense color surfaces and decorative flat composition seen in "Woman in Purple Coat" are applied.
The boundary between the figure and the background is blurred, and the colors themselves create space.

In "Woman in Purple Coat," Matisse conveys emotion through simplified forms and pure colors.
The enormous color surface of the purple coat dominates the picture plane and blends with the background pattern.

ja.original:
The flat color plane composition that defies perspective in 〈The Red Room〉 has been applied.
The red wall and red table merge into one as arabesque patterns blanket the entire space.

〈The Red Room〉 was originally painted in blue, but Matisse repainted it red just before completion.
Even the view through the window appears as flat decoration, erasing the boundary between inside and out.
ja.translated:
〈赤い部屋〉に見られる遠近法を無視した平面的な色面構成が適用されています。
赤い壁と赤いテーブルが一つに溶け合い、アラベスク模様が空間全体を覆い尽くしています。

〈赤い部屋〉はマティスが元々青で描いていたものを完成直前に赤で塗り替えた作品です。
窓の外の風景さえも平面的な装飾のように見せ、内と外の境界を消し去りました。
ja.back:
The flat color plane composition that ignores perspective, as seen in the "Red Room," is applied.
The red walls and the red table are blended into one, and an arabesque pattern covers the entire space.

The Red Room was originally painted by Matisse in blue, but was repainted in red just before completion.
Even the landscape outside the window appears as a flat decoration, erasing the boundary between inside and outside.

ja.original:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin colors of 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with bold primary colors, render the subject in a wild, Fauvist manner.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 is a portrait of friendship Matisse painted of his fellow artist André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing their Fauvist experiments together.
ja.translated:
〈ドランの肖像〉に見られる粗い筆致と非現実的な肌の色彩が適用されています。
緑の影とオレンジの肌、強烈な原色が人物を野獣的に表現しています。

〈ドランの肖像〉はマティスが同僚画家アンドレ・ドランを描いた友情の肖像画です。
二人の画家は同時期に互いの肖像画を交換し、フォーヴの実験を共有しました。
ja.back:
The same coarse brushwork and unrealistic skin tones found in "Portrait of Dolan" are applied to this painting.
The green shadows, orange skin, and intense primary colors give the figures a feral quality.

Portrait de Doran" is a friendship portrait of Matisse's colleague André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing the Fauves' experiments.

ja.original:
The dreamlike colors and overlapping images of 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and violet blend together, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 is a work Chagall painted of his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife.
A vivid bouquet of red and white flowers glows against a deep green and navy background, expressing the warmth of love.
ja.translated:
〈花と恋人たち〉に見られる夢幻的な色彩と夢のように重なり合うイメージが適用されています。
ピンク、コバルトブルー、紫が調和し、夢と現実の境界を消し去っています。

〈花と恋人たち〉はシャガールが生涯のミューズであり妻であったベラとの愛を画布に描いた作品です。
深い緑と紺色の背景に赤と白の花束が輝き、愛の温もりを表現しています。
ja.back:
The dreamy colors and dreamlike overlapping images found in "Flowers and Lovers" are applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and purple harmonize to erase the boundary between dream and reality.

Flowers and Lovers" depicts Chagall's love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife, on a canvas.
A bouquet of red and white flowers glows against a background of deep green and navy blue, expressing the warmth of love.

ja.original:
The dreamlike contrast of red bouquets and blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and figures blend like apparitions, unfolding a festive fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and church spires of Chagall's hometown Vitebsk as a landscape of memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet, overlaid with the village scene, holds both nostalgia and joy.
ja.translated:
〈花嫁〉に見られる赤い花束と青い夜空の夢幻的な対比が適用されています。
動物と人物が幻影のように溶け合い、祝祭の幻想を展開しています。

〈花嫁〉はシャガールが故郷ヴィテブスクの屋根と教会の尖塔を記憶の風景として描いた作品です。
赤い花束を抱えた花嫁と重なり合う村の風景が、郷愁と喜びを同時にたたえています。
ja.back:
The dreamy contrast between the red bouquet and the blue night sky seen in "The Bride" is applied.
Animals and figures blend into each other like illusions, creating a festive fantasy.

In Bride, Chagall depicts the roofs and church steeple of his hometown of Vitebsk as a scene from his memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet of flowers and the overlapping landscape of the village are simultaneously nostalgic and joyful.

ja.original:
The multiple perspectives and transparently overlapping images of 〈I and the Village〉 have been applied.
People, animals, and village scenery intermingle dreamlike, defying scale and proportion.

〈I and the Village〉 was painted in Paris as Chagall longed for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, capturing an endless yearning for home.
ja.translated:
〈私と村〉に見られる多視点と透明に重なり合うイメージが適用されています。
人と動物、村の風景が夢の中のように大きさと比率を無視して混ざり合っています。

〈私と村〉はシャガールがパリで故郷ヴィテブスクを恋しく思いながら描いた作品です。
現実と記憶が一つの画面に混ざり合い、故郷への尽きない思いを描き出しています。
ja.back:
The multiple perspectives and transparent overlapping images seen in "Me and My Village" are applied.
People, animals, and village scenes are mixed together as if in a dream, ignoring size and proportion.

I and the Village" is a work that Chagall painted in Paris while pining for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory are blended into a single image, depicting the endless longing for one's hometown.

ja.original:
The simultaneous front and side view of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 has been applied.
Intense primary color planes divide the face geometrically, creating Cubist tension.

〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 was painted by Picasso of his lover and fellow photographer Dora Maar.
The seated woman's front and side coexist in one face, revealing the complexity within.
ja.translated:
〈ドラ・マールの肖像〉に見られる正面と側面の同時表現が適用されています。
強烈な原色の色面が顔を幾何学的に分割し、キュビスムの緊張感を生み出しています。

〈ドラ・マールの肖像〉はピカソが恋人で写真家でもあったドラ・マールを描いた肖像画です。
椅子に座る女性の正面と側面が一つの顔に共存し、内面の複雑さを露わにしています。
ja.back:
The simultaneous representation of the front and side faces seen in "Portrait of Dora Maar" is applied.
The intense primary colors of the colored surfaces divide the face geometrically, creating a Cubist tension.

Portrait of Dora Maar is a portrait of Dora Maar, Picasso's lover and photographer.
The front and side views of the woman seated in the chair coexist in one face, revealing the complexity of her inner life.

ja.original:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Vivid reds, greens, and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.
ja.translated:
〈私とオウムたち〉に見られる強烈な正面の眼差しとメキシコの民俗色彩が適用されています。
鮮やかな赤、緑、青が熱帯の植物とともに画面いっぱいに広がっています。

〈私とオウムたち〉はフリーダが4羽のオウムに囲まれた自分自身を描いた自画像です。
オウムはメキシコの民間伝承で愛の伝達者であり、フリーダの孤独な伴侶でした。
ja.back:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folkloric colors seen in "Me and My Parrots" are applied.
Vibrant reds, greens, and blues fill the picture along with tropical plants.

Me and My Parrots is a self-portrait in which Frida depicts herself surrounded by four parrots.
In Mexican folklore, parrots were the carriers of love and Frida's solitary companions.

ja.original:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 have been applied.
Amid dark green leaves, her intense eyes and connected eyebrows reveal her identity.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait Frida painted with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were surrogates for the children she could never have.
ja.translated:
〈猿と自画像〉に見られる正面の眼差しと鬱蒼とした熱帯の背景が適用されています。
濃い緑の葉の間から、強烈な目とつながった眉がアイデンティティを浮かび上がらせています。

〈猿と自画像〉はフリーダが飼っていた猿とともに描いた自画像です。
猿はメキシコ神話で欲望の象徴ですが、フリーダにとっては持てなかった子どもの代わりでした。
ja.back:
The frontal gaze and dense tropical background seen in "Self-Portrait with Monkey" are applied.
Intense eyes and connected eyebrows emerge from the dark green foliage to reveal identity.

Self-Portrait with Monkey" is a self-portrait of Frida with her monkey.
The monkey is a symbol of desire in Mexican mythology, but for Frida it was a substitute for the child she could not have.

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of 〈In the Car〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill the canvas like a comic book panel.

〈In the Car〉 was created by enlarging a panel from the comic Girls' Romances onto canvas.
Lichtenstein deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image instead of narrative.
ja.translated:
〈車の中で〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと太い黒の輪郭線が適用されています。
赤、青、黄の純粋な原色だけが漫画のひとコマのように画面を満たしています。

〈車の中で〉はリキテンスタインが漫画《ガールズ・ロマンス》のひとコマをカンヴァスに拡大した作品です。
原作にあった吹き出しを意図的に削除し、物語ではなくイメージの力だけを残しました。
ja.back:
The bendy dots and thick black outlines seen in "In the Car" are applied.
Only the pure primary colors of red, blue, and yellow fill the screen, as in a comic strip.

In the Car" is Lichtenstein's expansion on canvas of a scene from the comic strip "Girls' Romance.
The speech bubbles in the original were intentionally removed, leaving only the power of the image, not the story.

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narrative of speech bubbles in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flatly within thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, which sold for $30,000—five times the going rate.
It is now displayed as a centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.
ja.translated:
〈たぶん〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと吹き出しの漫画的な語りが適用されています。
太い輪郭線の中に原色が平面的に塗られ、印刷物の質感を再現しています。

〈たぶん〉はリキテンスタインが個人所蔵していた作品で、当時の相場の5倍にあたる3万ドルで売却されました。
現在はドイツ・ケルンのルートヴィヒ美術館でポップアート・コレクションの代表作として展示されています。
ja.back:
The cartoonish narrative of venday dots and speech bubbles found in <I think> is applied.
Primary colors are applied flat within thick outlines to reproduce the texture of printed matter.

Probably" was in Lichtenstein's private collection and sold for $30,000, five times the market price at the time.
It is currently on display at the Ludwig Museum in Cologne, Germany, as a representative work of the Pop Art collection.

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterwork created just after Lichtenstein's first solo show in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: "Is He the Worst Artist in the U.S.?"—about him.
ja.translated:
〈忘れて！〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと強烈な原色のコントラストが適用されています。
太い黒の輪郭線が形を漫画のように固定し、感情を劇的に圧縮しています。

〈忘れて！〉はリキテンスタインの1962年の初個展直後に制作された初期の代表作です。
同年、ライフ誌は「アメリカ最悪の画家か？」という見出しで彼を取り上げました。
ja.back:
Forget [Joon]! The venday dots and intense primary color contrasts seen in
Thick black outlines anchor the shapes in a cartoon-like manner, dramatically compressing the emotion.

Forget [Joon! is one of Lichtenstein's earliest major works, created shortly after his first solo exhibition in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine featured him under the headline "America's Worst Painter?" in the same year Life magazine featured him under the headline "America's Worst Painter?

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow separate cleanly within black outlines, as crisp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work Lichtenstein borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, overturning the value of what he once called "used canvas."
ja.translated:
〈ああ、わかった〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと平面的な色面構成が適用されています。
赤と青と黄色が黒の輪郭線の中で印刷物のように鮮明に分離しています。

〈ああ、わかった〉は漫画《シークレット・ハーツ》88号から借用した1964年の作品です。
2010年のクリスティーズのオークションで4,260万ドルで落札され、彼が「使い古したカンヴァス」と呼んだものの価値を覆しました。
ja.back:
The bendy dots and flat color plane structure seen in "Oh, I get it.
Red, blue, and yellow are separated by black outlines as clearly as in a printed matter.

Oh, I get it" is a 1964 work borrowed from the "Secret Hearts" comic strip #88.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, reversing the value of what he called a "worn canvas.

ja.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in a comic-like manner, recreating the texture of print.

〈Still Life〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre as homage to Picasso.
"There's no mood in my still lifes—it's just lemons and grapefruits," he said.
ja.translated:
〈静物〉に見られるベンデイ・ドットと太い輪郭線の平面的表現が適用されています。
原色の色面が対象を漫画的に単純化し、印刷物の質感を再現しています。

〈静物〉はリキテンスタインが1970年代にピカソへのオマージュを込めて伝統的ジャンルを再解釈した連作です。
「私の静物画にはムードなんてない。レモンとグレープフルーツがあるだけだ」と語りました。
ja.back:
The flat expression of Benday dots and thick outlines seen in Still Life is applied.
The primary color surfaces cartoonishly simplify the subject and reproduce the texture of the printed matter.

Still Life is a series of works in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted the traditional genre in homage to Picasso in the 1970s.
There is no mood in my still lifes," Lichtenstein said. There are just lemons and grapefruit.

ja.translated: ファン・ゴッホ画風

ja.original:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

Van Gogh left over 40 self-portraits, confronting his inner self most honestly through them.
The eyes gazing directly forward show unwavering will even amid suffering.
ja.translated:
ファン・ゴッホの絵の具を厚く塗り重ねるインパスト技法と渦巻く筆致が適用されています。
黄色と青の強烈なコントラストで内面の感情を表しています。

40点を超える自画像で、自らの内面を最も率直に見つめ続けました。
正面を見据える眼差しは、苦悩の中でも揺るがない意志を示しています。
ja.back:
Van Gogh's impasto technique of applying thick layers of paint and swirling brushstrokes is applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue expresses inner emotions.

In over 40 self-portraits, he continued to look most candidly at his own inner world.
The gaze looking straight ahead shows an unwavering will even in the midst of suffering.

ja.translated: クリムト画風

ja.original:
Klimt's real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on men's clothing contrast with curves and circles on women's, expressing both opposition and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decoration wrap the figure in a mystical golden world.
Within this decoration, sensuality and divinity coexist.
ja.translated:
クリムトの実際の金箔とビザンティン・モザイクから霊感を得た幾何学的パターンが適用されています。
男性の衣に直線の矩形、女性の衣に曲線と円で、対比と調和を表現しています。

クリムトの螺旋形の曲線と華麗な装飾が、人物を神秘的な黄金の世界に包み込んでいます。
その装飾の中で、官能と神聖は同時に存在しています。
ja.back:
Geometric patterns inspired by Klimt's actual gold leaf and Byzantine mosaics are applied.
The straight rectangles on the men's robe and the curves and circles on the women's robe express contrast and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decorations envelop the figures in a mysterious golden world.
Within the ornamentation, the sensual and the sacred exist simultaneously.

ja.translated: ムンク画風

ja.original:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors have been applied.
Blood-red skies and wavering landscapes visualize inner emotions.

Munch wrote in his diary that while walking by the Oslo fjord, he "heard an enormous scream pass through nature."
Before his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.
ja.translated:
ムンクの渦巻く曲線と歪んだ形態、強烈な色彩が適用されています。
血の色に燃える空と揺らめく風景が、内面の感情を視覚的に具現化しています。

オスロのフィヨルドを散歩中に「自然全体を貫く巨大な叫びを聞いた」と日記に記しました。
彼の絵の前に立つと、色彩そのものが感情の振動となって伝わってきます。
ja.back:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors are applied.
The blood-red sky and shimmering landscape visually embody inner emotions.

During a walk in the Oslo fjord, he wrote in his diary, "I heard a huge cry that penetrated the whole of nature.
Standing in front of his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.

ja.translated: マティス画風

ja.original:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors have been applied.
Rather than anatomical accuracy, focus is on the energy of movement, with color directly conveying emotion.

Just as Matisse erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room, he the boundary between table and wall, he chose harmony of color planes over perspective.
Before his canvas, colors sing out.
ja.translated:
マティスの単純化された形態と大胆な原色が適用されています。
解剖学的な正確さよりも動きのエネルギーに集中し、色彩が感情を直接伝えています。

〈赤い部屋〉でテーブルと壁の境界を消し去ったように、遠近法よりも色面の調和を選びました。
彼の画面の前に立つと、色彩が歌うように響いてきます。
ja.back:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors are applied.
Concentrating on the energy of movement rather than anatomical precision, the colors convey emotion directly.

He chooses harmony of color surfaces over perspective, just as he erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room.
Standing in front of his paintings, the colors seem to sing.

ja.translated: シャガール画風

ja.original:
Chagall's dreamlike atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel-like colors has been applied.
Bouquets, lovers, and hometown scenes blend together like memories, lifting the weight of reality.

Chagall composed his canvases with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space halt before love, and the world fills with dreamlike light.
ja.translated:
シャガールのピンクとコバルトブルー、宝石のような色彩で織りなされた夢幻的な雰囲気が適用されています。
花束と恋人、故郷の風景が記憶のようにおぼろげに溶け合い、現実の重さを取り去っています。

遠近法や解剖学ではなく、感情の論理で画面を構成しました。
彼の絵の中では、時間も空間も愛の前に立ち止まり、世界は夢のような光に染まります。
ja.back:
A dreamy atmosphere is applied, woven with Chagall's pinks and cobalt blues and jewel-like colors.
Bouquets of flowers, lovers, and hometown scenes meld into each other dimly, like memories, taking away the weight of reality.

He composed his paintings not with perspective or anatomy, but with emotional logic.
In his paintings, both time and space stand still before love, and the world is tinged with a dreamy light.

ja.translated: ピカソ画風

ja.original:
Picasso's Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Like Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple viewpoints coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, Picasso conveyed the terror of war immediately. This destructive deconstruction was itself creation.
Five centuries of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.
ja.translated:
ピカソの対象を幾何学的な面に分解し、正面と側面を同時に見せるキュビスムの技法が適用されています。
〈ドラ・マールの肖像〉のように、一つの顔に複数の視点が共存しています。

〈ゲルニカ〉では白と黒だけで戦争の恐怖を即座に伝え、破壊的な解体がそのまま創造でした。
500年続いた遠近法の規則は、彼のカンヴァスの上で粉々に砕け散りました。
ja.back:
Picasso's cubist technique of breaking his subject down into geometric planes and showing both the front and side simultaneously is applied.
As in "Portrait of Dora Maar," multiple perspectives coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, black and white alone immediately conveys the horrors of war, and the destructive deconstruction of the work is its creation.
The rules of perspective, which had been in place for 500 years, were shattered on his canvas.

ja.translated: フリーダ・カーロ画風

ja.original:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and communion with animals have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird, thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys as family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not mere backgrounds but companions offering comfort amid suffering.
ja.translated:
フリーダのメキシコの伝統色彩と熱帯の葉、動物との交感が適用されています。
〈荊の首飾りと蜂鳥の自画像〉で、荊は苦痛を、死んだ蜂鳥は失った愛を象徴しています。

子どもを持てなかったフリーダにとって、オウムや猿は家族のような存在でした。
彼女の自画像の中で、動物たちは単なる背景ではなく、苦痛の中の慰めを伝える伴侶です。
ja.back:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and animal communion are applied.
In "Self-Portrait with Briar Necklace and Bee Bird," the briars symbolize pain and the dead bee bird symbolizes lost love.

For Frida, who was unable to have children, parrots and monkeys were like family.
In her self-portraits, the animals are not mere backgrounds, but companions who convey comfort in the midst of pain.

ja.translated: リキテンスタイン画風

ja.original:
Lichtenstein's Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines, and primary colors have been applied.
Like the glamorous couple In the Car, every image becomes a comic book panel.

Lichtenstein's dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of comic heroines, everyday moments transform into dramatic scenes.
Emotion exploding within mechanical dots and lines—that is Lichtenstein's paradox.
ja.translated:
リキテンスタインの印刷漫画のベンデイ・ドットと太い黒の輪郭線、原色のみを使ったポップアートの技法が適用されています。
〈車の中で〉のグラマラスなカップルのように、あらゆるイメージが漫画のひとコマになります。

リキテンスタインの漫画のヒロインのドラマチックな表情と吹き出しのように、日常が劇的な瞬間に変わります。
機械的なドットと線の中で感情が爆発する、それがリキテンスタインのパラドックスです。
ja.back:
Lichtenstein's print cartoon technique of pop art, using only bendy dots, thick black outlines, and primary colors, is applied.
Every image becomes a comic strip, like the glamorous couple in "In the Car.

Everyday life is transformed into a dramatic moment, like the dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of Lichtenstein's cartoon heroines.
Emotions explode amidst the mechanical dots and lines, which is Lichtenstein's paradox.
