fr.translated: Van Gogh
fr.back: Van Gogh

fr.original:
Van Gogh is a defining figure of Post-Impressionism.
He began painting at 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, yet only one sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces like The Starry Night, Sunflowers, and Café Terrace at Night were all born in poverty and mental anguish.

Van Gogh died at 37, but his art profoundly influenced Expressionism and Fauvism.
"I put my heart and soul into my work." — A painter who forged immortal beauty from suffering.
fr.translated:
La technique de l'impasto épais et les coups de pinceau tourbillonnants ont été appliqués.
L'intense contraste du jaune et du bleu exprime directement l'émotion de l'âme.

Van Gogh est une figure déterminante du Post-Impressionnisme.
Il a commencé à peindre à 27 ans et a produit 900 peintures à l'huile en seulement dix ans, mais une seule s'est vendue de son vivant.
Des chefs-d'œuvre comme La Nuit étoilée, Les Tournesols et La Terrasse du café le soir sont tous nés dans la pauvreté et la souffrance mentale.

"Je mets mon cœur et mon âme dans mon travail." — Un peintre qui forgea une beauté immortelle à partir de la souffrance.
fr.back:
The thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes were applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue directly expresses the emotion of the soul.

Van Gogh is a defining figure of Post-Impressionism.
He began painting at the age of 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, but only one sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces such as The Starry Night, The Sunflowers and The Terrace of the Café in the Evening were all born of poverty and mental suffering.

"I put my heart and soul into my work." - A painter who forged immortal beauty from suffering.

fr.translated: Klimt
fr.back: Klimt

fr.original:
Klimt was the leading Art Nouveau painter of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
Born the son of a goldsmith, he applied real gold leaf to canvas, creating an opulent decorative world inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
He left behind golden masterpieces where sensuality and divinity coexist, including The Kiss, Judith, and The Tree of Life.

Klimt founded the Vienna Secession, challenging the conservative art establishment.
"To every age its art, to every art its freedom." — A painter who captured fin-de-siècle beauty in gold.
fr.translated:
De vraies feuilles d'or et des motifs géométriques inspirés des mosaïques byzantines ont été appliqués.
Des rectangles sur les côtés et des cercles en haut—les détails décoratifs enveloppent maintenant votre silhouette.

Klimt était le principal peintre Art Nouveau de la Vienne de la fin du XIXe siècle.
Il a créé une technique unique combinant peinture à l'huile et incrustations d'or.

"À chaque époque son art, à chaque art sa liberté." — Un peintre qui captura la beauté du fin de siècle en or.
fr.back:
Real gold leaf and geometric motifs inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on the sides and circles at the top-the decorative details now envelop your silhouette.

Klimt was Vienna's leading Art Nouveau painter of the late 19th century.
He created a unique technique combining oil paint and gold inlay.

"To each age its art, to each art its freedom." - A painter who captured fin de siècle beauty in gold.

fr.translated: Munch
fr.back: Munch

fr.original:
Munch is the defining Expressionist painter from Norway.
He lost his mother at five and his sister at fourteen, and the memory of these losses dominated his canvases for life.
His works, including The Scream, Madonna, and The Dance of Life, form "The Frieze of Life" — a cycle of love, death, and anxiety.

Munch declared, "I paint not what I see, but what I saw,", exploring the deepest human emotions.
"Illness, madness, and death were the dark angels that watched over my cradle." — A painter who transformed inner darkness into art.
fr.translated:
Des courbes tourbillonnantes, des formes distordues et des couleurs intenses ont été appliquées.
Des ciels rouge sang et des lignes ondulantes expriment l'anxiété et les émotions profondes.

Munch fut le pionnier de l'Expressionnisme qui peignait non ce qu'il voyait, mais ce qu'il ressentait.
Il a perdu sa mère à 5 ans et sa sœur préférée à 14 ans—ce traumatisme est devenu la source permanente de son œuvre.

"La maladie, la folie et la mort furent les anges noirs qui veillèrent sur mon berceau." — Un peintre qui transforma ses ténèbres intérieures en art.
fr.back:
Swirling curves, distorted shapes and intense colors were applied.
Blood-red skies and undulating lines express anxiety and deep emotions.

Munch was a pioneer of Expressionism, painting not what he saw, but what he felt.
He lost his mother at the age of 5 and his favorite sister at 14 - a trauma that became the permanent source of his work.

"Illness, madness and death were the dark angels who watched over my cradle." - A painter who transformed his inner darkness into art.

fr.translated: Matisse
fr.back: Matisse

fr.original:
Matisse is the leading Fauvist painter of France.
At 21, he began painting with a box of colors his mother gave him while bedridden, later recalling it felt like "discovering paradise."
The 1905 cry of "Wild beasts!" became the birth of Fauvism, and he left behind color-revolutionary masterpieces like The Dance, The Green Stripe, and The Red Room.

Matisse conveyed emotion through pure color alone, pushing the decorative potential of painting to its limit.
"The purpose of color is not to describe form but to express emotion." — A painter who sang joy through color.
fr.translated:
Des formes simplifiées et des couleurs primaires audacieuses ont été appliquées.
Plutôt que la précision anatomique, l'accent est mis sur l'expression des émotions à travers la couleur.

Matisse était le chef de file du Fauvisme—mouvement utilisant des couleurs sauvages pour exprimer les sentiments plutôt que la réalité.
Il a découvert la peinture en se remettant d'une appendicite à l'âge de 20 ans et ne s'est jamais arrêté.

"Le but de la couleur n'est pas de décrire la forme mais d'exprimer l'émotion." — Un peintre qui chanta la joie par la couleur.
fr.back:
Simplified shapes and bold primary colors were applied.
Rather than anatomical precision, the emphasis is on expressing emotions through color.

Matisse was the leader of Fauvism-movement using wild colors to express feelings rather than reality.
He discovered painting while recovering from appendicitis at the age of 20, and never stopped.

"The purpose of color is not to describe form but to express emotion." - A painter who sang joy through color.

fr.translated: Chagall
fr.back: Chagall

fr.original:
Chagall was a painter of love and dreams.
Born in a Russian Jewish village, he moved to Paris and never put down his brush through two World Wars and exile.
His love for his wife Bella was a lifelong source of his art, leaving behind masterpieces like The Birthday, La Mariée, and I and the Village — canvases where bouquets and lovers float in midair.

Chagall worked until 97, enchanting the world with the Paris Opéra ceiling and stained glass windows.
"In our life there is a single color, as on an artist's palette, which provides the meaning of life and art — the color of love." — A poet who erased the boundary between dream and reality.
fr.translated:
Une atmosphère onirique de rose, de bleu cobalt et de couleurs chatoyantes a été appliquée.
Des bouquets, des amoureux flottants et des animaux fantastiques flottent entre rêve et réalité.

Chagall est né à Vitebsk, en Biélorussie, et y a trouvé toutes les couleurs de sa vie.
Son amour pour Bella, sa femme toute sa vie, est devenu le thème éternel de son œuvre.

"Dans notre vie, il n'y a qu'une seule couleur : celle de l'amour." — Un poète qui effaça la frontière entre rêve et réalité.
fr.back:
A dreamy atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue and shimmering colors was applied.
Bouquets, floating lovers and fantastical animals float between dream and reality.

Chagall was born in Vitebsk, Belarus, and found all the colors of his life there.
His love for Bella, his lifelong wife, became the eternal theme of his work.

"In our life, there is only one color: that of love." - A poet who blurred the line between dream and reality.

fr.translated: Picasso
fr.back: Picasso

fr.original:
Picasso co-founded Cubism and changed the direction of modern art.
In his 91-year life, he left approximately 50,000 works. Legend has it that at 13, his art teacher father saw his talent and broke his own brushes.
He opened Cubism with Les Demoiselles d'Avignon, denounced war with Guernica, and reached the pinnacle of deconstruction with Portrait of Dora Maar.

Through Blue Period, Rose Period, Cubism, and Surrealism, Picasso constantly reinvented himself.
"Good artists copy, great artists steal." — A painter who broke all rules and created new ones.
fr.translated:
Les techniques cubistes qui déconstruisent les sujets en plans géométriques et montrent le devant et le côté simultanément ont été appliquées.
Votre visage est maintenant partout à la fois—vu sous tous les angles simultanément.

Picasso était l'artiste le plus influent du XXe siècle.
À 13 ans, il peignait déjà mieux que son père, qui était professeur d'art.

Avec Braque, il a développé le Cubisme—une révolution qui a brisé la perspective traditionnelle et ouvert la porte à l'art abstrait.
fr.back:
Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show the front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Your face is now everywhere at once-seen from all angles simultaneously.

Picasso was the most influential artist of the 20th century.
By the age of 13, he was already painting better than his father, who was an art teacher.

With Braque, he developed Cubism-a revolution that shattered traditional perspective and opened the door to abstract art.

fr.translated: Frida Kahlo
fr.back: Frida Kahlo

fr.original:
Frida Kahlo is Mexico's most original painter and a feminist icon.
She contracted polio at 6 and at 18 suffered a bus accident that shattered her spine and pelvis, enduring over 30 surgeries throughout her life.
She began painting while bedridden, looking at a mirror on the ceiling — and much of her work, including Me and My Parrots, The Broken Column, and Self-Portrait with Monkeys, are self-portraits.

Frida's art, with its honest portrayal of pain, love, and identity, continues to resonate deeply today.
"Feet, what do I need them for if I have wings to fly?" — A painter who transformed pain into art.
fr.translated:
Des couleurs folkloriques mexicaines traditionnelles, du feuillage tropical et une communion avec les animaux ont été appliqués.
Dans Autoportrait au collier d'épines, la douleur devient art.

À 18 ans, Frida Kahlo a subi un terrible accident de bus qui lui a brisé la colonne vertébrale.
Pendant sa convalescence, elle a commencé à peindre—transformant la douleur en art.

"Des pieds, pourquoi en voudrais-je si j'ai des ailes pour voler ?" — Une peintre invincible qui transforma la douleur en art.
fr.back:
Traditional Mexican folk colors, tropical foliage and a communion with animals were applied.
In Autoportrait au collier d'épines, pain becomes art.

At the age of 18, Frida Kahlo suffered a terrible bus accident that broke her spine.
During her convalescence, she began to paint-transforming pain into art.

"Feet, why would I want feet if I have wings to fly on?" - An invincible painter who turned pain into art.

fr.translated: Lichtenstein
fr.back: Lichtenstein

fr.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who broke the boundaries of fine art with comic book panels.
When his son pointed to a Mickey Mouse comic and asked, "Can you draw as well as this, Dad?", he actually began transferring comics to canvas.
With In the Car, M-Maybe, and Forget It!, he shook the art world by blowing up comic panels onto massive canvases.

Lichtenstein, with Ben-Day dots and bold outlines, overturned the hierarchy between popular culture and high art.
"I don't draw comics, I paint pictures about comics." — A painter who erased the line between pop culture and fine art.
fr.translated:
Les techniques du pop art utilisant des points Ben-Day de bandes dessinées imprimées, des contours noirs épais et des couleurs primaires ont été appliquées.
Votre visage ressemble maintenant à un panneau de bande dessinée—brillant, audacieux et plein d'expression dramatique.

En 1961, Lichtenstein a peint une bande dessinée de Mickey Mouse et le monde de l'art a tremblé.
Le magazine Life a demandé : 'Est-il le pire artiste des États-Unis ?'

"Je ne dessine pas des BD, je peins des tableaux sur les BD." — Un peintre qui effaça la ligne entre culture pop et beaux-arts.
fr.back:
Pop art techniques using printed comic book Ben-Day dots, thick black outlines and primary colors have been applied.
Your face now looks like a comic-book panel-bright, bold and full of dramatic expression.

In 1961, Lichtenstein painted a Mickey Mouse cartoon and the art world shook.
Life magazine asked: 'Is he America's worst artist?'

"I don't draw comics, I paint pictures about comics." - A painter who blurred the line between pop culture and fine art.

fr.original:
The swirling impasto brushstrokes of 〈The Starry Night〉 have been applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow unleashes the energy of the night.

〈The Starry Night〉 was Van Gogh's reimagined vision of the landscape beyond his asylum window at Saint-Rémy.
Rather than painting the actual night sky, he projected his inner turmoil onto the stars.
fr.translated:
La technique d'impasto tourbillonnant de 〈La Nuit étoilée〉 a été appliquée.
Le contraste intense du bleu cobalt et du jaune chrome libère l'énergie de la nuit.

〈La Nuit étoilée〉 est la vision réimaginée par Van Gogh du paysage au-delà de la fenêtre de son asile à Saint-Rémy.
Plutôt que de peindre le ciel nocturne réel, il a projeté ses tourments intérieurs sur les étoiles.
fr.back:
The swirling impasto technique from 〈La Nuit étoilée〉 has been applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow releases the energy of the night.

〈La Nuit étoilée〉 is Van Gogh's reimagined vision of the landscape beyond the window of his asylum in Saint-Rémy.
Rather than painting the actual night sky, he projected his inner torments onto the stars.

fr.original:
The complementary color nightscape technique of 〈Café Terrace at Night〉—without using black—has been applied.
Yellow gaslights and deep blue night sky express darkness through color contrast alone.

〈Café Terrace at Night〉 is the first outdoor night scene Van Gogh painted under starlight at Arles' Place du Forum.
The empty chairs on the terrace and passing figures hold both the stillness and warmth of the night.
fr.translated:
La technique de paysage nocturne aux couleurs complémentaires de 〈La Terrasse du café le soir〉, sans utiliser le noir, a été appliquée.
Les réverbères jaunes et le ciel bleu profond expriment l'obscurité uniquement par contraste des couleurs.

〈La Terrasse du café le soir〉 est la première scène nocturne en extérieur que Van Gogh peignit sous les étoiles à la Place du Forum d'Arles.
Les chaises vides sur la terrasse et les passants évoquent à la fois la quiétude et la chaleur de la nuit.
fr.back:
The complementary color nightscape technique of 〈La Terrasse du café le soir〉, without using black, has been applied.
The yellow streetlamps and deep blue sky express darkness solely through color contrast.

〈La Terrasse du café le soir〉 is the first outdoor night scene Van Gogh painted under the stars at the Place du Forum in Arles.
The empty chairs on the terrace and the passers-by evoke both the quietude and warmth of the night.

fr.original:
The subtle tonal variations of a single chrome yellow in 〈Sunflowers〉 have been applied.
Thickly layered paint captures the texture of the petals as they are.

〈Sunflowers〉 is a series Van Gogh painted to decorate the Yellow House in Arles for Gauguin's arrival.
Full blooms, wilted flowers, and seeds together reveal the cycle of life.
fr.translated:
Les subtiles variations tonales d'un jaune chrome unique dans 〈Les Tournesols〉 ont été appliquées.
La peinture épaissement superposée capture la texture des pétales tels qu'ils sont.

〈Les Tournesols〉 est une série que Van Gogh peignit pour décorer la Maison Jaune à Arles avant l'arrivée de Gauguin.
Fleurs épanouies, fleurs fanées et graines révèlent ensemble le cycle de la vie.
fr.back:
The subtle tonal variations of a unique chrome yellow in 〈Les Tournesols〉 have been applied.
The thickly layered paint captures the texture of the petals as they are.

〈Les Tournesols〉 is a series Van Gogh painted to decorate the Maison Jaune in Arles before Gauguin arrived.
Blossoming flowers, faded blossoms and seeds together reveal the cycle of life.

fr.original:
The swirling background and thick impasto brushstrokes of 〈Self-Portrait〉 have been applied.
Cool tones of blue and green convey both solitude and resolve.

〈Self-Portrait〉 was painted when Van Gogh, too poor to afford models, used himself as subject.
The eyes gazing directly forward and tightly closed lips show unwavering will amid suffering.
fr.translated:
Le fond tourbillonnant et les épaisses touches d'impasto de 〈Autoportrait〉 ont été appliqués.
Les tons froids de vert et de bleu créent une intensité psychologique.

〈Autoportrait〉 est le miroir dans lequel Van Gogh se regardait lui-même—et nous avec lui.
Au cours de ses 10 années de carrière, il a peint plus de 30 autoportraits, chacun révélant différentes couches de l'âme.

Ce portrait, avec son fond bleu tourbillonnant, est considéré comme le sommet de ses capacités techniques et de son expression intérieure.
fr.back:
The swirling background and thick impasto touches of 〈Autoportrait〉 have been applied.
Cool tones of green and blue create a psychological intensity.

〈Autoportrait〉 is the mirror in which Van Gogh looked at himself-and us with him.
During his 10-year career, he painted over 30 self-portraits, each revealing different layers of the soul.

This portrait, with its swirling blue background, is considered the pinnacle of his technical ability and inner expression.

fr.original:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of 〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 have been applied.
Wind-blown wheat and flame-like cypresses convey nature's vital energy.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 is a landscape Van Gogh repeatedly painted near the asylum at Saint-Rémy.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the vertical cypress connect earth and sky.
fr.translated:
Les coups de pinceau courbes dynamiques et les contrastes de couleurs complémentaires de 〈Champ de blé avec cyprès〉 ont été appliqués.
Le blé agité par le vent et les cyprès semblables à des flammes transmettent l'énergie vitale de la nature.

〈Champ de blé avec cyprès〉 est un paysage que Van Gogh peignit à plusieurs reprises près de l'asile de Saint-Rémy.
L'horizon doré du champ de blé et le cyprès vertical relient la terre et le ciel.
fr.back:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of 〈Wheat field with cypress〉 have been applied.
The wind-stirred wheat and flame-like cypress trees convey the vital energy of nature.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 is a landscape that Van Gogh painted several times near the Saint-Rémy asylum.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the vertical cypress tree connect earth and sky.

fr.original:
The gold leaf background and geometric decorative patterns of 〈The Kiss〉 have been applied.
Rectangles on the man's robe contrast with curves and circles on the woman's, creating opposition and harmony.

〈The Kiss〉 is Klimt's masterpiece from his "Golden Phase," inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The lovers' embrace wrapped in gold holds both the ecstasy of love and the anxiety of its passing.
fr.translated:
Le fond de feuille d'or et les motifs décoratifs géométriques de 〈Le Baiser〉 ont été appliqués.
Des rectangles et des cercles alternent pour créer un manteau doré enveloppant deux silhouettes.

〈Le Baiser〉 est le chef-d'œuvre de l'Art Nouveau qui est maintenant l'une des peintures les plus aimées au monde.
L'or véritable brille sur la toile—Klimt a mélangé les techniques de peinture traditionnelles avec des matériaux luxueux.

Deux silhouettes qui s'étreignent au bord d'un précipice sont devenues un symbole universel de l'amour et de la vulnérabilité.
fr.back:
The gold leaf background and geometric decorative motifs from 〈Le Baiser〉 have been applied.
Rectangles and circles alternate to create a golden cloak enveloping two silhouettes.

〈Le Baiser〉 is the Art Nouveau masterpiece that is now one of the world's most beloved paintings.
Genuine gold shines on canvas-Klimt blended traditional painting techniques with luxurious materials.

Two silhouettes embracing on the edge of a precipice have become a universal symbol of love and vulnerability.

fr.original:
The spiral curves and golden decorative patterns of 〈The Tree of Life〉 have been applied.
Triangles, circles, and spirals repeat, creating a rhythmic flow of life's energy.

〈The Tree of Life〉 was created as a dining room mural for the Stoclet Palace in Brussels.
The endlessly branching spiral limbs are a metaphor for birth, death, and the cycle of life.
fr.translated:
Les courbes spirales et les motifs décoratifs dorés de 〈L'Arbre de vie〉 ont été appliqués.
Des triangles, des yeux et des spirales superposés créent une tapisserie de vie et de mort.

〈L'Arbre de vie〉 est la pièce centrale de la Frise Stoclet—une décoration murale monumentale que Klimt a créée pour un palais à Bruxelles.
L'arbre aux branches en spirale symbolise le cycle éternel de la vie.

C'est l'exemple parfait du Gesamtkunstwerk—œuvre d'art totale unissant peinture, architecture et artisanat.
fr.back:
The spiral curves and gilded decorative motifs of 〈L'Arbre de vie〉 have been applied.
Superimposed triangles, eyes and spirals create a tapestry of life and death.

〈L'Arbre de vie〉 is the centerpiece of the Frise Stoclet-a monumental wall decoration Klimt created for a palace in Brussels.
The tree with its spiraling branches symbolizes the eternal cycle of life.

It is the perfect example of Gesamtkunstwerk-a total work of art combining painting, architecture and craftsmanship.

fr.original:
The gold leaf decoration and jewel-like sensual colors of 〈Judith〉 have been applied.
Transparent veils and gold necklaces create tension between the sacred and the sensual.

〈Judith〉 is Klimt's reinterpretation of the biblical heroine as a sensual woman.
Half-closed eyes and a provocative smile completely overturn the piety of traditional religious painting.
fr.translated:
La décoration en feuille d'or et les couleurs sensuelles chatoyantes de 〈Judith〉 ont été appliquées.
Du velours transparent et un manteau doré créent une expression contradictoire de pouvoir et de désir.

〈Judith〉 est la guerrière de l'Ancien Testament qui a décapité Holopherne—mais Klimt l'a peinte non comme une héroïne, mais comme une femme fatale en extase.

Les yeux mi-clos et l'expression des lèvres entrouvertes créent une ambiguïté entre plaisir et victoire qui est la marque distinctive de Klimt.
fr.back:
Gold leaf decoration and shimmering sensual colors from 〈Judith〉 were applied.
Sheer velvet and a golden coat create a contradictory expression of power and desire.

〈Judith〉 is the Old Testament warrior who beheaded Holofernes-but Klimt painted her not as a heroine, but as an ecstatic femme fatale.

The half-closed eyes and parted lips create an ambiguity between pleasure and victory that is Klimt's hallmark.

fr.original:
The undulating curves and distorted forms of 〈The Scream〉 have been applied.
A blood-red sky and rippling background contrast with the straight railing, creating extreme anxiety.

〈The Scream〉 captures the terror Munch felt when the sky turned blood-red during a walk.
The open-mouthed figure's cry is expressed not as sound but as a tremor passing through all existence.
fr.translated:
Les courbes ondulantes et les formes distordues de 〈Le Cri〉 ont été appliquées.
Un ciel rouge sang et des lignes tremblantes expriment une pure anxiété existentielle.

〈Le Cri〉 est né d'une expérience réelle de Munch—un soir à Oslo, il a senti que la nature lui 'criait dessus'.
Trois versions en peinture et une en pastel—chacune capturant le moment où l'être humain ressent le vide de l'univers.

C'est maintenant le symbole visuel de l'anxiété moderne le plus reconnu au monde.
fr.back:
The undulating curves and distorted shapes of 〈Le Cri〉 have been applied.
Blood-red skies and trembling lines express pure existential anxiety.

〈Le Cri〉 was born of Munch's real-life experience-one evening in Oslo, he felt nature 'screaming at him'.
Three versions in paint and one in pastel-each capturing the moment when human beings feel the emptiness of the universe.

It is now the world's most recognized visual symbol of modern anxiety.

fr.original:
The red halo and undulating sensual curves of 〈Madonna〉 have been applied.
At the boundary between open and closed eyes, life and death merge into one.

〈Madonna〉 is Munch's reinterpretation of the Virgin Mary as a moment where sensuality and death intersect.
Closed eyes, parted lips, and a red halo reveal human desire rather than holiness.
fr.translated:
Le halo rouge et les courbes sensuelles ondulantes de 〈Madonna〉 ont été appliqués.
À la frontière entre la vie et la mort, la silhouette se dresse les yeux fermés.

〈Madonna〉 n'est pas une Madonna religieuse—c'est la célébration par Munch de la naissance, de l'amour et de la mort en une seule figure.
Des vagues rouges enveloppent la silhouette nue, tandis que le cadre montre un fœtus et un crâne.

C'est une méditation sur le cycle éternel de la vie que seul Munch pouvait exprimer avec un tel courage.
fr.back:
The red halo and sensual undulating curves of 〈Madonna〉 were applied.
On the border between life and death, the figure stands with eyes closed.

〈Madonna〉 is not a religious Madonna-it's Munch's celebration of birth, love and death in a single figure.
Red waves envelop the naked figure, while the frame shows a fetus and a skull.

It's a meditation on the eternal cycle of life that only Munch could express with such courage.

fr.original:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark colors in 〈The Dance of Life〉 has been applied.
With the central woman in a red dress as axis, innocence and despair are placed on either side.

〈The Dance of Life〉 is Munch's autobiographical work compressing memories of love and heartbreak into one canvas.
Light and dark colors alternate on a single canvas, capturing both the beginning and end of love.
fr.translated:
Le contraste symbolique des couleurs claires et sombres dans 〈La Danse de la vie〉 a été appliqué.
Avec la femme centrale en robe rouge comme axe, l'innocence et le désespoir sont placés de chaque côté.

〈La Danse de la vie〉 est l'œuvre autobiographique de Munch qui compresse des souvenirs d'amour et de chagrins en une seule toile.
Les couleurs claires et sombres alternent sur une seule toile, capturant à la fois le début et la fin de l'amour.
fr.back:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark colors in 〈La Danse de la vie〉 has been applied.
With the central woman in a red dress as the axis, innocence and despair are placed on either side.

〈La Danse de la vie〉 is Munch's autobiographical work that compresses memories of love and sorrow into a single canvas.
Light and dark colors alternate on a single canvas, capturing both the beginning and the end of love.

fr.original:
The bold color plane division that bisects the face in 〈The Green Stripe〉 has been applied.
Pink, yellow, and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring natural color.

〈The Green Stripe〉 is a portrait Matisse painted of his wife Amélie, drawing cries of "Wild beasts!" at the 1905 Salon.
A single green stripe down the center of the face divides light and shadow through color alone.
fr.translated:
La division audacieuse des plans de couleur qui divise le visage en deux dans 〈La Raie verte〉 a été appliquée.
Le rose, le jaune et le vert coexistent sur la peau, ignorant complètement la couleur naturelle.

〈La Raie verte〉 est un portrait que Matisse peignit de son épouse Amélie, provoquant des cris de 'bêtes sauvages !' au Salon de 1905.
Une seule raie verte au centre du visage divise lumière et ombre uniquement par la couleur.
fr.back:
The bold division of color planes that splits the face in two in 〈La Raie verte〉 has been applied.
Pink, yellow and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring the natural color.

〈La Raie verte〉 is a portrait Matisse painted of his wife Amélie, provoking cries of 'wild beasts!' at the 1905 Salon.
A single green stripe down the center of the face divides light and shadow solely through color.

fr.original:
The intense color planes and decorative flat composition of 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 have been applied.
The boundary between figure and background dissolves, with color itself creating space.

〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 is a work where Matisse conveyed emotion through simplified forms and pure color.
The vast color plane of the purple coat dominates the canvas, merging with the background patterns as one.
fr.translated:
Les intenses plans de couleur et la composition plate décorative de 〈Femme au manteau violet〉 ont été appliqués.
La frontière entre la figure et l'arrière-plan se dissout, la couleur elle-même créant l'espace.

〈Femme au manteau violet〉 est une œuvre où Matisse a exprimé l'émotion à travers des formes simplifiées et une couleur pure.
Le vaste plan de couleur du manteau violet domine la toile, se fondant avec les motifs du fond en un tout.
fr.back:
The intense planes of color and decorative flat composition of 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 have been applied.
The boundary between figure and background dissolves, with color itself creating space.

〈Femme au manteau violet〉 is a work in which Matisse expressed emotion through simplified forms and pure color.
The vast color plane of the purple coat dominates the canvas, blending with the background patterns into a whole.

fr.original:
The flat color plane composition that defies perspective in 〈The Red Room〉 has been applied.
The red wall and red table merge into one as arabesque patterns blanket the entire space.

〈The Red Room〉 was originally painted in blue, but Matisse repainted it red just before completion.
Even the view through the window appears as flat decoration, erasing the boundary between inside and out.
fr.translated:
La composition en plans de couleur plate qui défie la perspective dans 〈La Chambre rouge〉 a été appliquée.
Le mur rouge et la table rouge fusionnent en un seul—les motifs d'arabesques recouvrent tout l'espace.

〈La Chambre rouge〉 a d'abord été peinte en bleu, mais Matisse l'a repeinte en rouge juste avant de terminer.
Même la vue par la fenêtre apparaît comme une décoration plate—effaçant la frontière entre intérieur et extérieur.

C'est la preuve du courage de Matisse : que la couleur peut être plus puissante que la réalité.
fr.back:
The composition in flat planes of color that defies perspective in 〈La Chambre rouge〉 has been applied.
The red wall and red table merge into one-the arabesque motifs cover the entire space.

〈La Chambre rouge〉 was originally painted blue, but Matisse repainted it red just before finishing.
Even the view through the window appears as a flat decoration-erasing the boundary between inside and outside.

It's proof of Matisse's courage: that color can be more powerful than reality.

fr.original:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin colors of 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with bold primary colors, render the subject in a wild, Fauvist manner.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 is a portrait of friendship Matisse painted of his fellow artist André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing their Fauvist experiments together.
fr.translated:
Les coups de pinceau rugueux et les couleurs de peau non naturelles du 〈Portrait d'André Derain〉 ont été appliqués.
Les ombres vertes et la peau orange, avec des couleurs primaires audacieuses, rendent le sujet d'une manière sauvage et fauviste.

Le 〈Portrait d'André Derain〉 est un portrait d'amitié que Matisse peignit de son confrère André Derain.
Les deux peintres échangèrent des portraits l'un de l'autre en même temps, partageant ensemble leurs expériences fauves.
fr.back:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin colors of 〈Portrait d'André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with bold primary colors, render the subject in a wild, Fauvist manner.

The 〈Portrait d'André Derain〉 is a friendship portrait that Matisse painted of fellow painter André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing their Fauvist experiences together.

fr.original:
The dreamlike colors and overlapping images of 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and violet blend together, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 is a work Chagall painted of his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife.
A vivid bouquet of red and white flowers glows against a deep green and navy background, expressing the warmth of love.
fr.translated:
Les couleurs oniriques et les images superposées de 〈Les Amoureux aux fleurs〉 ont été appliquées.
Le rose, le bleu cobalt et le violet se fondent, effaçant la frontière entre rêve et réalité.

〈Les Amoureux aux fleurs〉 est une œuvre que Chagall peignit sur son amour pour Bella, sa muse et épouse toute sa vie.
Les amoureux superposés comme un rêve sur un fond réel expriment l'ivresse de l'amour.
fr.back:
The dreamlike colors and layered images of 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue and violet merge, blurring the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Les Amoureux aux fleurs〉 is a work Chagall painted about his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife.
The lovers superimposed like a dream on a real background express the intoxication of love.

fr.original:
The dreamlike contrast of red bouquets and blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and figures blend like apparitions, unfolding a festive fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and church spires of Chagall's hometown Vitebsk as a landscape of memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet, overlaid with the village scene, holds both nostalgia and joy.
fr.translated:
Le contraste onirique des bouquets rouges et du ciel nocturne bleu dans 〈La Mariée〉 a été appliqué.
Animaux et personnages se mêlent comme des apparitions, déployant une fantaisie festive.

〈La Mariée〉 capture les toits et les clochers de la ville natale de Chagall, Vitebsk, comme un paysage de mémoire.
La mariée tenant un bouquet rouge, superposée à la scène du village, renferme nostalgie et joie.
fr.back:
The dreamlike contrast of red bouquets and blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and characters mingle like apparitions, deploying a festive fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and steeples of Chagall's hometown, Vitebsk, as a landscape of memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet, superimposed on the village scene, contains nostalgia and joy.

fr.original:
The multiple perspectives and transparently overlapping images of 〈I and the Village〉 have been applied.
People, animals, and village scenery intermingle dreamlike, defying scale and proportion.

〈I and the Village〉 was painted in Paris as Chagall longed for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, capturing an endless yearning for home.
fr.translated:
Les perspectives multiples et les images transparentes superposées de 〈Moi et le village〉 ont été appliquées.
Personnes, animaux et paysages villageois se mêlent comme dans un rêve, défiant échelle et proportion.

〈Moi et le village〉 fut peint à Paris alors que Chagall était nostalgique de sa ville natale de Vitebsk.
Réalité et mémoire se fondent sur une seule toile, capturant un désir infini du foyer.
fr.back:
The multiple perspectives and superimposed transparent images of 〈Me and the village〉 were applied.
People, animals and village landscapes mingle as if in a dream, defying scale and proportion.

〈Moi et le village〉 was painted in Paris when Chagall was nostalgic for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, capturing an infinite longing for home.

fr.original:
The simultaneous front and side view of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 has been applied.
Intense primary color planes divide the face geometrically, creating Cubist tension.

〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 was painted by Picasso of his lover and fellow photographer Dora Maar.
The seated woman's front and side coexist in one face, revealing the complexity within.
fr.translated:
La vue simultanée de face et de profil de 〈Portrait de Dora Maar〉 a été appliquée.
Des couleurs primaires intenses remplissent les plans géométriques.

Dora Maar était photographe et artiste qui est devenue la muse et l'amante de Picasso pendant la Seconde Guerre mondiale.
〈Portrait de Dora Maar〉 capture son visage sous plusieurs angles simultanément—expression visuelle de la complexité psychologique d'une personne.

Picasso a ensuite dit : 'Dora, pour moi, pleurait toujours.' Ce tableau est un monument à son intelligence et à sa souffrance.
fr.back:
The simultaneous front and profile view of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 was applied.
Intense primary colors fill the geometric planes.

Dora Maar was a photographer and artist who became Picasso's muse and lover during the Second World War.
〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 captures her face from several angles simultaneously-a visual expression of a person's psychological complexity.

Picasso later said, 'Dora, for me, was always crying.' This painting is a monument to her intelligence and suffering.

fr.original:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Vivid reds, greens, and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.
fr.translated:
Le regard frontal intense et les couleurs folkloriques mexicaines de 〈Moi et mes perroquets〉 ont été appliqués.
Des rouges, verts et bleus vifs remplissent la toile aux côtés du feuillage tropical.

〈Moi et mes perroquets〉 est un autoportrait dans lequel Frida est entourée de quatre perroquets.
Les perroquets sont des messagers d'amour dans le folklore mexicain, et des compagnons fidèles dans la solitude de Frida.
fr.back:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Vivid reds, greens and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Moi et mes parroquets〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.

fr.original:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 have been applied.
Amid dark green leaves, her intense eyes and connected eyebrows reveal her identity.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait Frida painted with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were surrogates for the children she could never have.
fr.translated:
Le regard frontal et le luxuriant fond tropical de 〈Autoportrait aux singes〉 ont été appliqués.
Parmi les feuilles vert foncé, ses yeux intenses et ses sourcils joints révèlent son identité.

〈Autoportrait aux singes〉 est un autoportrait que Frida peignit avec ses singes de compagnie.
Dans la mythologie mexicaine, les singes symbolisent le désir, mais pour Frida ils remplaçaient les enfants qu'elle ne pouvait pas avoir.
fr.back:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Autoportrait aux singes〉 have been applied.
Among the dark green leaves, his intense eyes and joined eyebrows reveal his identity.

〈Autoportrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait that Frida painted with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology, monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they replaced the children she couldn't have.

fr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of 〈In the Car〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill the canvas like a comic book panel.

〈In the Car〉 was created by enlarging a panel from the comic Girls' Romances onto canvas.
Lichtenstein deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image instead of narrative.
fr.translated:
Les points Ben-Day et les contours noirs épais de 〈Dans la voiture〉 ont été appliqués.
Seules des couleurs primaires pures remplissent les contours épais—comme un panneau de bande dessinée agrandi.

〈Dans la voiture〉 capture un couple dans une voiture dans le style de bande dessinée caractéristique de Lichtenstein.
Les expressions faciales plates et la distance émotionnelle entre les deux figures créent l'ironie froide caractéristique de Lichtenstein.

Cette peinture pose la question : sommes-nous vraiment connectés, ou ne sommes-nous que des images de connexion ?
fr.back:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of 〈Dans la voiture〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors fill the thick outlines-like an enlarged comic-book panel.

〈Dans la voiture〉 captures a couple in a car in Lichtenstein's characteristic cartoon style.
The flat facial expressions and emotional distance between the two figures create Lichtenstein's characteristic cold irony.

This painting poses the question: are we really connected, or are we just images of connection?

fr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narrative of speech bubbles in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flatly within thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, which sold for $30,000—five times the going rate.
It is now displayed as a centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.
fr.translated:
Les points Ben-Day et la narration de bande dessinée des bulles de dialogue dans 〈M-Peut-être〉 ont été appliqués.
Les couleurs primaires remplissent à plat dans des contours épais, recréant la texture d'une page imprimée.

〈M-Peut-être〉 était une œuvre que Lichtenstein conservait dans sa collection personnelle, vendue 30 000 $—cinq fois le prix habituel.
Elle est maintenant exposée comme pièce maîtresse de la collection Pop Art au Museum Ludwig de Cologne, en Allemagne.
fr.back:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narration of the dialogue bubbles in 〈M-Peut-être〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flat in thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Peut-être〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, sold for $30,000-five times the usual price.
It is now on display as the centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at the Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.

fr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterwork created just after Lichtenstein's first solo show in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: "Is He the Worst Artist in the U.S.?"—about him.
fr.translated:
Les points Ben-Day et les intenses contrastes de couleurs primaires de 〈Oublie ça !〉 ont été appliqués.
Les épais contours noirs fixent la forme comme un panneau de bande dessinée, comprimant l'émotion dramatiquement.

〈Oublie ça !〉 est un chef-d'œuvre précoce créé juste après la première exposition solo de Lichtenstein en 1962.
Cette même année, le magazine Life titrait : 'Est-il le pire artiste des États-Unis ?'—à son sujet.
fr.back:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Oublie ça!"〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic-book panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterpiece created just after Lichtenstein's first solo exhibition in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: 'Is he America's worst artist?'-about him.

fr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow separate cleanly within black outlines, as crisp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work Lichtenstein borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, overturning the value of what he once called "used canvas."
fr.translated:
Les points Ben-Day et la composition de plans de couleur plate de 〈Ohhh...Très bien...〉 ont été appliqués.
Le rouge, le bleu et le jaune se séparent nettement dans des contours noirs, aussi nets qu'une page imprimée.

〈Ohhh...Très bien...〉 est une œuvre de 1964 empruntée au numéro 88 de Secret Hearts.
Elle s'est vendue 42,6 millions de dollars chez Christie's en 2010, renversant la valeur de ce qu'il appelait autrefois 'toile usagée'.
fr.back:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Very good...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue and yellow separate sharply into black outlines, as sharp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Very good...〉 is a 1964 work borrowed from Secret Hearts issue 88.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, reversing the value of what he once called 'used canvas'.

fr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in a comic-like manner, recreating the texture of print.

〈Still Life〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre as homage to Picasso.
"There's no mood in my still lifes—it's just lemons and grapefruits," he said.
fr.translated:
Les points Ben-Day et les contours épais créant une expression plate dans 〈Nature morte〉 ont été appliqués.
Les plans de couleurs primaires simplifient les objets à la manière d'une bande dessinée, recréant la texture de l'impression.

〈Nature morte〉 fait partie d'une série des années 1970 dans laquelle Lichtenstein réinterprétait un genre traditionnel en hommage à Picasso.
'Il n'y a pas d'ambiance dans mes natures mortes—ce ne sont que des citrons et des pamplemousses', disait-il.
fr.back:
Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating a flat expression in 〈Nature morte〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in comic-book fashion, recreating the texture of print.

〈Nature morte〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre in homage to Picasso.
'There's no atmosphere in my still lifes-it's all lemons and grapefruit,' he used to say.

fr.translated: Style Van Gogh
fr.back: Van Gogh style

fr.original:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

Van Gogh left over 40 self-portraits, confronting his inner self most honestly through them.
The eyes gazing directly forward show unwavering will even amid suffering.
fr.translated:
Le style expressif de Van Gogh, avec ses touches en spirale et ses couleurs intenses, a été appliqué.
Les touches fortes et directionnelles créent des textures vibrantes pleines d'énergie émotionnelle.

Le contraste entre bleu profond et jaune éclatant définit la palette d'un artiste qui peignit avec le cœur.
Un style inimitable où chaque trace révèle l'âme tourmentée et passionnée du peintre.
fr.back:
Van Gogh's expressive style, with its spiral strokes and intense colors, has been applied.
Strong, directional strokes create vibrant textures full of emotional energy.

The contrast between deep blue and bright yellow defines the palette of an artist who painted from the heart.
An inimitable style in which every trace reveals the painter's tormented and passionate soul.

fr.translated: Style Klimt
fr.back: Klimt style

fr.original:
Klimt's real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on men's clothing contrast with curves and circles on women's, expressing both opposition and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decoration wrap the figure in a mystical golden world.
Within this decoration, sensuality and divinity coexist.
fr.translated:
Le style décoratif de Klimt, avec feuille d'or et motifs géométriques, a été appliqué.
Les mosaïques dorées et les spirales ornementales créent un monde de luxe sensuel.

Des figures planes enveloppées de motifs où l'organique et le géométrique s'entrelacent.
L'esthétique de la Sécession viennoise, où la décoration devient expression de l'âme.
fr.back:
Klimt's decorative style, with gold leaf and geometric motifs, has been applied.
Golden mosaics and ornamental spirals create a world of sensual luxury.

Flat figures enveloped in patterns where the organic and the geometric intertwine.
The aesthetics of the Viennese Secession, where decoration becomes an expression of the soul.

fr.translated: Style Munch
fr.back: Munch style

fr.original:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors have been applied.
Blood-red skies and wavering landscapes visualize inner emotions.

Munch wrote in his diary that while walking by the Oslo fjord, he "heard an enormous scream pass through nature."
Before his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.
fr.translated:
Le style expressionniste de Munch, avec ses couleurs inquiétantes et ses formes ondulantes, a été appliqué.
Les lignes sinueuses et les tons maladifs transmettent directement l'angoisse psychologique.

Des paysages qui se distordent au rythme des émotions internes, où rien ne reste stable.
Le langage visuel de l'anxiété, pionnier dans la conversion de l'intérieur de l'âme en image.
fr.back:
Munch's expressionist style, with its eerie colors and undulating forms, has been applied.
The sinuous lines and sickly tones directly convey psychological anguish.

Landscapes that distort to the rhythm of internal emotions, where nothing remains stable.
The visual language of anxiety, pioneering the conversion of the soul's interior into image.

fr.translated: Style Matisse
fr.back: Matisse style

fr.original:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors have been applied.
Rather than anatomical accuracy, focus is on the energy of movement, with color directly conveying emotion.

Just as Matisse erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room, he the boundary between table and wall, he chose harmony of color planes over perspective.
Before his canvas, colors sing out.
fr.translated:
Le style fauviste de Matisse, avec ses couleurs pures et ses formes simplifiées, a été appliqué.
Des primaires audacieux sont disposés sans respecter la réalité, et les formes sont réduites à leur essence.

La ligne se fait danse et la couleur se fait musique — chaque élément chante avec indépendance.
L'art de la joie pure, où la couleur est libre de toute obligation descriptive.
fr.back:
Matisse's Fauvist style, with its pure colors and simplified forms, has been applied.
Bold primaries are arranged without respect for reality, and shapes are reduced to their essence.

Line becomes dance and color becomes music - each element sings with independence.
The art of pure joy, where color is free of any descriptive obligation.

fr.translated: Style Chagall
fr.back: Chagall style

fr.original:
Chagall's dreamlike atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel-like colors has been applied.
Bouquets, lovers, and hometown scenes blend together like memories, lifting the weight of reality.

Chagall composed his canvases with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space halt before love, and the world fills with dreamlike light.
fr.translated:
Le style onirique de Chagall, avec ses couleurs pastel et ses figures flottantes, a été appliqué.
Amoureux, animaux et toits flottent dans un espace sans gravité, teinté de nostalgie.

Roses, violets et bleus se fondent comme des souvenirs qui se mêlent en rêves.
Un monde où l'amour et la mémoire défient les lois de la physique et du temps.
fr.back:
Chagall's dreamlike style, with its pastel colors and floating figures, has been applied.
Lovers, animals and roofs float in a gravity-free space tinged with nostalgia.

Roses, purples and blues melt together like memories blending into dreams.
A world where love and memory defy the laws of physics and time.

fr.translated: Style Picasso
fr.back: Picasso style

fr.original:
Picasso's Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Like Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple viewpoints coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, Picasso conveyed the terror of war immediately. This destructive deconstruction was itself creation.
Five centuries of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.
fr.translated:
Le style cubiste de Picasso, avec ses formes géométriques et sa perspective multiple, a été appliqué.
Les visages et les corps se fragmentent en plans angulaires, montrant plusieurs points de vue simultanément.

Des lignes audacieuses et des couleurs contrastées construisent une réalité déconstruite et recomposée.
La révolution visuelle qui détruisit 500 ans de perspective traditionnelle.
fr.back:
Picasso's cubist style, with its geometric shapes and multiple perspectives, has been applied.
Faces and bodies are fragmented into angular planes, showing several points of view simultaneously.

Bold lines and contrasting colors construct a deconstructed and recomposed reality.
The visual revolution that destroyed 500 years of traditional perspective.

fr.translated: Style Frida Kahlo
fr.back: Frida Kahlo style

fr.original:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and communion with animals have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird, thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys as family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not mere backgrounds but companions offering comfort amid suffering.
fr.translated:
Le style symbolique de Frida, avec ses couleurs mexicaines et son regard frontal intense, a été appliqué.
Les rouges, verts et jaunes de la tradition mexicaine encadrent une iconographie profondément personnelle.

Chaque élément — épines, animaux, plantes — est un symbole codé de douleur, de résistance et d'amour.
L'art de la confession où le corps brisé devient territoire d'identité et de lutte.
fr.back:
Frida's symbolic style, with its Mexican colors and intense frontal gaze, has been applied.
The reds, greens and yellows of the Mexican tradition frame a deeply personal iconography.

Each element - thorns, animals, plants - is a coded symbol of pain, resistance and love.
The art of confession, where the broken body becomes the territory of identity and struggle.

fr.translated: Style Lichtenstein
fr.back: Lichtenstein style

fr.original:
Lichtenstein's Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines, and primary colors have been applied.
Like the glamorous couple In the Car, every image becomes a comic book panel.

Lichtenstein's dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of comic heroines, everyday moments transform into dramatic scenes.
Emotion exploding within mechanical dots and lines—that is Lichtenstein's paradox.
fr.translated:
Le style Pop Art de Lichtenstein, avec ses points Ben-Day et ses contours épais, a été appliqué.
Des trames de points réguliers et des couleurs primaires en aplat transforment l'esthétique de la bande dessinée en art.

Noir, rouge, bleu et jaune en compositions graphiques qui questionnent ce qui est art et ce qui est culture populaire.
L'essence du Pop Art qui effaça la frontière entre le musée et la culture de masse.
fr.back:
Lichtenstein's Pop Art style, with its Ben-Day dots and thick outlines, has been applied.
Regular dot patterns and solid primary colors transform comic-book aesthetics into art.

Black, red, blue and yellow in graphic compositions that question what is art and what is popular culture.
The essence of Pop Art, which erased the boundary between the museum and mass culture.

id.original: The night sky painted in swirls
id.back: The night sky painted in swirls

id.translated: Teras Kafe di Malam Hari (Café Terrace at Night)

id.original: A warm glow of yellow under starlight
id.translated: Cahaya kuning hangat di bawah sinar bintang
id.back: Warm yellow light in the starlight

id.translated: Bunga yang dicium matahari, membara penuh gairah

id.original: Gazing into the mirror of the soul
id.back: Staring into the mirror of the soul

id.translated: Ladang Gandum dengan Cemara (Wheat Field with Cypresses)

id.original: Golden fields dancing in the wind
id.back: Gold fields dance in the wind

id.original: The Kiss · Judith · The Tree of Life
An eternal kiss dissolved in gold
id.translated: Ciuman abadi yang larut dalam emas
id.back: An eternal kiss that dissolves in gold

id.original: Golden branches singing the song of life
id.back: Golden branches sing the song of life

id.original: Between the sacred and sensual, golden allure
id.translated: Antara yang sakral dan sensual, pesona emas
id.back: Between the sacred and the sensual, the charm of gold

id.original: An artist of the soul, born from loss
The Scream · Madonna · The Dance of Life
A soul's cry beneath a blood-red sky
id.translated: Jiwa yang menangis di bawah langit merah darah
id.back: The soul that cries under the blood-red sky

id.original: A mysterious figure between life and death
id.translated: Sosok misterius antara hidup dan mati
id.back: A mysterious figure between life and death

id.original: A waltz of love and loss
The Dance · The Red Room · The Green Stripe
A single green line that declared a color revolution
id.translated: Satu garis hijau yang mendeklarasikan revolusi warna
id.back: One green stripe that declares a color revolution

id.translated: Wanita Berjas Ungu (Woman in a Purple Coat)

id.original: A feast of decoration ruled by red

id.translated: Potret André Derain (Portrait of André Derain)

id.original: A Fauvist colleague painted in the colors of the wild
id.translated: Rekan Fauviis dilukis dengan warna alam liar
id.back: Fauviis's colleagues are painted in wild colors

id.original: A painter of 100 years of love
Lovers with Flowers · I and the Village · La Mariée
Love glowing warmly within a bouquet
id.translated: Cinta yang bersinar hangat dalam sebuket bunga
id.back: Love that shines warmly in a bouquet of flowers

id.original: A bride floating between dream and reality
id.translated: Pengantin yang melayang antara mimpi dan kenyataan
id.back: A bride who drifts between dream and reality

id.translated: Aku dan Desa (I and the Village)

id.original: Memories of home drifting like a dream
id.translated: Kenangan rumah yang melayang seperti mimpi
id.back: Memories of home that float like a dream
Revolutionary that deconstructs the view of

id.original: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon · Guernica · Portrait of Dora Maar
id.translated: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon · Guernica · Potret Dora Maar
id.back: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon - Guernica - Portrait of Dora Maar

id.translated: Potret Dora Maar (Portrait of Dora Maar)

id.original: A deconstructed portrait where front meets side
id.translated: Potret terdekonstruksi di mana depan bertemu samping
id.back: A deconstructed portrait where front meets side

id.translated: Potret diri yang menghadapi rasa sakit

id.back: Portrait of pain, embraced by a monkey

id.original: Revolution that started with Mickey Mouse

id.back: The man who turned comics into art

id.original: In the Car · M-Maybe · Forget It!
id.translated: In the Car · M-Maybe · Forget It!
