id.original: A single word of hesitation, a Pop Art drama
id.translated: Satu kata keraguan, drama Pop Art
id.back: One word of doubt, Pop Art drama

id.original: A farewell cry captured in a comic panel
id.translated: Jeritan perpisahan dalam satu panel komik
id.back: Farewell screams in one comic panel

id.original: Resignation and acceptance in one breath
id.translated: Kepasrahan dan penerimaan dalam satu napas
id.back: Surrender and acceptance in one breath

id.original: Still life reborn through Ben-Day dots
id.translated: Benda mati terlahir kembali lewat titik Ben-Day
id.back: Inanimate objects are reborn through Ben-Day points

id.original:
Van Gogh is a defining figure of Post-Impressionism.
He began painting at 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, yet only one sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces like The Starry Night, Sunflowers, and Café Terrace at Night were all born in poverty and mental anguish.

Van Gogh died at 37, but his art profoundly influenced Expressionism and Fauvism.
"I put my heart and soul into my work." — A painter who forged immortal beauty from suffering.
id.translated:
Teknik impasto tebal dan sapuan kuas berputar telah diterapkan.
Kontrasi intens kuning dan biru mengekspresikan emosi langsung dari jiwa.

Van Gogh adalah tokoh penentu Post-Impressionisme.
Ia mulai melukis pada usia 27 dan menghasilkan 900 lukisan minyak hanya dalam sepuluh tahun, namun hanya satu yang terjual semasa hidupnya.
Karya agung seperti The Starry Night, Sunflowers, dan Café Terrace at Night semuanya lahir dalam kemiskinan dan penderitaan jiwa.

"Aku menaruh hati dan jiwaku dalam karyaku." — Pelukis yang menempa keindahan abadi dari penderitaan.
id.back:
Thick impasto techniques and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrasts of yellow and blue express the direct emotion of the soul.

Van Gogh was the defining figure of Post-Impressionism.
He began painting at the age of 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, yet only one was sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces such as The Starry Night, Sunflowers, and Café Terrace at Night were all born in poverty and mental anguish.

"I put my heart and soul into my work." - A painter who forged eternal beauty from suffering.

id.original:
Klimt was the leading Art Nouveau painter of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
Born the son of a goldsmith, he applied real gold leaf to canvas, creating an opulent decorative world inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
He left behind golden masterpieces where sensuality and divinity coexist, including The Kiss, Judith, and The Tree of Life.

Klimt founded the Vienna Secession, challenging the conservative art establishment.
"To every age its art, to every art its freedom." — A painter who captured fin-de-siècle beauty in gold.
id.translated:
Daun emas nyata dan pola geometris terinspirasi mosaik Byzantium telah diterapkan.
Persegi panjang di sisi dan lingkaran di atas, detail dekoratif kini menyelimuti tubuh Anda.

Klimt adalah pelukis Art Nouveau terkemuka di Wina akhir abad ke-19.
Ia menciptakan teknik unik yang menggabungkan lukisan minyak dengan potongan emas.

"Setiap zaman seninya, setiap seni kebebasannya." — Pelukis yang menangkap keindahan akhir abad dalam emas.
id.back:
Real gold leaf and Byzantine mosaic-inspired geometric patterns have been applied.
Rectangles on the sides and circles on the top, decorative details now envelop your body.

Klimt was a leading Art Nouveau painter in late 19th century Vienna.
He created a unique technique that combined oil painting with gold pieces.

"Every age its art, every art its freedom." - The painter who captured the beauty of the end of the century in gold.

id.original:
Munch is the defining Expressionist painter from Norway.
He lost his mother at five and his sister at fourteen, and the memory of these losses dominated his canvases for life.
His works, including The Scream, Madonna, and The Dance of Life, form "The Frieze of Life" — a cycle of love, death, and anxiety.

Munch declared, "I paint not what I see, but what I saw,", exploring the deepest human emotions.
"Illness, madness, and death were the dark angels that watched over my cradle." — A painter who transformed inner darkness into art.
id.translated:
Kurva berputar, bentuk terdistorsi, dan warna intens telah diterapkan.
Langit merah darah dan garis bergelombang mengekspresikan kecemasan dan emosi yang mendalam.

Munch adalah pelopor Ekspresionisme yang melukis bukan apa yang dilihat, melainkan apa yang dirasakan.
Ia kehilangan ibunya pada usia 5 tahun dan kakak perempuannya pada usia 14 tahun—trauma itu menjadi sumber abadi karyanya.

"Penyakit, kegilaan, dan kematian adalah malaikat hitam yang menjaga ayunanku." — Pelukis yang mengubah kegelapan batin menjadi seni.
id.back:
Swirling curves, distorted shapes, and intense colors have been applied.
The blood-red sky and wavy lines express anxiety and deep emotion.

Munch was a pioneer of Expressionism who painted not what was seen, but what was felt.
He lost his mother at the age of 5 and his older sister at the age of 14-the trauma became the eternal source of his work.

"Sickness, madness, and death are the dark angels that guard my swing." - A painter who transforms inner darkness into art.

id.original:
Matisse is the leading Fauvist painter of France.
At 21, he began painting with a box of colors his mother gave him while bedridden, later recalling it felt like "discovering paradise."
The 1905 cry of "Wild beasts!" became the birth of Fauvism, and he left behind color-revolutionary masterpieces like The Dance, The Green Stripe, and The Red Room.

Matisse conveyed emotion through pure color alone, pushing the decorative potential of painting to its limit.
"The purpose of color is not to describe form but to express emotion." — A painter who sang joy through color.
id.translated:
Bentuk yang disederhanakan dan warna primer yang berani telah diterapkan.
Alih-alih akurasi anatomis, fokusnya adalah pada ekspresi emosi melalui warna.

Matisse adalah pemimpin Fauvisme—gerakan yang menggunakan warna liar untuk mengekspresikan perasaan daripada realitas.
Ia menemukan lukisan saat pulih dari sakit usus buntu pada usia 20 tahun dan tidak pernah berhenti sejak saat itu.

"Tujuan warna bukan menggambarkan bentuk, tapi mengekspresikan emosi." — Pelukis yang menyanyikan kegembiraan melalui warna.
id.back:
Simplified shapes and bold primary colors have been applied.
Instead of anatomical accuracy, the focus is on the expression of emotions through color.

Matisse was the leader of Fauvism-a movement that used wild colors to express feelings rather than reality.
He discovered painting while recovering from appendicitis at the age of 20 and never stopped since then.

"The purpose of color is not to depict shapes, but to express emotions." - A painter who sings joy through color.

id.original:
Chagall was a painter of love and dreams.
Born in a Russian Jewish village, he moved to Paris and never put down his brush through two World Wars and exile.
His love for his wife Bella was a lifelong source of his art, leaving behind masterpieces like The Birthday, La Mariée, and I and the Village — canvases where bouquets and lovers float in midair.

Chagall worked until 97, enchanting the world with the Paris Opéra ceiling and stained glass windows.
"In our life there is a single color, as on an artist's palette, which provides the meaning of life and art — the color of love." — A poet who erased the boundary between dream and reality.
id.translated:
Atmosfer seperti mimpi dari merah muda, biru kobalt, dan warna permata telah diterapkan.
Buket, kekasih yang melayang, dan hewan fantasi mengapung di antara batas mimpi dan kenyataan.

Chagall lahir di Vitebsk, Belarus, dan menemukan semua warna hidupnya di sana.
Cintanya pada Bella, istrinya seumur hidup, menjadi tema abadi dalam karyanya.

"Dalam hidup kita hanya ada satu warna — warna cinta." — Penyair yang menghapus batas antara mimpi dan kenyataan.
id.back:
Dreamlike atmospheres of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel tones have been applied.
A bouquet, floating lovers, and fantasy animals float between the border of dream and reality.

Chagall was born in Vitebsk, Belarus, and found all the colors of his life there.
His love for Bella, his wife for life, became an eternal theme in his work.

"In our life there is only one color - the color of love." - The poet who erased the boundaries between dreams and reality.

id.original:
Picasso co-founded Cubism and changed the direction of modern art.
In his 91-year life, he left approximately 50,000 works. Legend has it that at 13, his art teacher father saw his talent and broke his own brushes.
He opened Cubism with Les Demoiselles d'Avignon, denounced war with Guernica, and reached the pinnacle of deconstruction with Portrait of Dora Maar.

Through Blue Period, Rose Period, Cubism, and Surrealism, Picasso constantly reinvented himself.
"Good artists copy, great artists steal." — A painter who broke all rules and created new ones.
id.translated:
Teknik Kubisme yang mendekonstruksi subjek menjadi bidang geometris dan menampilkan depan dan samping secara bersamaan telah diterapkan.
Wajah Anda kini ada di mana-mana sekaligus—dilihat dari segala sudut sekaligus.

Picasso adalah seniman paling berpengaruh abad ke-20.
Pada usia 13 tahun, ia sudah melukis lebih baik dari ayahnya yang seorang guru seni.

Bersama Braque, ia mengembangkan Kubisme—sebuah revolusi yang menghancurkan perspektif tradisional dan membuka pintu menuju seni abstrak.
id.back:
The Cubist technique of deconstructing the subject into a geometric plane and showing the front and side simultaneously has been applied.
Your face is now everywhere at once-seen from all angles at once.

Picasso was the most influential artist of the 20th century.
By the age of 13, he was already painting better than his art teacher father.

Together with Braque, he developed Cubism-a revolution that shattered traditional perspectives and opened the door to abstract art.

id.original:
Frida Kahlo is Mexico's most original painter and a feminist icon.
She contracted polio at 6 and at 18 suffered a bus accident that shattered her spine and pelvis, enduring over 30 surgeries throughout her life.
She began painting while bedridden, looking at a mirror on the ceiling — and much of her work, including Me and My Parrots, The Broken Column, and Self-Portrait with Monkeys, are self-portraits.

Frida's art, with its honest portrayal of pain, love, and identity, continues to resonate deeply today.
"Feet, what do I need them for if I have wings to fly?" — A painter who transformed pain into art.
id.translated:
Warna folk Meksiko tradisional, dedaunan tropis, dan persekutuan dengan hewan telah diterapkan.
Dalam Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace, rasa sakit menjadi seni.

Pada usia 18 tahun, Frida Kahlo mengalami kecelakaan bus mengerikan yang menghancurkan tulang belakangnya.
Selama pemulihan, ia mulai melukis—mengubah rasa sakit menjadi seni.

"Kaki, untuk apa jika aku punya sayap untuk terbang?" — Pelukis tak terkalahkan yang mengubah rasa sakit menjadi seni.
id.back:
Traditional Mexican folk colors, tropical foliage and animal communion have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace, pain becomes art.

At the age of 18, Frida Kahlo had a terrible bus accident that shattered her spine.
During her recovery, she began painting-turning pain into art.

"Legs, what are they for if I have wings to fly?" - The invincible painter who turned pain into art.

id.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who broke the boundaries of fine art with comic book panels.
When his son pointed to a Mickey Mouse comic and asked, "Can you draw as well as this, Dad?", he actually began transferring comics to canvas.
With In the Car, M-Maybe, and Forget It!, he shook the art world by blowing up comic panels onto massive canvases.

Lichtenstein, with Ben-Day dots and bold outlines, overturned the hierarchy between popular culture and high art.
"I don't draw comics, I paint pictures about comics." — A painter who erased the line between pop culture and fine art.
id.translated:
Teknik pop art menggunakan titik Ben-Day dari komik cetak, kontur hitam tebal, dan warna primer telah diterapkan.
Wajah Anda kini terasa seperti panel komik—cerah, berani, dan penuh ekspresi dramatis.

Pada tahun 1961, Lichtenstein melukis komik Mickey Mouse dan dunia seni terguncang.
Majalah Life bertanya: 'Apakah ia seniman terburuk di AS?'

"Aku tidak menggambar komik, aku melukis tentang komik." — Pelukis yang menghapus batas antara budaya pop dan seni murni.
id.back:
Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black contours, and primary colors have been applied.
Your face now feels like a comic panel-bright, bold, and full of dramatic expression.

In 1961, Lichtenstein painted a Mickey Mouse comic and the art world was shaken.
Life magazine asked: 'Is he the worst artist in the US?'

"I don't draw comics, I paint about comics." - A painter who erased the boundaries between pop culture and fine art.

id.original:
The swirling impasto brushstrokes of 〈The Starry Night〉 have been applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow unleashes the energy of the night.

〈The Starry Night〉 was Van Gogh's reimagined vision of the landscape beyond his asylum window at Saint-Rémy.
Rather than painting the actual night sky, he projected his inner turmoil onto the stars.
id.translated:
Teknik impasto berputar dari 〈The Starry Night〉 telah diterapkan.
Kontrast intens biru kobalt dan kuning krom melepaskan energi malam.

〈The Starry Night〉 adalah visi reimajinasi Van Gogh atas lanskap di luar jendela rumah sakitnya di Saint-Rémy.
Alih-alih melukis langit malam nyata, ia memproyeksikan gejolak batinnya ke bintang-bintang.
id.back:
The swirling impasto technique of 〈The Starry Night〉 has been applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow releases the energy of the night.

〈The Starry Night〉 is Van Gogh's reimagined vision of the landscape outside the window of his hospital in Saint-Rémy.
Instead of painting a real night sky, he projected his inner turmoil onto the stars.

id.original:
The complementary color nightscape technique of 〈Café Terrace at Night〉—without using black—has been applied.
Yellow gaslights and deep blue night sky express darkness through color contrast alone.

〈Café Terrace at Night〉 is the first outdoor night scene Van Gogh painted under starlight at Arles' Place du Forum.
The empty chairs on the terrace and passing figures hold both the stillness and warmth of the night.
id.translated:
Teknik pemandangan malam warna komplementer dari 〈Café Terrace at Night〉 telah diterapkan—tanpa menggunakan hitam.
Lampu gas kuning dan langit biru gelap mengekspresikan kegelapan melalui kontras warna saja.

〈Café Terrace at Night〉 adalah pemandangan malam luar ruangan pertama yang dilukis Van Gogh di bawah cahaya bintang di Place du Forum, Arles.
Kursi kosong di teras dan sosok yang berlalu menyimpan ketenangan sekaligus kehangatan malam.
id.back:
The complementary color night scene technique of 〈Café Terrace at Night〉 has been applied-without using black.
The yellow gas lamp and dark blue sky express darkness through color contrast alone.

〈Café Terrace at Night〉 is the first outdoor night scene Van Gogh painted under starlight at Place du Forum, Arles.
The empty chairs on the terrace and the passing figures hold both the tranquility and warmth of the night.

id.original:
The subtle tonal variations of a single chrome yellow in 〈Sunflowers〉 have been applied.
Thickly layered paint captures the texture of the petals as they are.

〈Sunflowers〉 is a series Van Gogh painted to decorate the Yellow House in Arles for Gauguin's arrival.
Full blooms, wilted flowers, and seeds together reveal the cycle of life.
id.translated:
Variasi nada halus kuning krom tunggal dalam 〈Sunflowers〉 telah diterapkan.
Cat yang dilapis tebal menangkap tekstur kelopak apa adanya.

〈Sunflowers〉 adalah seri yang dilukis Van Gogh untuk mendekorasi Yellow House di Arles menyambut kedatangan Gauguin.
Bunga mekar penuh, bunga layu, dan biji bersama-sama mengungkap siklus kehidupan.
id.back:
Variations of a single chrome yellow subtle tone in 〈Sunflowers〉 have been applied.
The thickly coated paint captures the texture of the petals as they are.

〈Sunflowers〉 is a series that Van Gogh painted to decorate the Yellow House in Arles to welcome Gauguin.
Flowers in full bloom, withered flowers, and seeds together reveal the cycle of life.

id.original:
The swirling background and thick impasto brushstrokes of 〈Self-Portrait〉 have been applied.
Cool tones of blue and green convey both solitude and resolve.

〈Self-Portrait〉 was painted when Van Gogh, too poor to afford models, used himself as subject.
The eyes gazing directly forward and tightly closed lips show unwavering will amid suffering.
id.translated:
Latar belakang berputar dan sapuan impasto tebal dari 〈Self-Portrait〉 telah diterapkan.
Tona dingin hijau dan biru menciptakan intensitas psikologis.

〈Self-Portrait〉 adalah cermin di mana Van Gogh menatap dirinya sendiri—dan kita.
Selama 10 tahun berkarya, ia melukis lebih dari 30 self-portrait, masing-masing mengungkap lapisan jiwa yang berbeda.

Portret ini, dengan latar biru berputar, dianggap sebagai puncak kemampuan teknisnya dan ekspresi batinnya.
id.back:
The rotating background and thick impasto strokes of 〈Self-Portrait〉 have been applied.
The cold tones of green and blue create psychological intensity.

〈Self-Portrait〉 is a mirror in which Van Gogh looks at himself-and us.
During his 10 years of work, he painted more than 30 self-portraits, each revealing a different layer of the soul.

This portrait, with its swirling blue background, is considered the pinnacle of his technical ability and inner expression.

id.original:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of 〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 have been applied.
Wind-blown wheat and flame-like cypresses convey nature's vital energy.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 is a landscape Van Gogh repeatedly painted near the asylum at Saint-Rémy.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the vertical cypress connect earth and sky.
id.translated:
Sapuan kuas melengkung dinamis dan kontras warna komplementer dari 〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 telah diterapkan.
Gandum yang tertiup angin dan cemara seperti nyala api menyampaikan energi vital alam.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 adalah lanskap yang berulang kali dilukis Van Gogh di dekat rumah sakit jiwa Saint-Rémy.
Horizon emas ladang gandum dan cemara vertikal menghubungkan bumi dan langit.
id.back:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrast of 〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 have been applied.
The wind-blown wheat and flame-like cypresses convey the vital energy of nature.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 is a landscape that Van Gogh repeatedly painted near the Saint-Rémy mental hospital.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the vertical cypresses connect earth and sky.

id.original:
The gold leaf background and geometric decorative patterns of 〈The Kiss〉 have been applied.
Rectangles on the man's robe contrast with curves and circles on the woman's, creating opposition and harmony.

〈The Kiss〉 is Klimt's masterpiece from his "Golden Phase," inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The lovers' embrace wrapped in gold holds both the ecstasy of love and the anxiety of its passing.
id.translated:
Latar belakang daun emas dan pola dekoratif geometris dari 〈The Kiss〉 telah diterapkan.
Persegi panjang dan lingkaran bergantian menciptakan jubah emas yang menyelimuti dua figur.

〈The Kiss〉 adalah mahakarya Art Nouveau yang kini menjadi salah satu lukisan paling dicintai di dunia.
Emas nyata bersinar di atas kanvas—Klimt mencampur teknik lukis tradisional dengan material mewah.

Dua figur yang saling memeluk di tepi tebing menjadi simbol universal cinta dan kerentanan.
id.back:
The gold leaf background and geometric decorative patterns from 〈The Kiss〉 have been applied.
Alternating rectangles and circles create a golden cloak that envelops the two figures.

〈The Kiss〉 is an Art Nouveau masterpiece that is now one of the most beloved paintings in the world.
Real gold shines on the canvas-Klimt mixes traditional painting techniques with luxurious materials.

Two figures embracing each other on the edge of a cliff become a universal symbol of love and vulnerability.

id.original:
The spiral curves and golden decorative patterns of 〈The Tree of Life〉 have been applied.
Triangles, circles, and spirals repeat, creating a rhythmic flow of life's energy.

〈The Tree of Life〉 was created as a dining room mural for the Stoclet Palace in Brussels.
The endlessly branching spiral limbs are a metaphor for birth, death, and the cycle of life.
id.translated:
Kurva spiral dan pola dekoratif emas dari 〈The Tree of Life〉 telah diterapkan.
Segitiga, mata, dan spiral berlapis-lapis menciptakan tapestri kehidupan dan kematian.

〈The Tree of Life〉 adalah karya pusat dari Frieze Stoclet—dekorasi dinding megah yang Klimt buat untuk sebuah istana di Brussels.
Pohon bercabang spiral melambangkan siklus kehidupan abadi.

Ini adalah contoh sempurna dari Gesamtkunstwerk—karya seni total yang menyatukan lukisan, arsitektur, dan kerajinan.
id.back:
Spiral curves and gold decorative patterns from 〈The Tree of Life〉 have been applied.
Multi-layered triangles, eyes and spirals create a tapestry of life and death.

〈The Tree of Life〉 is the centerpiece of the Frieze Stoclet-a magnificent wall decoration that Klimt created for a palace in Brussels.
The spiral branching tree symbolizes the eternal cycle of life.

It is a perfect example of Gesamtkunstwerk-a total work of art that brings together painting, architecture, and craft.

id.original:
The gold leaf decoration and jewel-like sensual colors of 〈Judith〉 have been applied.
Transparent veils and gold necklaces create tension between the sacred and the sensual.

〈Judith〉 is Klimt's reinterpretation of the biblical heroine as a sensual woman.
Half-closed eyes and a provocative smile completely overturn the piety of traditional religious painting.
id.translated:
Dekorasi daun emas dan warna sensual permata dari 〈Judith〉 telah diterapkan.
Velvet transparan dan jubah emas menciptakan ekspresi daya dan hasrat yang kontradiktif.

〈Judith〉 adalah pejuang Perjanjian Lama yang memenggal kepala Holofernes—namun Klimt melukisnya bukan sebagai pahlawan, melainkan sebagai femme fatale yang ekstatik.

Mata setengah tertutup dan ekspresi bibir terbuka menciptakan ambiguitas antara kenikmatan dan kemenangan yang menjadi ciri khas Klimt.
id.back:
Gold leaf decorations and the sensual color of gems from 〈Judith〉 have been applied.
The transparent velvet and gold cape create a contradictory expression of power and desire.

〈Judith〉 is the Old Testament warrior who beheaded Holofernes-but Klimt painted her not as a hero, but as an ecstatic femme fatale.

Her half-closed eyes and open-lipped expression create the ambiguity between pleasure and triumph that characterizes Klimt.

id.original:
The undulating curves and distorted forms of 〈The Scream〉 have been applied.
A blood-red sky and rippling background contrast with the straight railing, creating extreme anxiety.

〈The Scream〉 captures the terror Munch felt when the sky turned blood-red during a walk.
The open-mouthed figure's cry is expressed not as sound but as a tremor passing through all existence.
id.translated:
Kurva bergelombang dan bentuk terdistorsi dari 〈The Scream〉 telah diterapkan.
Langit merah darah dan garis bergetar mengekspresikan kecemasan eksistensial murni.

〈The Scream〉 lahir dari pengalaman nyata Munch—suatu sore di Oslo, ia merasakan alam 'berteriak' padanya.
Tiga versi dalam cat dan satu dalam pastel—masing-masing menangkap momen ketika manusia merasakan kekosongan alam semesta.

Kini menjadi simbol visual kecemasan modern yang paling diakui di dunia.
id.back:
The undulating curves and distorted shapes from 〈The Scream〉 have been applied.
The blood-red sky and trembling lines express pure existential anxiety.

〈The Scream〉 was born from Munch's real experience-one afternoon in Oslo, he felt nature 'screaming' at him.
Three versions in paint and one in pastel-each captures a moment when man feels the emptiness of the universe.

It is now the world's most recognized visual symbol of modern anxiety.

id.original:
The red halo and undulating sensual curves of 〈Madonna〉 have been applied.
At the boundary between open and closed eyes, life and death merge into one.

〈Madonna〉 is Munch's reinterpretation of the Virgin Mary as a moment where sensuality and death intersect.
Closed eyes, parted lips, and a red halo reveal human desire rather than holiness.
id.translated:
Halo merah dan kurva sensual bergelombang dari 〈Madonna〉 telah diterapkan.
Di perbatasan antara kehidupan dan kematian, sosok itu berdiri dengan mata tertutup.

〈Madonna〉 adalah bukan Madonna agama—melainkan perayaan Munch atas kelahiran, cinta, dan kematian dalam satu sosok.
Gelombang merah menyelimuti sosok telanjang, sementara bingkai memperlihatkan janin dan tengkorak.

Ini adalah meditasi tentang siklus abadi kehidupan yang hanya bisa diungkapkan Munch dengan keberanian seperti ini.
id.back:
The red halo and wavy sensual curves of 〈Madonna〉 have been applied.
On the border between life and death, the figure stands with her eyes closed.

〈Madonna〉 is not a religious Madonna-it is Munch's celebration of birth, love, and death in one figure.
Red waves envelop the nude figure, while the frame reveals a fetus and skull.

It is a meditation on the eternal cycle of life that only Munch could express with such boldness.

id.original:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark colors in 〈The Dance of Life〉 has been applied.
With the central woman in a red dress as axis, innocence and despair are placed on either side.

〈The Dance of Life〉 is Munch's autobiographical work compressing memories of love and heartbreak into one canvas.
Light and dark colors alternate on a single canvas, capturing both the beginning and end of love.
id.translated:
Kontras simbolis warna terang dan gelap dalam 〈The Dance of Life〉 telah diterapkan.
Dengan wanita berbaju merah di tengah sebagai poros, kepolosan dan keputusasaan ditempatkan di kedua sisi.

〈The Dance of Life〉 adalah karya autobiografi Munch yang memampatkan kenangan cinta dan patah hati dalam satu kanvas.
Warna terang dan gelap bergantian dalam satu kanvas, menangkap awal dan akhir cinta.
id.back:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark colors in 〈The Dance of Life〉 has been applied.
With the woman in red in the center as the axis, innocence and despair are placed on either side.

〈The Dance of Life〉 is Munch's autobiographical work that compresses memories of love and heartbreak into one canvas.
Light and dark colors alternate in one canvas, capturing the beginning and end of love.

id.original:
The bold color plane division that bisects the face in 〈The Green Stripe〉 has been applied.
Pink, yellow, and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring natural color.

〈The Green Stripe〉 is a portrait Matisse painted of his wife Amélie, drawing cries of "Wild beasts!" at the 1905 Salon.
A single green stripe down the center of the face divides light and shadow through color alone.
id.translated:
Pembagian bidang warna tebal yang membelah wajah dalam 〈The Green Stripe〉 telah diterapkan.
Merah muda, kuning, dan hijau berdampingan di kulit, sama sekali mengabaikan warna alami.

〈The Green Stripe〉 adalah potret yang dilukis Matisse untuk istrinya Amélie, memicu seruan 'binatang buas!' di Salon 1905.
Satu garis hijau di tengah wajah membagi cahaya dan bayangan hanya melalui warna.
id.back:
The division of the thick color field that divides the face in 〈The Green Stripe〉 has been applied.
Pink, yellow, and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring the natural color.

〈The Green Stripe〉 is a portrait Matisse painted for his wife Amélie, sparking cries of 'beast!' at the 1905 Salon.
A single green stripe in the middle of the face divides light and shadow only through color.

id.original:
The intense color planes and decorative flat composition of 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 have been applied.
The boundary between figure and background dissolves, with color itself creating space.

〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 is a work where Matisse conveyed emotion through simplified forms and pure color.
The vast color plane of the purple coat dominates the canvas, merging with the background patterns as one.
id.translated:
Bidang warna intens dan komposisi datar dekoratif dari 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 telah diterapkan.
Batas antara figur dan latar belakang larut, dengan warna itu sendiri menciptakan ruang.

〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 adalah karya Matisse yang menyampaikan emosi melalui bentuk yang disederhanakan dan warna murni.
Bidang warna ungu yang luas mendominasi kanvas, menyatu dengan pola latar belakang menjadi satu.
id.back:
The intense color field and decorative flat composition of 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 have been applied.
The boundary between the figure and the background dissolves, with the color itself creating space.

〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 is a Matisse work that conveys emotions through simplified forms and pure colors.
A large field of purple color dominates the canvas, merging with the background pattern into one.

id.original:
The flat color plane composition that defies perspective in 〈The Red Room〉 has been applied.
The red wall and red table merge into one as arabesque patterns blanket the entire space.

〈The Red Room〉 was originally painted in blue, but Matisse repainted it red just before completion.
Even the view through the window appears as flat decoration, erasing the boundary between inside and out.
id.translated:
Komposisi bidang warna datar yang menentang perspektif dalam 〈The Red Room〉 telah diterapkan.
Dinding merah dan meja merah menyatu menjadi satu—pola arabesque menyelimuti seluruh ruang.

〈The Red Room〉 awalnya dilukis dalam biru, namun Matisse mengecat ulangnya dengan merah sesaat sebelum selesai.
Bahkan pemandangan melalui jendela tampak sebagai dekorasi datar—menghapus batas dalam dan luar.

Ini adalah bukti keberanian Matisse: bahwa warna bisa lebih kuat dari realitas.
id.back:
The perspective-defying composition of flat color planes in 〈The Red Room〉 has been applied.
The red wall and red table merge into one-the arabesque pattern envelops the entire space.

〈The Red Room〉 was originally painted in blue, but Matisse repainted it red shortly before completion.
Even the view through the window appears as a flat decoration-erasing the boundaries of inside and outside.

This is a testament to Matisse's courage: that color can be more powerful than reality.

id.original:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin colors of 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with bold primary colors, render the subject in a wild, Fauvist manner.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 is a portrait of friendship Matisse painted of his fellow artist André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing their Fauvist experiments together.
id.translated:
Sapuan kuas kasar dan warna kulit tidak alami dari 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 telah diterapkan.
Bayangan hijau dan kulit oranye, dengan warna primer tebal, merender subjek dengan cara liar ala Fauvis.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 adalah potret persahabatan yang dilukis Matisse untuk sesama seniman André Derain.
Kedua pelukis saling bertukar potret satu sama lain pada waktu yang sama, berbagi eksperimen Fauvisme bersama.
id.back:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin color from 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with thick primary colors, render the subject in a wild Fauvist way.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 is a friendly portrait that Matisse painted for fellow artist André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing the experiment of Fauvism together.

id.original:
The dreamlike colors and overlapping images of 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and violet blend together, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 is a work Chagall painted of his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife.
A vivid bouquet of red and white flowers glows against a deep green and navy background, expressing the warmth of love.
id.translated:
Warna seperti mimpi dan gambar yang saling tumpang tindih dari 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 telah diterapkan.
Merah muda, biru kobalt, dan violet berpadu, menghapus batas antara mimpi dan kenyataan.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 adalah karya Chagall yang dilukis tentang cintanya pada Bella, muse dan istrinya seumur hidup.
Buket bunga merah dan putih yang cerah bersinar di atas latar hijau tua dan biru laut, mengekspresikan kehangatan cinta.
id.back:
Dreamlike colors and overlapping images from 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and violet blend together, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 is Chagall's painted work about his love for Bella, his muse and wife for life.
A bright bouquet of red and white flowers shines on a dark green and navy blue background, expressing the warmth of love.

id.original:
The dreamlike contrast of red bouquets and blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and figures blend like apparitions, unfolding a festive fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and church spires of Chagall's hometown Vitebsk as a landscape of memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet, overlaid with the village scene, holds both nostalgia and joy.
id.translated:
Kontras seperti mimpi buket merah dan langit malam biru dalam 〈La Mariée〉 telah diterapkan.
Hewan dan figur berpadu seperti penampakan, menggelar fantasi meriah.

〈La Mariée〉 menangkap atap dan menara gereja kota asal Chagall, Vitebsk, sebagai lanskap memori.
Mempelai yang memegang buket merah, dilapisi adegan desa, menyimpan nostalgia sekaligus kegembiraan.
id.back:
The dreamlike contrast of the red bouquet and the blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and figures combine like apparitions, staging a lively fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and church spires of Chagall's home town Vitebsk as a memory landscape.
The bride holding a red bouquet, overlaid with a village scene, holds both nostalgia and joy.

id.original:
The multiple perspectives and transparently overlapping images of 〈I and the Village〉 have been applied.
People, animals, and village scenery intermingle dreamlike, defying scale and proportion.

〈I and the Village〉 was painted in Paris as Chagall longed for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, capturing an endless yearning for home.
id.translated:
Perspektif berganda dan gambar yang transparan saling tumpang tindih dari 〈I and the Village〉 telah diterapkan.
Orang, hewan, dan pemandangan desa bercampur seperti mimpi, menentang skala dan proporsi.

〈I and the Village〉 dilukis di Paris saat Chagall merindukan kampung halamannya Vitebsk.
Realitas dan memori menyatu di satu kanvas, menangkap kerinduan tak berujung akan rumah.
id.back:
The multiple perspectives and transparent overlapping images of 〈I and the Village〉 have been applied.
People, animals and village scenery are mixed together like a dream, defying scale and proportion.

〈I and the Village〉 was painted in Paris while Chagall was missing his hometown Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on one canvas, capturing the endless longing for home.

id.original:
The simultaneous front and side view of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 has been applied.
Intense primary color planes divide the face geometrically, creating Cubist tension.

〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 was painted by Picasso of his lover and fellow photographer Dora Maar.
The seated woman's front and side coexist in one face, revealing the complexity within.
id.translated:
Tampilan depan dan samping secara bersamaan dari 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 telah diterapkan.
Warna primer intens mengisi bidang-bidang geometris.

Dora Maar adalah fotografer dan seniman yang menjadi muse dan kekasih Picasso selama Perang Dunia II.
〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 menangkap wajahnya dari beberapa sudut sekaligus—ekspresi visual dari kerumitan psikologis seseorang.

Picasso kemudian berkata: 'Dora, untuk saya, selalu menangis.' Lukisan ini adalah monumen untuk kecerdasan dan penderitaannya.
id.back:
Simultaneous front and side views of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 have been applied.
Intense primary colors fill the geometric planes.

Dora Maar was a photographer and artist who became Picasso's muse and lover during World War II.
〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 captures her face from several angles at once-a visual expression of one's psychological complexity.

Picasso later said: 'Dora, for me, is always crying.' This painting is a monument to her intelligence and suffering.

id.original:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Vivid reds, greens, and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.
id.translated:
Tatapan frontal intens dan warna folk Meksiko dari 〈Me and My Parrots〉 telah diterapkan.
Merah, hijau, dan biru cerah memenuhi kanvas bersama dedaunan tropis.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 adalah potret diri di mana Frida dikelilingi empat ekor burung beo.
Burung beo adalah pembawa pesan cinta dalam cerita rakyat Meksiko, dan teman setia dalam kesendirian Frida.
id.back:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Bright reds, greens and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.

id.original:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 have been applied.
Amid dark green leaves, her intense eyes and connected eyebrows reveal her identity.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait Frida painted with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were surrogates for the children she could never have.
id.translated:
Tatapan frontal dan latar belakang tropis subur dari 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 telah diterapkan.
Di antara daun hijau gelap, matanya yang intens dan alis yang menyatu mengungkapkan identitasnya.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 adalah potret diri yang dilukis Frida bersama monyet peliharaannya.
Dalam mitologi Meksiko monyet melambangkan hasrat, namun bagi Frida mereka adalah pengganti anak yang tidak pernah bisa ia miliki.
id.back:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 have been applied.
Among the dark green leaves, her intense eyes and fused eyebrows reveal her identity.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait that Frida painted with her pet monkey.
In Mexican mythology monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were a substitute for the child she could never have.

id.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of 〈In the Car〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill the canvas like a comic book panel.

〈In the Car〉 was created by enlarging a panel from the comic Girls' Romances onto canvas.
Lichtenstein deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image instead of narrative.
id.translated:
Titik Ben-Day dan kontur hitam tebal dari 〈In the Car〉 telah diterapkan.
Hanya warna primer murni yang mengisi kontur tebal—seperti panel komik yang diperbesar.

〈In the Car〉 menangkap sepasang kekasih dalam mobil dengan gaya komik yang khas.
Ekspresi wajah datar dan jarak emosional antara dua figur menciptakan ironi dingin yang khas Lichtenstein.

Lukisan ini mempertanyakan: apakah kita benar-benar terhubung, atau hanya gambar dari koneksi?
id.back:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black contours from 〈In the Car〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors fill the thick contours-like an enlarged comic panel.

〈In the Car〉 captures a couple in a car in a typical comic style.
The flat facial expressions and emotional distance between the two figures create a cold irony that is typical of Lichtenstein.

This painting questions: are we really connected, or just images of connection?

id.original:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narrative of speech bubbles in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flatly within thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, which sold for $30,000—five times the going rate.
It is now displayed as a centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.
id.translated:
Titik Ben-Day dan narasi buku komik balon ucapan dalam 〈M-Maybe〉 telah diterapkan.
Warna primer mengisi datar dalam kontur tebal, merekonstruksi tekstur halaman cetak.

〈M-Maybe〉 adalah karya yang disimpan Lichtenstein dalam koleksi pribadinya, terjual $30.000—lima kali harga pasar.
Kini dipajang sebagai pusat koleksi Pop Art di Museum Ludwig di Cologne, Jerman.
id.back:
Ben-Day's dots and speech balloon comic book narration in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill the flat in the thick contours, reconstructing the texture of the printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work that Lichtenstein kept in his private collection, selling for $30,000-five times the market price.
It is now displayed as the centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at the Ludwig Museum in Cologne, Germany.

id.original:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterwork created just after Lichtenstein's first solo show in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: "Is He the Worst Artist in the U.S.?"—about him.
id.translated:
Titik Ben-Day dan kontras warna primer intens dari 〈Forget It!〉 telah diterapkan.
Kontur hitam tebal memfikasikan bentuk seperti panel komik, mengompres emosi secara dramatis.

〈Forget It!〉 adalah mahakarya awal yang dibuat sesaat setelah pameran solo pertama Lichtenstein pada 1962.
Tahun yang sama, majalah Life menulis judul: 'Apakah Dia Seniman Terburuk di AS?'—tentang dirinya.
id.back:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrast of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
The thick black contour classifies the shape like a comic panel, compressing the emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterpiece made shortly after Lichtenstein's first solo exhibition in 1962.
The same year, Life magazine wrote a headline: 'Is He the Worst Artist in the US?'-about him.

id.original:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow separate cleanly within black outlines, as crisp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work Lichtenstein borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, overturning the value of what he once called "used canvas."
id.translated:
Titik Ben-Day dan komposisi bidang warna datar dari 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 telah diterapkan.
Merah, biru, dan kuning terpisah bersih dalam kontur hitam, setajam halaman cetak.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 adalah karya 1964 yang dipinjam dari edisi #88 Secret Hearts.
Terjual $42,6 juta di Christie's pada 2010, membalik nilai apa yang pernah ia sebut 'kanvas bekas'.
id.back:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color field composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow are cleanly separated within the black contour, as sharp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, reversing the value of what he once called a 'used canvas'.

id.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in a comic-like manner, recreating the texture of print.

〈Still Life〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre as homage to Picasso.
"There's no mood in my still lifes—it's just lemons and grapefruits," he said.
id.translated:
Titik Ben-Day dan kontur tebal yang menciptakan ekspresi datar dalam 〈Still Life〉 telah diterapkan.
Bidang warna primer menyederhanakan objek dengan cara seperti komik, merekonstruksi tekstur cetakan.

〈Still Life〉 adalah bagian dari seri 1970-an di mana Lichtenstein menafsirkan ulang genre tradisional sebagai penghormatan kepada Picasso.
'Tidak ada suasana dalam still life saya—hanya lemon dan jeruk bali,' katanya.
id.back:
The Ben-Day dots and thick contours that created the flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
The primary color field simplifies the object in a comic-like manner, reconstructing the texture of the print.

〈Still Life〉 was part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted traditional genres as a tribute to Picasso.
'There is no atmosphere in my still life-just lemons and grapefruits,' he said.

id.original:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

Van Gogh left over 40 self-portraits, confronting his inner self most honestly through them.
The eyes gazing directly forward show unwavering will even amid suffering.
id.translated:
Gaya ekspresif Van Gogh dengan sapuan spiral dan warna intens telah diterapkan.
Sapuan kuas kuat dan terarah menciptakan tekstur bersemangat penuh energi emosional.

Kontras antara biru dalam dan kuning cerah mendefinisikan palet seniman yang melukis dengan hati.
Gaya tak salah lagi di mana setiap goresan mengungkap jiwa yang tersiksa dan penuh gairah.
id.back:
Van Gogh's expressive style with spiral strokes and intense colors has been applied.
The strong, directional brushstrokes create a vibrant texture full of emotional energy.

The contrast between deep blue and bright yellow defines the palette of an artist who paints with heart.
An unmistakable style where every stroke reveals a tortured and passionate soul.

id.original:
Klimt's real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on men's clothing contrast with curves and circles on women's, expressing both opposition and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decoration wrap the figure in a mystical golden world.
Within this decoration, sensuality and divinity coexist.
id.translated:
Gaya dekoratif Klimt dengan lembaran emas dan pola geometris telah diterapkan.
Mosaik emas dan spiral ornamental menciptakan dunia kemewahan sensual.

Figur datar terbungkus pola di mana yang organik dan geometris berkelindan.
Estetika Sezession Wina, di mana dekorasi menjadi ekspresi jiwa.
id.back:
Klimt's decorative style with gold sheets and geometric patterns has been applied.
Gold mosaics and ornamental spirals create a world of sensual luxury.

Flat figures are wrapped in patterns where the organic and geometric intertwine.
The aesthetic of the Vienna Sezession, where decoration becomes an expression of the soul.

id.original:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors have been applied.
Blood-red skies and wavering landscapes visualize inner emotions.

Munch wrote in his diary that while walking by the Oslo fjord, he "heard an enormous scream pass through nature."
Before his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.
id.translated:
Gaya ekspresionis Munch dengan warna mengganggu dan bentuk bergelombang telah diterapkan.
Garis sinuosa dan nada pucat menyampaikan kecemasan psikologis secara langsung.

Lanskap yang terdistorsi mengikuti ritme emosi internal, di mana tidak ada yang stabil.
Bahasa visual kecemasan, pelopor dalam mengubah isi jiwa menjadi gambar.
id.back:
Munch's expressionist style with disturbing colors and undulating shapes has been applied.
Sinuose lines and pale tones convey psychological anxiety directly.

The distorted landscape follows the rhythm of internal emotions, where nothing is stable.
The visual language of anxiety, a pioneer in transforming the contents of the soul into images.

id.original:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors have been applied.
Rather than anatomical accuracy, focus is on the energy of movement, with color directly conveying emotion.

Just as Matisse erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room, he the boundary between table and wall, he chose harmony of color planes over perspective.
Before his canvas, colors sing out.
id.translated:
Gaya fauvis Matisse dengan warna murni dan bentuk sederhana telah diterapkan.
Warna primer berani disusun tanpa menghormati kenyataan, dan bentuk direduksi ke esensi.

Garis menjadi tarian dan warna menjadi musik — setiap elemen bernyanyi dengan kemandiriannya.
Seni kegembiraan murni, di mana warna bebas dari segala kewajiban deskriptif.
id.back:
Matisse's fauvist style with pure colors and simple shapes has been applied.
Bold primary colors are composed without respect for reality, and shapes are reduced to the essence.

Lines become dance and colors become music - each element sings with its independence.
An art of pure joy, where color is free from any descriptive obligations.

id.original:
Chagall's dreamlike atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel-like colors has been applied.
Bouquets, lovers, and hometown scenes blend together like memories, lifting the weight of reality.

Chagall composed his canvases with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space halt before love, and the world fills with dreamlike light.
id.translated:
Gaya mimpi Chagall dengan warna pastel dan figur melayang telah diterapkan.
Kekasih, hewan, dan atap melayang dalam ruang tanpa gravitasi, diwarnai nostalgia.

Merah muda, ungu, dan biru melebur bagaikan kenangan yang berbaur dalam mimpi.
Dunia di mana cinta dan memori menantang hukum fisika dan waktu.
id.back:
Chagall's dreamy style with pastel colors and floating figures has been applied.
Lovers, animals and rooftops float in a space without gravity, tinged with nostalgia.

Pinks, purples and blues meld like memories mingling in a dream.
A world where love and memory defy the laws of physics and time.

id.original:
Picasso's Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Like Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple viewpoints coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, Picasso conveyed the terror of war immediately. This destructive deconstruction was itself creation.
Five centuries of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.
id.translated:
Gaya kubis Picasso dengan bentuk geometris dan perspektif ganda telah diterapkan.
Wajah dan tubuh terfragmentasi menjadi bidang bersudut, menampilkan beberapa sudut pandang secara bersamaan.

Garis berani dan warna kontras membangun kenyataan yang didekonstruksi dan dirangkai ulang.
Revolusi visual yang menghancurkan 500 tahun perspektif tradisional.
id.back:
Picasso's cubist style with geometric shapes and multiple perspectives has been applied.
The face and body are fragmented into angular planes, showing multiple viewpoints simultaneously.

Bold lines and contrasting colors build a deconstructed and reassembled reality.
A visual revolution that shatters 500 years of traditional perspective.

id.original:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and communion with animals have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird, thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys as family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not mere backgrounds but companions offering comfort amid suffering.
id.translated:
Gaya simbolis Frida dengan warna Meksiko dan tatapan frontal intens telah diterapkan.
Merah, hijau, dan kuning tradisi Meksiko membingkai ikonografi yang sangat personal.

Setiap elemen — duri, hewan, tanaman — adalah simbol berkode dari rasa sakit, perlawanan, dan cinta.
Seni pengakuan di mana tubuh yang patah menjadi wilayah identitas dan perjuangan.
id.back:
Frida's symbolic style with Mexican colors and intense frontal gaze has been applied.
The reds, greens and yellows of Mexican tradition frame the highly personalized iconography.

Each element - thorn, animal, plant - is a coded symbol of pain, resistance and love.
Confessional art where the broken body becomes a territory of identity and struggle.

id.original:
Lichtenstein's Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines, and primary colors have been applied.
Like the glamorous couple In the Car, every image becomes a comic book panel.

Lichtenstein's dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of comic heroines, everyday moments transform into dramatic scenes.
Emotion exploding within mechanical dots and lines—that is Lichtenstein's paradox.
id.translated:
Gaya Pop Art Lichtenstein dengan titik Ben-Day dan kontur tebal telah diterapkan.
Pola titik teratur dan warna primer datar mengubah estetika komik menjadi seni.

Hitam, merah, biru, dan kuning dalam komposisi grafis yang mempertanyakan apa itu seni dan apa itu budaya populer.
Esensi Pop Art yang menghapus batas antara museum dan budaya massa.
id.back:
Lichtenstein's Pop Art style with Ben-Day dots and thick contours has been applied.
The regular dot patterns and flat primary colors transform the comic aesthetic into art.

Black, red, blue and yellow in a graphic composition that questions what is art and what is popular culture.
The essence of Pop Art that erases the boundaries between museums and mass culture.

id.back: Gold fields dance in the wind

The Dance · The Red Room · The Green Stripe

Lovers with Flowers · I and the Village · La Mariée
Love glowing warmly within a bouquet

id.back: In the Car - M-Maybe - Forget It!

id.back: One word of doubt, Pop Art drama

id.translated: Jeritan perpisahan dalam satu panel komik
id.back: Farewell screams in one comic panel
