The flat color composition of the Red Room, which ignores perspective, was applied.
The red wall and the red table merge; arabesque patterns cover the entire space.

Matisse initially painted the Red Room blue, but changed it to red before finishing it.
Even the view from the window looks like a flat decoration; the boundary between inside and outside is erased.

tr.original:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin colors of 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with bold primary colors, render the subject in a wild, Fauvist manner.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 is a portrait of friendship Matisse painted of his fellow artist André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing their Fauvist experiments together.
tr.translated:
André Derain Portresi'nin kaba fırça darbeleri ve gerçek dışı ten renkleri uygulandı.
Yeşil gölgeler, turuncu ten ve yoğun ana renkler figürü "vahşi" bir biçimde betimliyor.

André Derain Portresi, Matisse'in ressam arkadaşı André Derain için yaptığı dostluk portresidir.
İki ressam aynı dönemde birbirlerinin portrelerini yaparak Fovizm deneyimlerini paylaştı.
tr.back:
Portrait of André Derain's rough brushstrokes and unrealistic skin colors were applied.
Green shadows, orange skin and intense primary colors depict the figure in a "wild" way.

Portrait of André Derain is a friendship portrait that Matisse painted for his painter friend André Derain.
The two painters shared their experiences of Fauvism by painting each other's portraits in the same period.

tr.original:
The dreamlike colors and overlapping images of 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and violet blend together, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 is a work Chagall painted of his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife.
A vivid bouquet of red and white flowers glows against a deep green and navy background, expressing the warmth of love.
tr.translated:
Doğum Günü'nün rüya gibi renkleri ve rüya gibi üst üste binen görüntüleri uygulandı.
Pembe, kobalt mavisi ve mor kaynaşıyor; rüya ile gerçek arasındaki sınır siliniyor.

Doğum Günü, Chagall'ın ömür boyu ilham perisi ve eşi Bella'ya olan aşkını tuvale döktüğü eserdir.
Gerçek arka plan üzerine rüya gibi üst üste binen âşıklar, aşkın sarhoşluğunu ifade ediyor.
tr.back:
The dreamy colors and dreamy overlapping images of Birthday are applied.
Pink, cobalt blue and purple fuse, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

Birthday is Chagall's canvas expression of his love for his lifelong muse and wife Bella.
The lovers overlapping like a dream on a real background expresses the intoxication of love.

tr.original:
The dreamlike contrast of red bouquets and blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and figures blend like apparitions, unfolding a festive fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and church spires of Chagall's hometown Vitebsk as a landscape of memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet, overlaid with the village scene, holds both nostalgia and joy.
tr.translated:
Gelin'in kırmızı buket ile mavi gece gökyüzü arasındaki rüya gibi kontrastı uygulandı.
Hayvanlar ve figürler hayaletler gibi kaynaşıyor; şenlikli bir hayal açılıyor.

Gelin, Chagall'ın memleketi Vitebsk'in çatılarını ve çan kulelerini bir anı manzarası olarak yakalıyor.
Kırmızı buketli gelin ile üst üste binen köy manzarası aynı anda özlem ve neşe barındırıyor.
tr.back:
The dreamy contrast between the Bride's red bouquet and the blue night sky is applied.
Animals and figures mingle like ghosts; a festive dream opens up.

The bride captures the rooftops and bell towers of Chagall's hometown of Vitebsk as a landscape of memories.
The bride with the red bouquet and the overlapping village landscape are at once wistful and joyful.

tr.original:
The multiple perspectives and transparently overlapping images of 〈I and the Village〉 have been applied.
People, animals, and village scenery intermingle dreamlike, defying scale and proportion.

〈I and the Village〉 was painted in Paris as Chagall longed for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, capturing an endless yearning for home.
tr.translated:
Ben ve Köy'ün çoklu perspektifleri ve şeffaf üst üste binen görüntüleri uygulandı.
İnsanlar, hayvanlar ve köy manzarası bir rüyadaki gibi oran ve ölçek tanımadan karışıyor.

Ben ve Köy, Chagall'ın Paris'te memleketi Vitebsk'i özleyerek yaptığı eserdir.
Gerçek ve anı tek bir tuvalde kaynaşıyor; vatana sonsuz bir özlemi taşıyor.
tr.back:
Multiple perspectives and transparent overlapping images of Me and the Village were applied.
People, animals and the village landscape blend like in a dream, without proportion or scale.

Me and the Village is a work Chagall painted in Paris, longing for his hometown Vitebsk.
Reality and memory fuse on a single canvas; it carries an endless longing for the homeland.

tr.original:
The simultaneous front and side view of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 has been applied.
Intense primary color planes divide the face geometrically, creating Cubist tension.

〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 was painted by Picasso of his lover and fellow photographer Dora Maar.
The seated woman's front and side coexist in one face, revealing the complexity within.
tr.translated:
Dora Maar Portresi'nin cephe ve profili aynı anda gösterimi uygulandı.
Yoğun ana renk yüzeyleri yüzü geometrik olarak bölüyor; kübist bir gerilim yaratıyor.

Dora Maar Portresi, Picasso'nun sevgilisi ve fotoğrafçı Dora Maar için yaptığı portredir.
Oturan kadının cephesi ve profili tek bir yüzde bir arada yaşıyor; iç dünyanın karmaşıklığını açığa çıkarıyor.
tr.back:
Portrait of Dora Maar's façade and profile were rendered simultaneously.
Intense primary color surfaces geometrically divide the face, creating a cubist tension.

Portrait of Dora Maar is Picasso's portrait of his lover and photographer Dora Maar.
The façade and profile of the seated woman coexist in a single face, revealing the complexity of the inner world.

tr.original:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Vivid reds, greens, and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.
tr.translated:
Ben ve Papağanlarım'ın yoğun doğrudan bakışı ve Meksika halk renkleri uygulandı.
Canlı kırmızı, yeşil ve maviler tropikal bitkilerle birlikte tuvali dolduruyor.

Ben ve Papağanlarım, Frida'nın dört papağanla çevrili bir otoportresidir.
Papağanlar Meksika folklorunda aşkın elçileridir ve Frida'nın yalnızlığında sadık yoldaşlarıdır.
tr.back:
The intense direct gaze of Me and My Parrots and Mexican folk colors are applied.
Vibrant reds, greens and blues fill the canvas along with tropical plants.

Me and My Parrots is a self-portrait of Frida surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are the messengers of love in Mexican folklore and Frida's faithful companions in her loneliness.

tr.original:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 have been applied.
Amid dark green leaves, her intense eyes and connected eyebrows reveal her identity.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait Frida painted with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were surrogates for the children she could never have.
tr.translated:
Maymunlu Otoportre'nin doğrudan bakışı ve yemyeşil tropikal arka planı uygulandı.
Koyu yeşil yapraklar arasında keskin bakışı ve birleşik kaşları kimliğini ortaya koyuyor.

Maymunlu Otoportre, Frida'nın evcil maymunlarıyla birlikte yaptığı otoportredir.
Meksika mitolojisinde maymunlar arzuyu simgeler, ama Frida için sahip olamadığı çocukların yerini tutan varlıklardı.
tr.back:
Self-Portrait with Monkey's direct gaze and lush tropical background are applied.
Among the dark green leaves, his sharp gaze and united eyebrows reveal his identity.

Self-Portrait with Monkeys is a self-portrait of Frida with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology, monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were a substitute for the children she could not have.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of 〈In the Car〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill the canvas like a comic book panel.

〈In the Car〉 was created by enlarging a panel from the comic Girls' Romances onto canvas.
Lichtenstein deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image instead of narrative.
tr.translated:
Arabada'nın Ben-Day noktaları ve kalın siyah kontur çizgileri uygulandı.
Yalnızca saf ana renkler — kırmızı, mavi ve sarı — tuvali bir çizgi roman karesi gibi dolduruyor.

Arabada, Lichtenstein'ın Girls' Romances çizgi roman dergisinden bir kareyi tuvale büyüttüğü eserdir.
Orijinaldeki konuşma balonunu bilerek çıkardı; yalnızca görüntünün gücünü bıraktı.
tr.back:
In the car's Ben-Day dots and thick black contour lines were applied.
Only pure primary colors - red, blue and yellow - fill the canvas like a comic book frame.

In the Car is Lichtenstein's enlargement on canvas of a frame from the comic strip Girls' Romances.
He deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narrative of speech bubbles in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flatly within thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, which sold for $30,000—five times the going rate.
It is now displayed as a centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.
tr.translated:
Belki'nin Ben-Day noktaları ve çizgi roman konuşma balonu anlatısı uygulandı.
Ana renkler kalın kontur çizgileri içinde düz boyandı; baskı sayfası dokusu yeniden yaratıldı.

Belki, Lichtenstein'ın özel koleksiyonunda tuttuğu ve 30.000 dolara — piyasa fiyatının beş katına — sattığı eserdir.
Bugün Almanya Köln'deki Ludwig Müzesi'nin Pop Art koleksiyonunun simge eseri olarak sergileniyor.
tr.back:
Maybe's Ben-Day dots and the comic book speech bubble narrative were applied.
Primary colors were painted flat within thick contour lines; the texture of the printed page was recreated.

Perhaps it is the work Lichtenstein kept in his private collection and sold for $30,000 - five times the market price.
Today it is on display at the Ludwig Museum in Cologne, Germany, as a landmark in the Pop Art collection.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterwork created just after Lichtenstein's first solo show in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: "Is He the Worst Artist in the U.S.?"—about him.
tr.translated:
Unut Gitsin!'in Ben-Day noktaları ve yoğun ana renk kontrastları uygulandı.
Kalın siyah kontur çizgileri formu çizgi roman karesi gibi sabitledi; duyguyu dramatik biçimde sıkıştırdı.

Unut Gitsin!, 1962'de Lichtenstein'ın ilk kişisel sergisinden sonra ürettiği erken dönem eseridir.
Aynı yıl Life dergisi "Amerika'nın en kötü sanatçısı mı?" diye manşet attı — ondan bahsediyordu.
tr.back:
Forget It!'s Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts were applied.
Thick black contour lines fix the form like a comic book frame, dramatically compressing the emotion.

Forget It! is Lichtenstein's early work, produced after his first solo exhibition in 1962.
In the same year, Life magazine headlined "America's worst artist?" - referring to him.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow separate cleanly within black outlines, as crisp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work Lichtenstein borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, overturning the value of what he once called "used canvas."
tr.translated:
Ohh... Tamam...'ın Ben-Day noktaları ve düz renk kompozisyonu uygulandı.
Kırmızı, mavi ve sarı siyah kontur çizgileri içinde net bir şekilde ayrılıyor; basılı sayfa kadar keskin.

Ohh... Tamam... 1964'te Secret Hearts dergisinin 88. sayısından alınmış bir eserdir.
2010'da Christie's'te 42,6 milyon dolara satılarak onun "kullanılmış tuval" dediği şeyin değerini altüst etti.
tr.back:
Ohh... Okay...'s Ben-Day dots and solid color composition are applied.
Red, blue and yellow are clearly separated within black contour lines, as sharp as the printed page.

Ohh... Okay... It's from the 88th issue of Secret Hearts magazine in 1964.
In 2010, it sold at Christie's for $42.6 million, undercutting the value of what he called "used canvas."

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in a comic-like manner, recreating the texture of print.

〈Still Life〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre as homage to Picasso.
"There's no mood in my still lifes—it's just lemons and grapefruits," he said.
tr.translated:
Natürmort'un Ben-Day noktaları ve kalın kontur çizgilerinin düz ifadesi uygulandı.
Ana renk yüzeyleri nesneleri çizgi roman tarzında sadeleştiriyor; baskı dokusu yeniden yaratılıyor.

Natürmort, 1970'lerde Lichtenstein'ın Picasso'ya saygı olarak geleneksel konuyu yeniden yorumladığı serinin bir parçasıdır.
"Natürmortlarımda atmosfer yok — sadece limon ve greyfurt var" dedi.
tr.back:
Still Life's Ben-Day dots and the flat expression of thick contour lines are applied.
Primary color surfaces simplify objects in a comic book style; the print texture is recreated.

Still Life is part of a series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted the traditional subject matter in homage to Picasso in the 1970s.
"There is no atmosphere in my still lifes - just lemon and grapefruit," he said.

tr.original:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

Van Gogh left over 40 self-portraits, confronting his inner self most honestly through them.
The eyes gazing directly forward show unwavering will even amid suffering.
tr.translated:
Van Gogh'un kalın impasto tekniği ve dönen fırça darbeleri uygulandı.
Sarı ile mavi arasındaki yoğun kontrast iç dünyanın duygularını açığa çıkarıyor.

40'tan fazla otoportre bıraktı; iç dünyasıyla en büyük dürüstlükle yüzleşti.
Doğrudan bakış, acı içinde bile sarsılmayan bir iradeyi gösteriyor.
tr.back:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes were applied.
The intense contrast between yellow and blue reveals the emotions of the inner world.

He left more than 40 self-portraits; he faced his inner world with the greatest honesty.
The direct gaze shows a will that remains unshaken even in pain.

tr.original:
Klimt's real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on men's clothing contrast with curves and circles on women's, expressing both opposition and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decoration wrap the figure in a mystical golden world.
Within this decoration, sensuality and divinity coexist.
tr.translated:
Gerçek altın varak ve Bizans mozaiklerinden ilham alan geometrik desenler uygulandı.
Erkek giysisindeki dikdörtgenler kadın giysisindeki eğriler ve dairelerle kontrast ve uyum yaratıyor.

Sarmal eğriler ve zengin süsleme figürü gizemli bir altın dünyada sarıyor.
Bu dekorasyonda şehvani ve kutsal bir arada yaşıyor.
tr.back:
Real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The rectangles in menswear contrast and harmonize with the curves and circles in womenswear.

Spiral curves and rich ornamentation envelop the figure in a mysterious golden world.
The sensual and the sacred coexist in this decoration.

tr.original:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors have been applied.
Blood-red skies and wavering landscapes visualize inner emotions.

Munch wrote in his diary that while walking by the Oslo fjord, he "heard an enormous scream pass through nature."
Before his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.
tr.translated:
Dönen eğriler, çarpık biçimler ve yoğun renkler uygulandı.
Kan kırmızısı gökyüzü ve sallanan manzaralar iç duyguları görünür kılıyor.

Günlüğünde Oslo fiyordunda yürürken "doğayı delip geçen devasa bir çığlık duyduğunu" yazdı.
Tablolarının önünde renkler duyguların titreşimine dönüşüyor.
tr.back:
Twisting curves, distorted forms and intense colors are applied.
Blood-red skies and swaying landscapes make inner feelings visible.

In his diary he wrote that while walking in the Oslo fjord he "heard a gigantic scream piercing the nature".
In front of his paintings, colors turn into vibrations of emotions.

tr.original:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors have been applied.
Rather than anatomical accuracy, focus is on the energy of movement, with color directly conveying emotion.

Just as Matisse erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room, he the boundary between table and wall, he chose harmony of color planes over perspective.
Before his canvas, colors sing out.
tr.translated:
Basitleştirilmiş formlar ve cesur ana renkler uygulandı.
Anatomik doğruluğun peşinden gitmek yerine hareketin enerjisine odaklanıyor; renk duyguyu doğrudan aktarıyor.

Kırmızı Oda'da masa ile duvar arasındaki sınırı sildiği gibi, perspektif yerine renk yüzeylerinin uyumunu seçti.
Tuvalinin önünde renkler bir şarkı gibi yankılanıyor.
tr.back:
Simplified forms and bold primary colors are applied.
Instead of pursuing anatomical accuracy, he focuses on the energy of movement; color conveys emotion directly.

In the Red Room, he erased the boundary between the table and the wall, choosing the harmony of color surfaces instead of perspective.
Colors echo like a song in front of his canvas.

tr.original:
Chagall's dreamlike atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel-like colors has been applied.
Bouquets, lovers, and hometown scenes blend together like memories, lifting the weight of reality.

Chagall composed his canvases with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space halt before love, and the world fills with dreamlike light.
tr.translated:
Pembe, kobalt mavisi ve mücevher renklerinin rüya gibi atmosferi uygulandı.
Buketler, âşıklar ve memleket manzaraları anılar gibi kaynaşıyor; gerçekliğin ağırlığından arınıyor.

Kompozisyonlarını perspektif ya da anatomi yerine duygunun mantığıyla kurdu.
Tablolarında zaman ve mekân aşkın karşısında duruyor; dünya rüya gibi bir ışıkla doluyor.
tr.back:
The dreamy atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue and jewel colors is applied.
Bouquets, lovers and landscapes of the country fuse like memories, free from the weight of reality.

He built his compositions with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space stand against love; the world is filled with a dreamlike light.

tr.original:
Picasso's Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Like Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple viewpoints coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, Picasso conveyed the terror of war immediately. This destructive deconstruction was itself creation.
Five centuries of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.
tr.translated:
Nesneleri geometrik düzlemlere ayıran ve cephe ile profili aynı anda gösteren Kübist teknikler uygulandı.
Dora Maar Portresi'ndeki gibi çoklu bakış açıları tek bir yüzde bir arada yaşıyor.

Guernica'da yalnızca siyah beyazla savaşın dehşetini doğrudan aktardı; bu yıkıcı yapıbozum kendi başına yaratımdı.
Beş yüz yıllık perspektif kuralları onun tuvalinde paramparça oldu.
tr.back:
Cubist techniques were applied, separating objects into geometric planes and showing facade and profile simultaneously.
As in Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple perspectives coexist in a single face.

In Guernica he directly conveyed the horrors of war in black and white alone; this devastating deconstruction was a creation in itself.
Five hundred years of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.

tr.original:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and communion with animals have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird, thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys as family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not mere backgrounds but companions offering comfort amid suffering.
tr.translated:
Meksika geleneksel renkleri, tropikal yapraklar ve hayvanlarla uyum uygulandı.
Dikenli Kolye ve Sinek Kuşu Otoportresi'nde dikenler acıyı, ölü sinek kuşu yitik aşkı simgeler.

Çocuk sahibi olamayan Frida, papağanlarına ve maymunlarına ailesi gibi davrandı.
Otoportrelerinde hayvanlar sıradan birer arka plan değil, acı içinde teselli sunan yoldaşlardır.
tr.back:
Mexican traditional colors are harmonized with tropical leaves and animals.
In Necklace of Thorns and Self-Portrait of a Hummingbird, the thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird symbolizes lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys like family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not just backgrounds, but companions who offer solace in pain.

tr.original:
Lichtenstein's Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines, and primary colors have been applied.
Like the glamorous couple In the Car, every image becomes a comic book panel.

Lichtenstein's dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of comic heroines, everyday moments transform into dramatic scenes.
Emotion exploding within mechanical dots and lines—that is Lichtenstein's paradox.
tr.translated:
Baskı çizgi romanlarının Ben-Day noktaları, kalın siyah kontur çizgileri ve ana renklerle Pop Art teknikleri uygulandı.
Arabada'daki çekici çift gibi her görüntü bir çizgi roman karesine dönüşüyor.

Çizgi roman kadın kahramanlarının dramatik ifadeleri ve konuşma balonları gibi günlük anlar dramatik sahnelere dönüşüyor.
Mekanik noktalar ve çizgiler içinde patlayan duygular — Lichtenstein'ın paradoksu budur.
tr.back:
Pop Art techniques are applied with the Ben-Day dots of print comics, thick black contour lines and primary colors.
Like the attractive couple in In the Car, every image turns into a comic book frame.

Everyday moments become dramatic scenes, like the dramatic expressions of comic book heroines and speech bubbles.
Emotions exploding in mechanical dots and lines - this is Lichtenstein's paradox.

tr.original: The night sky painted in swirls
tr.back: A night sky painted with swirls

tr.translated: Gece Kafe Terası (Café Terrace at Night)

tr.original: A warm glow of yellow under starlight
tr.translated: Yıldızlar altında parlayan altın bir gece
tr.back: A golden night shining under the stars

tr.original: Gazing into the mirror of the soul
tr.back: Gazing into the mirror of the soul

tr.translated: Selvili Buğday Tarlası (Wheat Field with Cypresses)

tr.original: Golden fields dancing in the wind
tr.back: Golden lands fluttering in the wind
Vienna's golden dream at the end of the century

tr.original: The Kiss · Judith · The Tree of Life
tr.back: The Kiss - Judith - Tree of Life

tr.original: An eternal kiss dissolved in gold
tr.back: An eternal kiss melting beneath you

tr.original: Golden branches singing the song of life
tr.back: Golden branches sing the song of life

tr.original: Between the sacred and sensual, golden allure
tr.translated: Kutsal ile şehvani arasında altın bir baştan çıkarma
tr.back: A golden seduction between the sacred and the carnal

tr.original: An artist of the soul, born from loss
The Scream · Madonna · The Dance of Life
A soul's cry beneath a blood-red sky
tr.back: The scream of the soul under the bloody sky

tr.original: A mysterious figure between life and death
tr.translated: Yaşam ile ölüm arasında gizemli bir figür
tr.back: A mysterious figure between life and death

tr.original: A waltz of love and loss
tr.back: A waltz of love and loss
Painter singing a song of joy with colors

tr.original: The Dance · The Red Room · The Green Stripe
A single green line that declared a color revolution
tr.translated: Renk devrimini ilan eden tek bir yeşil çizgi
tr.back: A single green line proclaiming the color revolution

tr.translated: Mor Paltolu Kadın (Woman in a Purple Coat)

tr.original: A feast of decoration ruled by red
tr.back: A decorative feast dominated by red

tr.translated: André Derain Portresi (Portrait of André Derain)

tr.original: A Fauvist colleague painted in the colors of the wild
tr.translated: Vahşi renklerle resmedilmiş bir fovist yoldaş
tr.back: A Fauvist comrade painted in wild colors

tr.original: A painter of 100 years of love
tr.back: Painter of a hundred years of love

tr.original: Lovers with Flowers · I and the Village · La Mariée
tr.translated: Çiçekli Âşıklar · Ben ve Köy · Gelin
tr.back: Flowery Minstrels - Me and the Village - Bride

tr.original: Love glowing warmly within a bouquet
tr.translated: Bir buket içinde sıcacık parlayan aşk
tr.back: Love shining warmly in a bouquet

tr.original: A bride floating between dream and reality
tr.translated: Rüya ile gerçek arasında süzülen bir gelin
tr.back: A bride floating between dream and reality

tr.translated: Ben ve Köy (I and the Village)

tr.original: Memories of home drifting like a dream
tr.translated: Vatanın anıları bir rüya gibi akıyor
tr.back: Memories of the homeland flow like a dream

tr.original: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon · Guernica · Portrait of Dora Maar
tr.translated: Avignonlu Kızlar · Guernica · Dora Maar Portresi
tr.back: Girls from Avignon - Guernica - Portrait of Dora Maar
The revolutionary who deconstructs the point of view

tr.translated: Dora Maar Portresi (Portrait of Dora Maar)

tr.original: A deconstructed portrait where front meets side
tr.translated: Yüz ve profil tek bir yüzde bir arada
tr.back: Face and profile together in one face

tr.original: Me and My Parrots · The Broken Column · Self-Portrait with Monkeys
tr.translated: Ben ve Papağanlarım · Kırık Sütun · Maymunlu Otoportre
tr.back: Me and My Parrots - Broken Column - Self-Portrait with Monkey

tr.translated: Ben ve Papağanlarım (Me and My Parrots)

tr.original: A self-portrait in solitude, with parrots
tr.back: A self-portrait with parrots in solitude

tr.original: A portrait of pain, embraced by monkeys
tr.back: A portrait of pain in the embrace of monkeys

tr.original: Revolution that started with Mickey Mouse
tr.back: The revolution that started with Mickey Mouse

tr.original: In the Car · M-Maybe · Forget It!
tr.translated: In the Car · M-Maybe · Forget It!
tr.back: In the Car - M-Maybe - Forget It!
The man who turned comics into art

tr.original: A single word of hesitation, a Pop Art drama
tr.translated: Bir tereddüt kelimesi Pop Art dramasına dönüşüyor
tr.back: A word of hesitation turns into a Pop Art drama

tr.original: A farewell cry captured in a comic panel
tr.translated: Tek bir çizgi roman karesinde yakalanan bir veda çığlığı
tr.back: A farewell cry captured in a single comic book frame

tr.original: Resignation and acceptance in one breath
tr.translated: Boyun eğiş ve kabulün iç çekişi
Kristal Kâseli Natürmort (Still Life with Crystal Bowl)

tr.original: Still life reborn through Ben-Day dots
tr.translated: Ben-Day noktalarıyla yeniden doğan natürmort dünyası
tr.back: Still life world reborn with Ben-Day dots

tr.original:
Van Gogh is a defining figure of Post-Impressionism.
He began painting at 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, yet only one sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces like The Starry Night, Sunflowers, and Café Terrace at Night were all born in poverty and mental anguish.

Van Gogh died at 37, but his art profoundly influenced Expressionism and Fauvism.
"I put my heart and soul into my work." — A painter who forged immortal beauty from suffering.
tr.translated:
Van Gogh, Post-Empresyonizm'in temsilcisi olan ressamdır.
27 yaşında resim yapmaya başladı ve yalnızca on yılda 900 yağlıboya üretti, ama hayattayken yalnızca bir tane sattı.
Yıldızlı Gece, Ayçiçekleri ve Gece Kafe Terası gibi başyapıtları yoksulluk ve ruhsal sıkıntı içinde doğdu.

Van Gogh 37 yaşında hayata veda etti, ama sanatı Ekspresyonizm ve Fovizm'i derinden etkiledi.
"Kalbimi ve ruhumu işime koyuyorum." — Acıdan ölümsüz güzellik dokuyan ressam.
tr.back:
Van Gogh was a painter who represented Post-Impressionism.
He started painting at the age of 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, but sold only one in his lifetime.
His masterpieces such as Starry Night, Sunflowers and Night Café Terrace were born in poverty and mental distress.

Van Gogh died at the age of 37, but his art profoundly influenced Expressionism and Fauvism.
"I put my heart and soul into my work." - The painter who wove immortal beauty from pain.

tr.original:
Klimt was the leading Art Nouveau painter of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
Born the son of a goldsmith, he applied real gold leaf to canvas, creating an opulent decorative world inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
He left behind golden masterpieces where sensuality and divinity coexist, including The Kiss, Judith, and The Tree of Life.

Klimt founded the Vienna Secession, challenging the conservative art establishment.
"To every age its art, to every art its freedom." — A painter who captured fin-de-siècle beauty in gold.
tr.translated:
Klimt, yüzyıl sonu Viyana'sında Art Nouveau'yu temsil eden sanatçıdır.
Bir oymacının oğlu olarak tuval üzerine gerçek altın varak yapıştırdı, Bizans mozaiklerinden ilham alarak göz kamaştırıcı bir dekoratif dünya yarattı.
Öpücük, Judith ve Hayat Ağacı, şehvanilik ile kutsallığın bir arada yaşadığı altın başyapıtlardır.

Klimt, Viyana Secession'unu kurarak muhafazakâr sanat dünyasına meydan okudu.
"Her çağın kendi sanatı, sanatın kendi özgürlüğü vardır." — Yüzyıl sonu güzelliğini altınla yakalayan ressam.
tr.back:
Klimt was the artist who represented Art Nouveau in turn-of-the-century Vienna.
As the son of an engraver, he applied real gold leaf on canvas, inspired by Byzantine mosaics to create a dazzling decorative world.
The Kiss, Judith and The Tree of Life are golden masterpieces in which sensuality and sacredness coexist.

Klimt challenged the conservative art world by founding the Vienna Secession.
"Every age has its own art, and art has its own freedom." - The painter who captured the beauty of the end of the century in gold.

tr.original:
Munch is the defining Expressionist painter from Norway.
He lost his mother at five and his sister at fourteen, and the memory of these losses dominated his canvases for life.
His works, including The Scream, Madonna, and The Dance of Life, form "The Frieze of Life" — a cycle of love, death, and anxiety.

Munch declared, "I paint not what I see, but what I saw,", exploring the deepest human emotions.
"Illness, madness, and death were the dark angels that watched over my cradle." — A painter who transformed inner darkness into art.
tr.translated:
Munch, Ekspresyonizm'i temsil eden büyük Norveçli ressamdır.
Beş yaşında annesini, on dört yaşında ablasını kaybetti; kayıp anısı ömür boyu tuvallerine hâkim oldu.
Çığlık, Madonna ve Hayatın Dansı, aşkı, ölümü ve kaygıyı keşfeden "Hayat Frizi" serisinin eserleridir.

Munch, "Gördüğümü değil, görmüş olduğumu resmederim" diyerek insanın en ilkel duygularını keşfetti.
"Hastalık, delilik ve ölüm beşiğimi bekleyen kara meleklerdi." — İçindeki karanlığı sanata yücelten ressam.
tr.back:
Munch was the great Norwegian painter who represented Expressionism.
He lost his mother at the age of five and his sister at fourteen; the memory of loss dominated his canvases throughout his life.
The Scream, Madonna and The Dance of Life are works from the "Life Frieze" series, exploring love, death and anxiety.

Munch discovered the most primitive human emotions by saying "I paint what I see, not what I see".
"Sickness, madness and death were the dark angels waiting for my cradle." - The painter who sublimated his inner darkness into art.

tr.original:
Matisse is the leading Fauvist painter of France.
At 21, he began painting with a box of colors his mother gave him while bedridden, later recalling it felt like "discovering paradise."
The 1905 cry of "Wild beasts!" became the birth of Fauvism, and he left behind color-revolutionary masterpieces like The Dance, The Green Stripe, and The Red Room.

Matisse conveyed emotion through pure color alone, pushing the decorative potential of painting to its limit.
"The purpose of color is not to describe form but to express emotion." — A painter who sang joy through color.
tr.translated:
Matisse, Fransız Fovizm'inin en büyük temsilcisidir.
21 yaşında yatağa düştüğünde annesi bir boya kutusu verdi; "Cenneti keşfetmiş gibi oldum" diye hatırladı.
1905'te "Vahşiler!" çığlığı Fovizm'in başlangıcı oldu; Dans, Yeşil Çizgi ve Kırmızı Oda gibi renk devriminin başyapıtlarını bıraktı.

Matisse saf renkle duyguları aktardı ve resmin dekoratif olanaklarını sınırlarına kadar zorladı.
"Rengin amacı biçim değil, duyguyu ifade etmektir." — Renklerle neşe şarkısı söyleyen ressam.
tr.back:
Matisse is the greatest representative of French Fauvism.
At the age of 21, when he fell into bed, his mother gave him a paint box; "It was as if I had discovered paradise," he recalled.
In 1905, his cry of "Savages!" marked the beginning of Fauvism; he left masterpieces of the color revolution such as Dance, Green Line and Red Room.

Matisse conveyed emotions with pure color and pushed the decorative possibilities of painting to its limits.
"The purpose of color is to express emotion, not form." - The painter who sang a song of joy with color.

tr.original:
Chagall was a painter of love and dreams.
Born in a Russian Jewish village, he moved to Paris and never put down his brush through two World Wars and exile.
His love for his wife Bella was a lifelong source of his art, leaving behind masterpieces like The Birthday, La Mariée, and I and the Village — canvases where bouquets and lovers float in midair.

Chagall worked until 97, enchanting the world with the Paris Opéra ceiling and stained glass windows.
"In our life there is a single color, as on an artist's palette, which provides the meaning of life and art — the color of love." — A poet who erased the boundary between dream and reality.
tr.translated:
Chagall, aşkı ve rüyaları tuvale döken bir ressamdır.
Rusya'daki bir Yahudi köyünde doğup Paris'e göç etti; iki dünya savaşı ve sürgünde bile fırçasını bırakmadı.
Eşi Bella ömür boyu sanatının kaynağıydı; Doğum Günü, Gelin ve Ben ve Köy gibi buketlerin ve âşıkların süzüldüğü başyapıtlar bıraktı.

Chagall 97 yaşına kadar çalıştı ve Paris Operası tavan resimleri ile vitraylarıyla dünyayı büyüledi.
"Hayatımızda yalnızca bir renk var — aşkın rengi." — Gerçek ile rüya arasındaki sınırı silen hayalperest şair.
tr.back:
Chagall was a painter who put love and dreams on canvas.
Born in a Jewish village in Russia, he emigrated to Paris and never put down his brush, even through two world wars and exile.
His wife Bella was the source of his lifelong art; she left masterpieces such as Birthday, The Bride and Me and The Village, where bouquets and lovers float.

Chagall worked until the age of 97 and captivated the world with his Paris Opera ceiling paintings and stained glass.
"There is only one color in our life - the color of love." - The dreamy poet who erased the boundary between reality and dream.

tr.original:
Picasso co-founded Cubism and changed the direction of modern art.
In his 91-year life, he left approximately 50,000 works. Legend has it that at 13, his art teacher father saw his talent and broke his own brushes.
He opened Cubism with Les Demoiselles d'Avignon, denounced war with Guernica, and reached the pinnacle of deconstruction with Portrait of Dora Maar.

Through Blue Period, Rose Period, Cubism, and Surrealism, Picasso constantly reinvented himself.
"Good artists copy, great artists steal." — A painter who broke all rules and created new ones.
tr.translated:
Picasso, Kübizm'i kuran ve modern sanatın yönünü değiştiren devrimcidir.
91 yıllık hayatında yaklaşık 50.000 eser bıraktı; 13 yaşında sanat öğretmeni babası onun yeteneğini görünce fırçayı bıraktı.
Avignonlu Kızlar ile Kübizm'in kapısını açtı, Guernica ile savaşı lanetledi, Dora Maar Portresi ile yapıbozumun zirvesini gösterdi.

Picasso, Mavi Dönem, Pembe Dönem, Kübizm, Sürrealizm'den geçerek durmaksızın kendini yeniden yarattı.
"İyi sanatçılar taklit eder, büyük sanatçılar çalar." — Tüm kuralları yıkıp yenilerini yaratan ressam.
tr.back:
Picasso is the revolutionary who founded Cubism and changed the direction of modern art.
In his 91 years of life, he left nearly 50,000 works of art; at the age of 13, when his art teacher father saw his talent, he put down the brush.
He opened the door to Cubism with The Girls from Avignon, condemned war with Guernica, and showed the peak of deconstruction with Portrait of Dora Maar.

Picasso went through the Blue Period, the Pink Period, Cubism, Surrealism and constantly reinvented himself.
"Good artists imitate, great artists steal." - The painter who broke all the rules and created new ones.

tr.original:
Frida Kahlo is Mexico's most original painter and a feminist icon.
She contracted polio at 6 and at 18 suffered a bus accident that shattered her spine and pelvis, enduring over 30 surgeries throughout her life.
She began painting while bedridden, looking at a mirror on the ceiling — and much of her work, including Me and My Parrots, The Broken Column, and Self-Portrait with Monkeys, are self-portraits.

Frida's art, with its honest portrayal of pain, love, and identity, continues to resonate deeply today.
"Feet, what do I need them for if I have wings to fly?" — A painter who transformed pain into art.
tr.translated:
Frida Kahlo, Meksika'nın en özgün ressamı ve feminizmin simgesidir.
6 yaşında çocuk felcine yakalandı, 18 yaşında bir otobüs kazasında omurgası ve leğen kemiği parçalandı, hayatı boyunca 30'dan fazla ameliyat geçirdi.
Yatağa düştükten sonra tavana monte edilmiş aynaya bakarak kendini çizmeye başladı; Ben ve Papağanlarım, Kırık Sütun, Maymunlu Otoportre — eserlerinin çoğu otoportredir.

Frida'nın acıyı, aşkı ve kimliği açıkça tuvale döktüğü sanat dünyası bugün hâlâ derin yankılar uyandırıyor.
"Ayaklar, sizi ne yapayım, uçacak kanatlarım varken." — Acıyı sanata yücelten yenilmez ressam.
tr.back:
Frida Kahlo is Mexico's most original painter and an icon of feminism.
At the age of 6 she contracted polio, at 18 her spine and pelvis were shattered in a bus accident, and she underwent more than 30 operations throughout her life.
After falling into bed, he began to draw himself looking into the mirror mounted on the ceiling; Me and My Parrots, Broken Column, Self-Portrait with Monkey - most of his works are self-portraits.

Frida's art world, where pain, love and identity were openly put on canvas, still resonates deeply today.
"Feet, what should I do with you, when I have wings to fly." - The invincible painter who sublimated pain into art.

tr.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who broke the boundaries of fine art with comic book panels.
When his son pointed to a Mickey Mouse comic and asked, "Can you draw as well as this, Dad?", he actually began transferring comics to canvas.
With In the Car, M-Maybe, and Forget It!, he shook the art world by blowing up comic panels onto massive canvases.

Lichtenstein, with Ben-Day dots and bold outlines, overturned the hierarchy between popular culture and high art.
"I don't draw comics, I paint pictures about comics." — A painter who erased the line between pop culture and fine art.
tr.translated:
Lichtenstein, tek bir çizgi roman karesiyle güzel sanatların sınırını yıkan Pop Art ustasıdır.
Oğlu Mickey Mouse çizgi romanını gösterip "Baba, sen de bu kadar iyi çizebilir misin?" diye sorunca, gerçekten çizgi romanları tuvale taşımaya başladı.
Arabada, Belki ve Unut Gitsin! — çizgi roman sahnelerini dev tuvallere taşıyarak sanat dünyasını sarstı.

Lichtenstein, Ben-Day noktaları ve kalın kontur çizgileriyle popüler kültür ile sanat arasındaki hiyerarşiyi altüst etti.
"Çizgi roman çizmiyorum, çizgi romanı konu alan resimler yapıyorum." — Kitle kültürü ile güzel sanatlar arasındaki sınırı silen ressam.
tr.back:
Lichtenstein is the Pop Art master who broke the boundaries of fine art with a single comic book frame.
When his son pointed to a Mickey Mouse comic book and asked, "Dad, can you draw that well?" he really started to bring comics to canvas.
In the Car, Maybe and Forget It! - shook the art world by bringing comic book scenes to giant canvases.

Lichtenstein subverted the hierarchy between popular culture and art with his Ben-Day dots and thick contour lines.
"I don't draw comics, I make paintings about comics." - The painter who erased the boundary between mass culture and fine art.

tr.original:
The swirling impasto brushstrokes of 〈The Starry Night〉 have been applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow unleashes the energy of the night.

〈The Starry Night〉 was Van Gogh's reimagined vision of the landscape beyond his asylum window at Saint-Rémy.
Rather than painting the actual night sky, he projected his inner turmoil onto the stars.
tr.translated:
Yıldızlı Gece'nin dönen impasto fırça darbeleri uygulandı.
Kobalt mavisi ile krom sarısı arasındaki yoğun kontrast, gecenin enerjisini patlatıyor.

Yıldızlı Gece, Van Gogh'un Saint-Rémy akıl hastanesinin penceresinden gördüğü manzarayı hayal gücüyle yeniden kurduğu eserdir.
Gerçek gece gökyüzünü değil, iç dünyasındaki girdabı yıldızlara yansıttı.
tr.back:
Starry Night's swirling impasto brushstrokes were applied.
The intense contrast between cobalt blue and chrome yellow explodes the energy of the night.

Starry Night is Van Gogh's imaginative reconstruction of the view from the window of the Saint-Rémy asylum.
He reflected not the real night sky, but the whirlpool of his inner world in the stars.

tr.original:
The complementary color nightscape technique of 〈Café Terrace at Night〉—without using black—has been applied.
Yellow gaslights and deep blue night sky express darkness through color contrast alone.

〈Café Terrace at Night〉 is the first outdoor night scene Van Gogh painted under starlight at Arles' Place du Forum.
The empty chairs on the terrace and passing figures hold both the stillness and warmth of the night.
tr.translated:
Gece Kafe Terası'nın siyah kullanmadan tamamlayıcı renklerle gece sahnesi tekniği uygulandı.
Altın sarısı gaz lambası ve derin mavi gece gökyüzü karanlığı yalnızca renk kontrastıyla ifade ediyor.

Gece Kafe Terası, Van Gogh'un yıldızlı gökyüzünü resmettiği ilk eserdir; daha sonra Yıldızlı Gece'ye ilham verdi.
Kafenin sıcak ışığı altında yıldızlar, gecenin küçük güneşleri gibi parıldıyor.
tr.back:
Night Café Terrace's night scene technique was applied with complementary colors without using black.
The golden yellow kerosene lamp and the deep blue night sky express darkness only through color contrast.

The Night Café Terrace was Van Gogh's first painting of a starry sky; it later inspired Starry Night.
In the warm light of the café, the stars twinkle like little suns in the night.

tr.original:
The subtle tonal variations of a single chrome yellow in 〈Sunflowers〉 have been applied.
Thickly layered paint captures the texture of the petals as they are.

〈Sunflowers〉 is a series Van Gogh painted to decorate the Yellow House in Arles for Gauguin's arrival.
Full blooms, wilted flowers, and seeds together reveal the cycle of life.
tr.translated:
Ayçiçekleri'nin kalın yağlıboya ve krom sarısı tonu uygulandı.
Sarı üstüne sarı, boya o kadar kalın ki tuval üzerinde heykelsi bir doku kazanıyor.

Ayçiçekleri, Van Gogh'un yakın dostu Gauguin'i Arles'a gelişinde karşılamak için yaptığı hoşgeldin hediyesidir.
Tam açmıştan solmuşa, canlılık ve çürüme aynı anda ifade ediliyor.
tr.back:
Sunflowers was painted in a thick oil and chrome yellow tone.
Yellow on yellow, the paint is so thick that it takes on a sculptural texture on the canvas.

Sunflowers were Van Gogh's welcome gift to his close friend Gauguin on his arrival in Arles.
From full bloom to wilting, vitality and decay are expressed simultaneously.

tr.original:
The swirling background and thick impasto brushstrokes of 〈Self-Portrait〉 have been applied.
Cool tones of blue and green convey both solitude and resolve.

〈Self-Portrait〉 was painted when Van Gogh, too poor to afford models, used himself as subject.
The eyes gazing directly forward and tightly closed lips show unwavering will amid suffering.
tr.translated:
Otoportre'nin kısa fırça darbeleri ve sarmal arka planı uygulandı.
Soğuk tonlar ve dönen fırça darbeleri psikolojik bir gerilim atmosferi yaratıyor.

Otoportre, Van Gogh'un model tutacak parası olmadığında kendini çizerek ürettiği bir eserdir.
Doğrudan bakış ve sıkılmış dudaklar, acı içinde bile sarsılmayan bir iradeyi gösteriyor.
tr.back:
The short brushstrokes and spiraling background of Self-Portrait are applied.
Cold tones and swirling brushstrokes create an atmosphere of psychological tension.

Self-portrait is a work Van Gogh produced by drawing himself when he could not afford a model.
The direct gaze and pursed lips show a will that remains unshaken even in pain.

tr.original:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of 〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 have been applied.
Wind-blown wheat and flame-like cypresses convey nature's vital energy.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 is a landscape Van Gogh repeatedly painted near the asylum at Saint-Rémy.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the vertical cypress connect earth and sky.
tr.translated:
Selvili Buğday Tarlası'nın dinamik kavisli fırça darbeleri ve tamamlayıcı renk kontrastları uygulandı.
Rüzgârda sallanan başaklar ve alev gibi yükselen selviler doğanın yaşam gücünü aktarıyor.

Selvili Buğday Tarlası, Van Gogh'un Saint-Rémy akıl hastanesi yakınında tekrar tekrar resmettiği manzaradır.
Altın buğday tarlasının ufuk çizgisi ve dikey selviler yeryüzü ile gökyüzünü birleştiriyor.
tr.back:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of Wheat Field with Cypresses were applied.
Spikes swaying in the wind and cypresses rising like flames convey the life force of nature.

Wheat Field with Cypresses is the landscape Van Gogh painted repeatedly near the Saint-Rémy asylum.
The horizon line of the golden wheat field and the vertical cypresses unite earth and sky.

tr.original:
The gold leaf background and geometric decorative patterns of 〈The Kiss〉 have been applied.
Rectangles on the man's robe contrast with curves and circles on the woman's, creating opposition and harmony.

〈The Kiss〉 is Klimt's masterpiece from his "Golden Phase," inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The lovers' embrace wrapped in gold holds both the ecstasy of love and the anxiety of its passing.
tr.translated:
Öpücük'ün altın varak arka planı ve geometrik dekoratif desenleri uygulandı.
Erkek giysisindeki dikdörtgenler kadın giysisindeki eğriler ve dairelerle kontrast ve uyum oluşturuyor.

Öpücük, Klimt'in Bizans mozaiklerinden ilham alarak "Altın Dönem"de yaptığı başyapıttır.
Altınla sarılmış âşıkların kucaklaşması aynı anda aşkın coşkusunu ve geçiciliğin huzursuzluğunu barındırıyor.
tr.back:
The Kiss has a gold foil background and geometric decorative patterns.
The rectangles in menswear contrast and harmonize with the curves and circles in womenswear.

The Kiss is Klimt's masterpiece from the "Golden Period", inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The embrace of the lovers wrapped in gold simultaneously embodies the ecstasy of love and the restlessness of impermanence.

tr.original:
The spiral curves and golden decorative patterns of 〈The Tree of Life〉 have been applied.
Triangles, circles, and spirals repeat, creating a rhythmic flow of life's energy.

〈The Tree of Life〉 was created as a dining room mural for the Stoclet Palace in Brussels.
The endlessly branching spiral limbs are a metaphor for birth, death, and the cycle of life.
tr.translated:
Hayat Ağacı'nın sarmal eğrileri ve altın dekoratif desenleri uygulandı.
Üçgenler, daireler ve girdaplar tekrarlanıyor; yaşam enerjisi ritmik bir şekilde akıyor.

Hayat Ağacı, Klimt'in Brüksel'deki Stoclet Sarayı yemek odası için yaptığı duvar resmidir.
Sonsuzca uzanan sarmal dallar, doğum, ölüm ve yaşam döngüsünün metaforu.
tr.back:
The spiral curves of the Tree of Life and gold decorative patterns were applied.
Triangles, circles and swirls repeat; life energy flows rhythmically.

The Tree of Life is Klimt's mural for the dining room of the Stoclet Palace in Brussels.
The endlessly spiraling branches are a metaphor for the cycle of birth, death and life.

tr.original:
The gold leaf decoration and jewel-like sensual colors of 〈Judith〉 have been applied.
Transparent veils and gold necklaces create tension between the sacred and the sensual.

〈Judith〉 is Klimt's reinterpretation of the biblical heroine as a sensual woman.
Half-closed eyes and a provocative smile completely overturn the piety of traditional religious painting.
tr.translated:
Judith'in altın varak dekorasyonu ve mücevher gibi şehvani renkleri uygulandı.
Şeffaf peçe ve altın kolye, kutsal ile şehvani arasında bir gerilim yaratıyor.

Judith, Klimt'in İncil'deki kadın kahramanı şehvani bir kadın olarak yeniden yorumladığı eserdir.
Yarı kapalı gözler ve kışkırtıcı gülümseme, geleneksel dini resmin ciddiyetini tamamen altüst ediyor.
tr.back:
Judith's gold leaf decoration and jewel-like sensual colors are applied.
The transparent veil and the gold necklace create a tension between the sacred and the carnal.

Judith is Klimt's reinterpretation of the biblical heroine as a sensual woman.
The half-closed eyes and provocative smile completely subvert the seriousness of traditional religious painting.

tr.original:
The undulating curves and distorted forms of 〈The Scream〉 have been applied.
A blood-red sky and rippling background contrast with the straight railing, creating extreme anxiety.

〈The Scream〉 captures the terror Munch felt when the sky turned blood-red during a walk.
The open-mouthed figure's cry is expressed not as sound but as a tremor passing through all existence.
tr.translated:
Çığlık'ın dalgalanan eğrileri ve çarpık biçimleri uygulandı.
Kanlı gökyüzü ve sallanan manzara, düz korkuluk çizgisiyle kontrast oluşturarak aşırı huzursuzluk yaratıyor.

Çığlık, Munch'un yürüyüş sırasında gökyüzü kırmızıya döndüğünde duyduğu korkuyu resmetttiği eserdir.
Ağzı açık figürün çığlığı bir ses değil, tüm varoluşu delip geçen bir titreşimdir.
tr.back:
The undulating curves and distorted forms of the Scream are applied.
The bloody sky and swaying landscape contrast with the straight line of the railing, creating extreme unease.

The Scream is the work in which Munch depicts the fear he feels when the sky turns red during a walk.
The scream of the open-mouthed figure is not a sound, but a vibration that pierces the whole existence.

tr.original:
The red halo and undulating sensual curves of 〈Madonna〉 have been applied.
At the boundary between open and closed eyes, life and death merge into one.

〈Madonna〉 is Munch's reinterpretation of the Virgin Mary as a moment where sensuality and death intersect.
Closed eyes, parted lips, and a red halo reveal human desire rather than holiness.
tr.translated:
Madonna'nın kırmızı halesi ve dalgalanan şehvani eğrileri uygulandı.
Açık gözler ile kapalı gözler arasındaki sınırda yaşam ve ölüm bir oluyor.

Madonna, Munch'un Meryem Ana'yı şehvet ile ölümün kesiştiği bir an olarak yeniden yorumladığı eserdir.
Kapalı gözler, aralık dudaklar ve kırmızı hale, kutsallığı değil insani arzuyu ortaya koyuyor.
tr.back:
Madonna's red halo and undulating sensual curves are applied.
At the border between open eyes and closed eyes, life and death become one.

Madonna is Munch's reinterpretation of the Virgin Mary as a moment where lust and death intersect.
Closed eyes, parted lips and a red halo reveal human desire, not divinity.

tr.original:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark colors in 〈The Dance of Life〉 has been applied.
With the central woman in a red dress as axis, innocence and despair are placed on either side.

〈The Dance of Life〉 is Munch's autobiographical work compressing memories of love and heartbreak into one canvas.
Light and dark colors alternate on a single canvas, capturing both the beginning and end of love.
tr.translated:
Hayatın Dansı'nın açık ve koyu renkler arasındaki sembolik kontrastlar uygulandı.
Ortadaki kırmızı elbiseli kadın eksen; saflık ve umutsuzluk iki yana yerleştirilmiş.

Hayatın Dansı, Munch'un aşk ve hayal kırıklığı anılarını tek bir karede sıkıştırdığı otobiyografik eserdir.
Açık ve koyu renkler aynı sahnede kesişiyor, aşkın başlangıcını ve sonunu aynı anda yakalıyor.
tr.back:
The symbolic contrasts between light and dark colors of the Dance of Life were applied.
The woman in the red dress in the center is the axis; purity and despair are placed on either side.

The Dance of Life is an autobiographical work in which Munch compresses his memories of love and disappointment into a single frame.
Light and dark colors intersect in the same scene, capturing the beginning and end of love at the same time.

tr.original:
The bold color plane division that bisects the face in 〈The Green Stripe〉 has been applied.
Pink, yellow, and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring natural color.

〈The Green Stripe〉 is a portrait Matisse painted of his wife Amélie, drawing cries of "Wild beasts!" at the 1905 Salon.
A single green stripe down the center of the face divides light and shadow through color alone.
tr.translated:
Yeşil Çizgi'nin yüzü cesurca kesen renk yüzeyi bölümlemesi uygulandı.
Pembe, sarı ve yeşil ten üzerinde bir arada yaşıyor, gerçekliğin renklerini tamamen görmezden geliyor.

Yeşil Çizgi, Matisse'in eşi Amélie'nin portresidir; 1905 Salon'unda "Vahşiler!" diye bağırılmasına neden oldu.
Yüzün ortasındaki tek yeşil çizgi, ışık ve gölgeyi yalnızca renkle ayırıyor.
tr.back:
Green Line's color surface segmentation is applied, boldly cutting across the face.
Pink, yellow and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring the colors of reality.

The Green Line is a portrait of Matisse's wife Amélie; at the Salon of 1905, it prompted shouts of "Savages!".
The single green line in the center of the face separates light and shadow with color alone.

tr.original:
The intense color planes and decorative flat composition of 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 have been applied.
The boundary between figure and background dissolves, with color itself creating space.

〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 is a work where Matisse conveyed emotion through simplified forms and pure color.
The vast color plane of the purple coat dominates the canvas, merging with the background patterns as one.
tr.translated:
Mor Paltolu Kadın'ın yoğun renk yüzeyleri ve dekoratif düz kompozisyonu uygulandı.
Figür ile arka plan arasındaki sınır bulanıklaşıyor; renk kendi başına mekân yaratıyor.

Mor Paltolu Kadın, Matisse'in basitleştirilmiş formlar ve saf renkle duyguları aktardığı eserdir.
Mor paltonun büyük renk yüzeyi kompozisyona hâkim olup arka plan desenleriyle kaynaşıyor.
tr.back:
The Woman in the Purple Coat's intense color surfaces and decorative flat composition are applied.
The boundary between the figure and the background blurs; color creates space on its own.

Woman in a Purple Coat is a work in which Matisse conveys emotion through simplified forms and pure color.
The large color surface of the purple coat dominates the composition and fuses with the background patterns.

tr.original:
The flat color plane composition that defies perspective in 〈The Red Room〉 has been applied.
The red wall and red table merge into one as arabesque patterns blanket the entire space.

〈The Red Room〉 was originally painted in blue, but Matisse repainted it red just before completion.
Even the view through the window appears as flat decoration, erasing the boundary between inside and out.
tr.translated:
Kırmızı Oda'nın perspektifi yok sayan düz renk kompozisyonu uygulandı.
Kırmızı duvar ve kırmızı masa birleşiyor; arabesk desenler tüm mekânı kaplıyor.

Kırmızı Oda'yı Matisse ilk başta mavi boyamış, ama bitirmeden kırmızıya çevirmiştir.
Pencereden görünen manzara bile düz bir dekorasyon gibi görünüyor; iç ile dış arasındaki sınır siliniyor.
tr.back:
The flat color composition of the Red Room, which ignores perspective, was applied.
The red wall and the red table merge; arabesque patterns cover the entire space.

Matisse initially painted the Red Room blue, but changed it to red before finishing it.
Even the view from the window looks like a flat decoration; the boundary between inside and outside is erased.

tr.original:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin colors of 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with bold primary colors, render the subject in a wild, Fauvist manner.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 is a portrait of friendship Matisse painted of his fellow artist André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing their Fauvist experiments together.
tr.translated:
André Derain Portresi'nin kaba fırça darbeleri ve gerçek dışı ten renkleri uygulandı.
Yeşil gölgeler, turuncu ten ve yoğun ana renkler figürü "vahşi" bir biçimde betimliyor.

André Derain Portresi, Matisse'in ressam arkadaşı André Derain için yaptığı dostluk portresidir.
İki ressam aynı dönemde birbirlerinin portrelerini yaparak Fovizm deneyimlerini paylaştı.
tr.back:
Portrait of André Derain's rough brushstrokes and unrealistic skin colors were applied.
Green shadows, orange skin and intense primary colors depict the figure in a "wild" way.

Portrait of André Derain is a friendship portrait that Matisse painted for his painter friend André Derain.
The two painters shared their experiences of Fauvism by painting each other's portraits in the same period.

tr.original:
The dreamlike colors and overlapping images of 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and violet blend together, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 is a work Chagall painted of his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife.
A vivid bouquet of red and white flowers glows against a deep green and navy background, expressing the warmth of love.
tr.translated:
Doğum Günü'nün rüya gibi renkleri ve rüya gibi üst üste binen görüntüleri uygulandı.
Pembe, kobalt mavisi ve mor kaynaşıyor; rüya ile gerçek arasındaki sınır siliniyor.

Doğum Günü, Chagall'ın ömür boyu ilham perisi ve eşi Bella'ya olan aşkını tuvale döktüğü eserdir.
Gerçek arka plan üzerine rüya gibi üst üste binen âşıklar, aşkın sarhoşluğunu ifade ediyor.
tr.back:
The dreamy colors and dreamy overlapping images of Birthday are applied.
Pink, cobalt blue and purple fuse, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

Birthday is Chagall's canvas expression of his love for his lifelong muse and wife Bella.
The lovers overlapping like a dream on a real background expresses the intoxication of love.

tr.original:
The dreamlike contrast of red bouquets and blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and figures blend like apparitions, unfolding a festive fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and church spires of Chagall's hometown Vitebsk as a landscape of memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet, overlaid with the village scene, holds both nostalgia and joy.
tr.translated:
Gelin'in kırmızı buket ile mavi gece gökyüzü arasındaki rüya gibi kontrastı uygulandı.
Hayvanlar ve figürler hayaletler gibi kaynaşıyor; şenlikli bir hayal açılıyor.

Gelin, Chagall'ın memleketi Vitebsk'in çatılarını ve çan kulelerini bir anı manzarası olarak yakalıyor.
Kırmızı buketli gelin ile üst üste binen köy manzarası aynı anda özlem ve neşe barındırıyor.
tr.back:
The dreamy contrast between the Bride's red bouquet and the blue night sky is applied.
Animals and figures mingle like ghosts; a festive dream opens up.

The bride captures the rooftops and bell towers of Chagall's hometown of Vitebsk as a landscape of memories.
The bride with the red bouquet and the overlapping village landscape are at once wistful and joyful.

tr.original:
The multiple perspectives and transparently overlapping images of 〈I and the Village〉 have been applied.
People, animals, and village scenery intermingle dreamlike, defying scale and proportion.

〈I and the Village〉 was painted in Paris as Chagall longed for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, capturing an endless yearning for home.
tr.translated:
Ben ve Köy'ün çoklu perspektifleri ve şeffaf üst üste binen görüntüleri uygulandı.
İnsanlar, hayvanlar ve köy manzarası bir rüyadaki gibi oran ve ölçek tanımadan karışıyor.

Ben ve Köy, Chagall'ın Paris'te memleketi Vitebsk'i özleyerek yaptığı eserdir.
Gerçek ve anı tek bir tuvalde kaynaşıyor; vatana sonsuz bir özlemi taşıyor.
tr.back:
Multiple perspectives and transparent overlapping images of Me and the Village were applied.
People, animals and the village landscape blend like in a dream, without proportion or scale.

Me and the Village is a work Chagall painted in Paris, longing for his hometown Vitebsk.
Reality and memory fuse on a single canvas; it carries an endless longing for the homeland.

tr.original:
The simultaneous front and side view of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 has been applied.
Intense primary color planes divide the face geometrically, creating Cubist tension.

〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 was painted by Picasso of his lover and fellow photographer Dora Maar.
The seated woman's front and side coexist in one face, revealing the complexity within.
tr.translated:
Dora Maar Portresi'nin cephe ve profili aynı anda gösterimi uygulandı.
Yoğun ana renk yüzeyleri yüzü geometrik olarak bölüyor; kübist bir gerilim yaratıyor.

Dora Maar Portresi, Picasso'nun sevgilisi ve fotoğrafçı Dora Maar için yaptığı portredir.
Oturan kadının cephesi ve profili tek bir yüzde bir arada yaşıyor; iç dünyanın karmaşıklığını açığa çıkarıyor.
tr.back:
Portrait of Dora Maar's façade and profile were rendered simultaneously.
Intense primary color surfaces geometrically divide the face, creating a cubist tension.

Portrait of Dora Maar is Picasso's portrait of his lover and photographer Dora Maar.
The façade and profile of the seated woman coexist in a single face, revealing the complexity of the inner world.

tr.original:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Vivid reds, greens, and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.
tr.translated:
Ben ve Papağanlarım'ın yoğun doğrudan bakışı ve Meksika halk renkleri uygulandı.
Canlı kırmızı, yeşil ve maviler tropikal bitkilerle birlikte tuvali dolduruyor.

Ben ve Papağanlarım, Frida'nın dört papağanla çevrili bir otoportresidir.
Papağanlar Meksika folklorunda aşkın elçileridir ve Frida'nın yalnızlığında sadık yoldaşlarıdır.
tr.back:
The intense direct gaze of Me and My Parrots and Mexican folk colors are applied.
Vibrant reds, greens and blues fill the canvas along with tropical plants.

Me and My Parrots is a self-portrait of Frida surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are the messengers of love in Mexican folklore and Frida's faithful companions in her loneliness.

tr.original:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 have been applied.
Amid dark green leaves, her intense eyes and connected eyebrows reveal her identity.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait Frida painted with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were surrogates for the children she could never have.
tr.translated:
Maymunlu Otoportre'nin doğrudan bakışı ve yemyeşil tropikal arka planı uygulandı.
Koyu yeşil yapraklar arasında keskin bakışı ve birleşik kaşları kimliğini ortaya koyuyor.

Maymunlu Otoportre, Frida'nın evcil maymunlarıyla birlikte yaptığı otoportredir.
Meksika mitolojisinde maymunlar arzuyu simgeler, ama Frida için sahip olamadığı çocukların yerini tutan varlıklardı.
tr.back:
Self-Portrait with Monkey's direct gaze and lush tropical background are applied.
Among the dark green leaves, his sharp gaze and united eyebrows reveal his identity.

Self-Portrait with Monkeys is a self-portrait of Frida with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology, monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were a substitute for the children she could not have.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of 〈In the Car〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill the canvas like a comic book panel.

〈In the Car〉 was created by enlarging a panel from the comic Girls' Romances onto canvas.
Lichtenstein deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image instead of narrative.
tr.translated:
Arabada'nın Ben-Day noktaları ve kalın siyah kontur çizgileri uygulandı.
Yalnızca saf ana renkler — kırmızı, mavi ve sarı — tuvali bir çizgi roman karesi gibi dolduruyor.

Arabada, Lichtenstein'ın Girls' Romances çizgi roman dergisinden bir kareyi tuvale büyüttüğü eserdir.
Orijinaldeki konuşma balonunu bilerek çıkardı; yalnızca görüntünün gücünü bıraktı.
tr.back:
In the car's Ben-Day dots and thick black contour lines were applied.
Only pure primary colors - red, blue and yellow - fill the canvas like a comic book frame.

In the Car is Lichtenstein's enlargement on canvas of a frame from the comic strip Girls' Romances.
He deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narrative of speech bubbles in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flatly within thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, which sold for $30,000—five times the going rate.
It is now displayed as a centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.
tr.translated:
Belki'nin Ben-Day noktaları ve çizgi roman konuşma balonu anlatısı uygulandı.
Ana renkler kalın kontur çizgileri içinde düz boyandı; baskı sayfası dokusu yeniden yaratıldı.

Belki, Lichtenstein'ın özel koleksiyonunda tuttuğu ve 30.000 dolara — piyasa fiyatının beş katına — sattığı eserdir.
Bugün Almanya Köln'deki Ludwig Müzesi'nin Pop Art koleksiyonunun simge eseri olarak sergileniyor.
tr.back:
Maybe's Ben-Day dots and the comic book speech bubble narrative were applied.
Primary colors were painted flat within thick contour lines; the texture of the printed page was recreated.

Perhaps it is the work Lichtenstein kept in his private collection and sold for $30,000 - five times the market price.
Today it is on display at the Ludwig Museum in Cologne, Germany, as a landmark in the Pop Art collection.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterwork created just after Lichtenstein's first solo show in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: "Is He the Worst Artist in the U.S.?"—about him.
tr.translated:
Unut Gitsin!'in Ben-Day noktaları ve yoğun ana renk kontrastları uygulandı.
Kalın siyah kontur çizgileri formu çizgi roman karesi gibi sabitledi; duyguyu dramatik biçimde sıkıştırdı.

Unut Gitsin!, 1962'de Lichtenstein'ın ilk kişisel sergisinden sonra ürettiği erken dönem eseridir.
Aynı yıl Life dergisi "Amerika'nın en kötü sanatçısı mı?" diye manşet attı — ondan bahsediyordu.
tr.back:
Forget It!'s Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts were applied.
Thick black contour lines fix the form like a comic book frame, dramatically compressing the emotion.

Forget It! is Lichtenstein's early work, produced after his first solo exhibition in 1962.
In the same year, Life magazine headlined "America's worst artist?" - referring to him.

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow separate cleanly within black outlines, as crisp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work Lichtenstein borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, overturning the value of what he once called "used canvas."
tr.translated:
Ohh... Tamam...'ın Ben-Day noktaları ve düz renk kompozisyonu uygulandı.
Kırmızı, mavi ve sarı siyah kontur çizgileri içinde net bir şekilde ayrılıyor; basılı sayfa kadar keskin.

Ohh... Tamam... 1964'te Secret Hearts dergisinin 88. sayısından alınmış bir eserdir.
2010'da Christie's'te 42,6 milyon dolara satılarak onun "kullanılmış tuval" dediği şeyin değerini altüst etti.
tr.back:
Ohh... Okay...'s Ben-Day dots and solid color composition are applied.
Red, blue and yellow are clearly separated within black contour lines, as sharp as the printed page.

Ohh... Okay... It's from the 88th issue of Secret Hearts magazine in 1964.
In 2010, it sold at Christie's for $42.6 million, undercutting the value of what he called "used canvas."

tr.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in a comic-like manner, recreating the texture of print.

〈Still Life〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre as homage to Picasso.
"There's no mood in my still lifes—it's just lemons and grapefruits," he said.
tr.translated:
Natürmort'un Ben-Day noktaları ve kalın kontur çizgilerinin düz ifadesi uygulandı.
Ana renk yüzeyleri nesneleri çizgi roman tarzında sadeleştiriyor; baskı dokusu yeniden yaratılıyor.

Natürmort, 1970'lerde Lichtenstein'ın Picasso'ya saygı olarak geleneksel konuyu yeniden yorumladığı serinin bir parçasıdır.
"Natürmortlarımda atmosfer yok — sadece limon ve greyfurt var" dedi.
tr.back:
Still Life's Ben-Day dots and the flat expression of thick contour lines are applied.
Primary color surfaces simplify objects in a comic book style; the print texture is recreated.

Still Life is part of a series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted the traditional subject matter in homage to Picasso in the 1970s.
"There is no atmosphere in my still lifes - just lemon and grapefruit," he said.

tr.original:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

Van Gogh left over 40 self-portraits, confronting his inner self most honestly through them.
The eyes gazing directly forward show unwavering will even amid suffering.
tr.translated:
Van Gogh'un kalın impasto tekniği ve dönen fırça darbeleri uygulandı.
Sarı ile mavi arasındaki yoğun kontrast iç dünyanın duygularını açığa çıkarıyor.

40'tan fazla otoportre bıraktı; iç dünyasıyla en büyük dürüstlükle yüzleşti.
Doğrudan bakış, acı içinde bile sarsılmayan bir iradeyi gösteriyor.
tr.back:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes were applied.
The intense contrast between yellow and blue reveals the emotions of the inner world.

He left more than 40 self-portraits; he faced his inner world with the greatest honesty.
The direct gaze shows a will that remains unshaken even in pain.

tr.original:
Klimt's real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on men's clothing contrast with curves and circles on women's, expressing both opposition and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decoration wrap the figure in a mystical golden world.
Within this decoration, sensuality and divinity coexist.
tr.translated:
Gerçek altın varak ve Bizans mozaiklerinden ilham alan geometrik desenler uygulandı.
Erkek giysisindeki dikdörtgenler kadın giysisindeki eğriler ve dairelerle kontrast ve uyum yaratıyor.

Sarmal eğriler ve zengin süsleme figürü gizemli bir altın dünyada sarıyor.
Bu dekorasyonda şehvani ve kutsal bir arada yaşıyor.
tr.back:
Real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The rectangles in menswear contrast and harmonize with the curves and circles in womenswear.

Spiral curves and rich ornamentation envelop the figure in a mysterious golden world.
The sensual and the sacred coexist in this decoration.

tr.original:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors have been applied.
Blood-red skies and wavering landscapes visualize inner emotions.

Munch wrote in his diary that while walking by the Oslo fjord, he "heard an enormous scream pass through nature."
Before his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.
tr.translated:
Dönen eğriler, çarpık biçimler ve yoğun renkler uygulandı.
Kan kırmızısı gökyüzü ve sallanan manzaralar iç duyguları görünür kılıyor.

Günlüğünde Oslo fiyordunda yürürken "doğayı delip geçen devasa bir çığlık duyduğunu" yazdı.
Tablolarının önünde renkler duyguların titreşimine dönüşüyor.
tr.back:
Twisting curves, distorted forms and intense colors are applied.
Blood-red skies and swaying landscapes make inner feelings visible.

In his diary he wrote that while walking in the Oslo fjord he "heard a gigantic scream piercing the nature".
In front of his paintings, colors turn into vibrations of emotions.

tr.original:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors have been applied.
Rather than anatomical accuracy, focus is on the energy of movement, with color directly conveying emotion.

Just as Matisse erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room, he the boundary between table and wall, he chose harmony of color planes over perspective.
Before his canvas, colors sing out.
tr.translated:
Basitleştirilmiş formlar ve cesur ana renkler uygulandı.
Anatomik doğruluğun peşinden gitmek yerine hareketin enerjisine odaklanıyor; renk duyguyu doğrudan aktarıyor.

Kırmızı Oda'da masa ile duvar arasındaki sınırı sildiği gibi, perspektif yerine renk yüzeylerinin uyumunu seçti.
Tuvalinin önünde renkler bir şarkı gibi yankılanıyor.
tr.back:
Simplified forms and bold primary colors are applied.
Instead of pursuing anatomical accuracy, he focuses on the energy of movement; color conveys emotion directly.

In the Red Room, he erased the boundary between the table and the wall, choosing the harmony of color surfaces instead of perspective.
Colors echo like a song in front of his canvas.

tr.original:
Chagall's dreamlike atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel-like colors has been applied.
Bouquets, lovers, and hometown scenes blend together like memories, lifting the weight of reality.

Chagall composed his canvases with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space halt before love, and the world fills with dreamlike light.
tr.translated:
Pembe, kobalt mavisi ve mücevher renklerinin rüya gibi atmosferi uygulandı.
Buketler, âşıklar ve memleket manzaraları anılar gibi kaynaşıyor; gerçekliğin ağırlığından arınıyor.

Kompozisyonlarını perspektif ya da anatomi yerine duygunun mantığıyla kurdu.
Tablolarında zaman ve mekân aşkın karşısında duruyor; dünya rüya gibi bir ışıkla doluyor.
tr.back:
The dreamy atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue and jewel colors is applied.
Bouquets, lovers and landscapes of the country fuse like memories, free from the weight of reality.

He built his compositions with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space stand against love; the world is filled with a dreamlike light.

tr.original:
Picasso's Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Like Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple viewpoints coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, Picasso conveyed the terror of war immediately. This destructive deconstruction was itself creation.
Five centuries of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.
tr.translated:
Nesneleri geometrik düzlemlere ayıran ve cephe ile profili aynı anda gösteren Kübist teknikler uygulandı.
Dora Maar Portresi'ndeki gibi çoklu bakış açıları tek bir yüzde bir arada yaşıyor.

Guernica'da yalnızca siyah beyazla savaşın dehşetini doğrudan aktardı; bu yıkıcı yapıbozum kendi başına yaratımdı.
Beş yüz yıllık perspektif kuralları onun tuvalinde paramparça oldu.
tr.back:
Cubist techniques were applied, separating objects into geometric planes and showing facade and profile simultaneously.
As in Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple perspectives coexist in a single face.

In Guernica he directly conveyed the horrors of war in black and white alone; this devastating deconstruction was a creation in itself.
Five hundred years of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.

tr.original:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and communion with animals have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird, thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys as family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not mere backgrounds but companions offering comfort amid suffering.
tr.translated:
Meksika geleneksel renkleri, tropikal yapraklar ve hayvanlarla uyum uygulandı.
Dikenli Kolye ve Sinek Kuşu Otoportresi'nde dikenler acıyı, ölü sinek kuşu yitik aşkı simgeler.

Çocuk sahibi olamayan Frida, papağanlarına ve maymunlarına ailesi gibi davrandı.
Otoportrelerinde hayvanlar sıradan birer arka plan değil, acı içinde teselli sunan yoldaşlardır.
tr.back:
Mexican traditional colors are harmonized with tropical leaves and animals.
In Necklace of Thorns and Self-Portrait of a Hummingbird, the thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird symbolizes lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys like family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not just backgrounds, but companions who offer solace in pain.

tr.original:
Lichtenstein's Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines, and primary colors have been applied.
Like the glamorous couple In the Car, every image becomes a comic book panel.

Lichtenstein's dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of comic heroines, everyday moments transform into dramatic scenes.
Emotion exploding within mechanical dots and lines—that is Lichtenstein's paradox.
tr.translated:
Baskı çizgi romanlarının Ben-Day noktaları, kalın siyah kontur çizgileri ve ana renklerle Pop Art teknikleri uygulandı.
Arabada'daki çekici çift gibi her görüntü bir çizgi roman karesine dönüşüyor.

Çizgi roman kadın kahramanlarının dramatik ifadeleri ve konuşma balonları gibi günlük anlar dramatik sahnelere dönüşüyor.
Mekanik noktalar ve çizgiler içinde patlayan duygular — Lichtenstein'ın paradoksu budur.
tr.back:
Pop Art techniques are applied with the Ben-Day dots of print comics, thick black contour lines and primary colors.
Like the attractive couple in In the Car, every image turns into a comic book frame.

Everyday moments become dramatic scenes, like the dramatic expressions of comic book heroines and speech bubbles.
Emotions exploding in mechanical dots and lines - this is Lichtenstein's paradox.

pt.back: Vincent van Gogh (1853-1890)
