pt.translated: Van Gogh
pt.back: Van Gogh

pt.original:
Van Gogh is a defining figure of Post-Impressionism.
He began painting at 27 and produced 900 oil paintings in just ten years, yet only one sold during his lifetime.
Masterpieces like The Starry Night, Sunflowers, and Café Terrace at Night were all born in poverty and mental anguish.

Van Gogh died at 37, but his art profoundly influenced Expressionism and Fauvism.
"I put my heart and soul into my work." — A painter who forged immortal beauty from suffering.
pt.translated:
Van Gogh é o pintor que representa o pós-impressionismo.
Começou a pintar aos 27 anos e produziu 900 telas em apenas dez anos, mas vendeu apenas uma em vida.
Obras-primas como A Noite Estrelada, Girassóis e Terraço do Café à Noite nasceram da pobreza e do sofrimento mental.

Partiu aos 37 anos, mas sua arte influenciou profundamente o expressionismo e o fauvismo.
"Ponho meu coração e minha alma no meu trabalho." — Pintor que forjou beleza eterna a partir do sofrimento.
pt.back:
Van Gogh is the painter who represents post-impressionism.
He began painting at the age of 27 and produced 900 canvases in just ten years, but sold only one in his lifetime.
Masterpieces such as The Starry Night, Sunflowers and Café Terrace at Night were born out of poverty and mental suffering.

He left at the age of 37, but his art had a profound influence on expressionism and fauvism.
"I put my heart and soul into my work." - A painter who forged eternal beauty out of suffering.

pt.translated: Klimt
pt.back: Klimt

pt.original:
Klimt was the leading Art Nouveau painter of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
Born the son of a goldsmith, he applied real gold leaf to canvas, creating an opulent decorative world inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
He left behind golden masterpieces where sensuality and divinity coexist, including The Kiss, Judith, and The Tree of Life.

Klimt founded the Vienna Secession, challenging the conservative art establishment.
"To every age its art, to every art its freedom." — A painter who captured fin-de-siècle beauty in gold.
pt.translated:
Klimt é o artista que representou o Art Nouveau vienense na virada do século.
Filho de um gravador, colou folhas de ouro reais sobre telas e inspirou-se em mosaicos bizantinos para criar um mundo decorativo deslumbrante.
O Beijo, Judite e A Árvore da Vida são obras-primas douradas onde sensualidade e sacralidade coexistem.

Fundou o movimento da Secessão de Viena e desafiou o conservador mundo da arte.
"A cada época a sua arte, à arte a sua liberdade." — Pintor que capturou a beleza da virada do século em ouro.
pt.back:
Klimt is the artist who represented Viennese Art Nouveau at the turn of the century.
The son of an engraver, he pasted real gold leaf onto canvases and drew inspiration from Byzantine mosaics to create a dazzling decorative world.
The Kiss, Judith and The Tree of Life are golden masterpieces where sensuality and sacredness coexist.

He founded the Vienna Secession movement and challenged the conservative art world.
"To every age its art, to art its freedom." - A painter who captured the beauty of the turn of the century in gold.

pt.translated: Munch
pt.back: Munch

pt.original:
Munch is the defining Expressionist painter from Norway.
He lost his mother at five and his sister at fourteen, and the memory of these losses dominated his canvases for life.
His works, including The Scream, Madonna, and The Dance of Life, form "The Frieze of Life" — a cycle of love, death, and anxiety.

Munch declared, "I paint not what I see, but what I saw,", exploring the deepest human emotions.
"Illness, madness, and death were the dark angels that watched over my cradle." — A painter who transformed inner darkness into art.
pt.translated:
Munch é o grande pintor norueguês que representa o expressionismo.
Perdeu a mãe aos cinco anos e a irmã aos catorze; a memória da perda dominou suas telas por toda a vida.
O Grito, Madonna e A Dança da Vida fazem parte da série "Friso da Vida", que explora amor, morte e angústia.

Munch disse: "Não pinto o que vejo, mas o que vi", ao explorar as emoções humanas mais primitivas.
"A doença, a loucura e a morte foram os anjos negros que velaram meu berço." — Pintor que elevou sua escuridão interior à categoria de arte.
pt.back:
Munch is the great Norwegian painter who represents expressionism.
He lost his mother when he was five and his sister when he was fourteen; the memory of the loss dominated his paintings throughout his life.
The Scream, Madonna and The Dance of Life are part of the "Frieze of Life" series, which explores love, death and anguish.

Munch said: "I don't paint what I see, but what I have seen", as he explored the most primitive human emotions.
"Sickness, madness and death were the dark angels that watched over my cradle." - A painter who elevated his inner darkness to the category of art.

pt.translated: Matisse
pt.back: Matisse

pt.original:
Matisse is the leading Fauvist painter of France.
At 21, he began painting with a box of colors his mother gave him while bedridden, later recalling it felt like "discovering paradise."
The 1905 cry of "Wild beasts!" became the birth of Fauvism, and he left behind color-revolutionary masterpieces like The Dance, The Green Stripe, and The Red Room.

Matisse conveyed emotion through pure color alone, pushing the decorative potential of painting to its limit.
"The purpose of color is not to describe form but to express emotion." — A painter who sang joy through color.
pt.translated:
Matisse é o maior representante do fauvismo francês.
Aos 21 anos, acamado, sua mãe lhe deu uma caixa de tintas e ele sentiu "como se tivesse descoberto o paraíso".
Em 1905, com o grito "Feras!", nasceu o fauvismo, deixando obras revolucionárias como A Dança, A Faixa Verde e O Quarto Vermelho.

Matisse transmitiu emoções com cores puras e levou as possibilidades da decoração na pintura ao extremo.
"A finalidade da cor não é a forma, mas a expressão das emoções." — Pintor que cantou a alegria com cores.
pt.back:
Matisse is the greatest representative of French Fauvism.
At the age of 21, bedridden, his mother gave him a box of paints and he felt "as if he had discovered paradise".
In 1905, with the cry "Feras!", Fauvism was born, leaving behind revolutionary works such as The Dance, The Green Lane and The Red Room.

Matisse conveyed emotions with pure colors and took the possibilities of decoration in painting to the extreme.
"The purpose of color is not form, but the expression of emotions." - A painter who sang joy with color.

pt.translated: Chagall
pt.back: Chagall

pt.original:
Chagall was a painter of love and dreams.
Born in a Russian Jewish village, he moved to Paris and never put down his brush through two World Wars and exile.
His love for his wife Bella was a lifelong source of his art, leaving behind masterpieces like The Birthday, La Mariée, and I and the Village — canvases where bouquets and lovers float in midair.

Chagall worked until 97, enchanting the world with the Paris Opéra ceiling and stained glass windows.
"In our life there is a single color, as on an artist's palette, which provides the meaning of life and art — the color of love." — A poet who erased the boundary between dream and reality.
pt.translated:
Chagall é o pintor que colocou o amor e os sonhos sobre a tela.
Nasceu numa aldeia judaica na Rússia e emigrou para Paris; não largou o pincel durante duas guerras mundiais e um exílio.
Sua esposa Bella foi a fonte de sua arte por toda a vida, deixando obras onde buquês e amantes flutuam como O Aniversário, A Noiva e Eu e a Aldeia.

Chagall trabalhou até os 97 anos e encantou o mundo com o teto da Ópera de Paris e seus vitrais.
"Há apenas uma cor em nossas vidas — a cor do amor." — Poeta dos sonhos que apagou os limites entre realidade e fantasia.
pt.back:
Chagall is the painter who put love and dreams on canvas.
He was born in a Jewish village in Russia and emigrated to Paris; he didn't put down his brush during two world wars and an exile.
His wife Bella was the source of his art throughout his life, leaving behind works in which bouquets and lovers float, such as The Anniversary, The Bride and Me and the Village.

Chagall worked until the age of 97 and enchanted the world with the ceiling of the Paris Opera and his stained glass windows.
"There is only one color in our lives - the color of love." - A poet of dreams who blurred the boundaries between reality and fantasy.

pt.translated: Picasso
pt.back: Picasso

pt.original:
Picasso co-founded Cubism and changed the direction of modern art.
In his 91-year life, he left approximately 50,000 works. Legend has it that at 13, his art teacher father saw his talent and broke his own brushes.
He opened Cubism with Les Demoiselles d'Avignon, denounced war with Guernica, and reached the pinnacle of deconstruction with Portrait of Dora Maar.

Through Blue Period, Rose Period, Cubism, and Surrealism, Picasso constantly reinvented himself.
"Good artists copy, great artists steal." — A painter who broke all rules and created new ones.
pt.translated:
Picasso é o revolucionário que fundou o cubismo e mudou os rumos da arte moderna.
Numa vida de 91 anos, deixou cerca de 50.000 obras; aos treze anos, seu pai — professor de arte — depôs o pincel ao ver o talento do filho.
Abriu as portas do cubismo com As Senhoritas de Avignon, condenou a guerra com Guernica e exibiu o ápice da desconstrução com o Retrato de Dora Maar.

Picasso, através da fase azul, fase rosa, cubismo e surrealismo, reinventou-se sem parar.
"Bons artistas copiam, grandes artistas roubam." — Pintor que quebrou todas as regras e criou novas.
pt.back:
Picasso is the revolutionary who founded Cubism and changed the course of modern art.
In a life span of 91 years, he left around 50,000 works; at the age of thirteen, his father - an art teacher - laid down his brush when he saw his son's talent.
He opened the doors to Cubism with The Ladies of Avignon, condemned war with Guernica and exhibited the pinnacle of deconstruction with Portrait of Dora Maar.

Picasso, through the blue phase, the pink phase, cubism and surrealism, reinvented himself endlessly.
"Good artists copy, great artists steal." - A painter who broke all the rules and created new ones.

pt.translated: Frida Kahlo
pt.back: Frida Kahlo

pt.original:
Frida Kahlo is Mexico's most original painter and a feminist icon.
She contracted polio at 6 and at 18 suffered a bus accident that shattered her spine and pelvis, enduring over 30 surgeries throughout her life.
She began painting while bedridden, looking at a mirror on the ceiling — and much of her work, including Me and My Parrots, The Broken Column, and Self-Portrait with Monkeys, are self-portraits.

Frida's art, with its honest portrayal of pain, love, and identity, continues to resonate deeply today.
"Feet, what do I need them for if I have wings to fly?" — A painter who transformed pain into art.
pt.translated:
Frida Kahlo é a pintora mexicana mais original e símbolo do feminismo.
Aos seis anos teve poliomielite, e aos dezoito um acidente de ônibus destruiu sua coluna e pelve; suportou mais de 30 cirurgias.
Acamada, começou a pintar olhando para um espelho preso ao teto; Eu e Meus Papagaios, A Coluna Partida e Autorretrato com Macacos — a maioria de suas obras são autorretratos.

O mundo artístico de Frida, que expressou dor, amor e identidade com honestidade brutal, ainda ressoa hoje.
"Pés, para que os quero, se tenho asas para voar?" — Pintora invencível que elevou a dor à categoria de arte.
pt.back:
Frida Kahlo is the most original Mexican painter and a symbol of feminism.
At the age of six she had polio, and at eighteen a bus accident destroyed her spine and pelvis; she endured more than 30 surgeries.
Bedridden, she began painting by looking at a mirror attached to the ceiling; Me and My Parrots, The Broken Spine and Self-Portrait with Monkeys - most of her works are self-portraits.

Frida's artistic world, which expressed pain, love and identity with brutal honesty, still resonates today.
"Feet, why do I want them if I have wings to fly?" - An invincible painter who elevated pain to the category of art.

pt.translated: Lichtenstein
pt.back: Lichtenstein

pt.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who broke the boundaries of fine art with comic book panels.
When his son pointed to a Mickey Mouse comic and asked, "Can you draw as well as this, Dad?", he actually began transferring comics to canvas.
With In the Car, M-Maybe, and Forget It!, he shook the art world by blowing up comic panels onto massive canvases.

Lichtenstein, with Ben-Day dots and bold outlines, overturned the hierarchy between popular culture and high art.
"I don't draw comics, I paint pictures about comics." — A painter who erased the line between pop culture and fine art.
pt.translated:
Lichtenstein é o mestre do pop art que demoliu as fronteiras entre arte erudita e cultura popular com um único quadro de quadrinhos.
Quando seu filho apontou para uma história em quadrinhos do Mickey Mouse e perguntou "Pai, você consegue pintar tão bem assim?", ele de fato começou a transferir quadrinhos para a tela.
No Carro, Talvez e Esqueça! — levou cenas de quadrinhos para telas gigantes e sacudiu o mundo da arte.

Lichtenstein, com pontos Ben-Day e contornos pretos espessos, inverteu a hierarquia entre cultura popular e arte.
"Não pinto quadrinhos; pinto telas cujo tema são os quadrinhos." — Pintor que apagou a fronteira entre cultura de massa e belas-artes.
pt.back:
Lichtenstein is the master of pop art who demolished the boundaries between erudite art and popular culture with a single comic book painting.
When his son pointed to a Mickey Mouse comic and asked, "Dad, can you paint that well?", he actually started transferring comics to canvas.
In the Car, Maybe and Forget It! - brought comic book scenes to giant screens and shook up the art world.

Lichtenstein, with Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines, inverted the hierarchy between popular culture and art.
"I don't paint comics; I paint canvases whose subject is comics." - A painter who erased the boundary between mass culture and fine art.

pt.original:
The swirling impasto brushstrokes of 〈The Starry Night〉 have been applied.
The intense contrast of cobalt blue and chrome yellow unleashes the energy of the night.

〈The Starry Night〉 was Van Gogh's reimagined vision of the landscape beyond his asylum window at Saint-Rémy.
Rather than painting the actual night sky, he projected his inner turmoil onto the stars.
pt.translated:
As pinceladas em espiral do impasto de A Noite Estrelada foram aplicadas.
O contraste intenso entre o azul cobalto e o amarelo cromo faz explodir a energia da noite.

A Noite Estrelada foi pintada por Van Gogh reconstruindo a cena com a imaginação pela janela do sanatório de Saint-Rémy.
Ele não pintou o céu noturno real, mas projetou seus próprios turbilhões interiores nas estrelas.
pt.back:
The spiral brushstrokes of the impasto in The Starry Night have been applied.
The intense contrast between cobalt blue and chrome yellow makes the energy of the night explode.

The Starry Night was painted by Van Gogh reconstructing the scene with his imagination through the window of the Saint-Rémy sanatorium.
He didn't paint the real night sky, but projected his own inner swirls onto the stars.

pt.original:
The complementary color nightscape technique of 〈Café Terrace at Night〉—without using black—has been applied.
Yellow gaslights and deep blue night sky express darkness through color contrast alone.

〈Café Terrace at Night〉 is the first outdoor night scene Van Gogh painted under starlight at Arles' Place du Forum.
The empty chairs on the terrace and passing figures hold both the stillness and warmth of the night.
pt.translated:
A técnica de cena noturna com cores complementares sem preto de Terraço do Café à Noite foi aplicada.
O lampião a gás dourado e o céu noturno azul profundo expressam a escuridão apenas pelo contraste de cores.

Terraço do Café à Noite é a primeira obra em que Van Gogh pintou o céu estrelado, inspirando depois A Noite Estrelada.
Sob a luz acolhedora do café, as estrelas cintilam como pequenos sóis na noite.
pt.back:
The night scene technique with complementary colors without black from Terraço do Café à Noite was applied.
The golden gas lamp and the deep blue night sky express the darkness through color contrast alone.

Café Terrace at Night is the first work in which Van Gogh painted the starry sky, later inspiring The Starry Night.
Under the warm light of the café, the stars twinkle like little suns in the night.

pt.original:
The subtle tonal variations of a single chrome yellow in 〈Sunflowers〉 have been applied.
Thickly layered paint captures the texture of the petals as they are.

〈Sunflowers〉 is a series Van Gogh painted to decorate the Yellow House in Arles for Gauguin's arrival.
Full blooms, wilted flowers, and seeds together reveal the cycle of life.
pt.translated:
A tinta espessa e os tons de amarelo cromo de Girassóis foram aplicados.
Amarelo sobre amarelo, a tinta tão espessa que ganha uma textura escultórica sobre a tela.

Girassóis é um presente de boas-vindas que Van Gogh pintou para o amigo Gauguin esperando sua visita em Arles.
Da plena floração ao murchamento, vitalidade e dissolução são expressos ao mesmo tempo.
pt.back:
The thick paint and chrome yellow tones of Sunflowers were applied.
Yellow on yellow, the paint so thick that it takes on a sculptural texture on the canvas.

Sunflowers is a welcome gift that Van Gogh painted for his friend Gauguin, waiting for his visit to Arles.
From full bloom to withering, vitality and dissolution are expressed at the same time.

pt.original:
The swirling background and thick impasto brushstrokes of 〈Self-Portrait〉 have been applied.
Cool tones of blue and green convey both solitude and resolve.

〈Self-Portrait〉 was painted when Van Gogh, too poor to afford models, used himself as subject.
The eyes gazing directly forward and tightly closed lips show unwavering will amid suffering.
pt.translated:
As pinceladas curtas e os fundos em espiral do Autorretrato foram aplicados.
A paleta fria e as pinceladas giratórias criam uma atmosfera de tensão psicológica.

O autorretrato nasceu da pobreza: quando Van Gogh não podia pagar um modelo, pintava a si mesmo.
O olhar direto e os lábios cerrados mostram uma vontade inabalável mesmo no sofrimento.
pt.back:
The short brushstrokes and spiral backgrounds of Self-Portrait were applied.
The cold palette and swirling brushstrokes create an atmosphere of psychological tension.

The self-portrait was born out of poverty: when Van Gogh couldn't afford a model, he painted himself.
The direct gaze and clenched lips show an unwavering will even in suffering.

pt.original:
The dynamic curved brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of 〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 have been applied.
Wind-blown wheat and flame-like cypresses convey nature's vital energy.

〈Wheat Field with Cypresses〉 is a landscape Van Gogh repeatedly painted near the asylum at Saint-Rémy.
The golden horizon of the wheat field and the vertical cypress connect earth and sky.
pt.translated:
As pinceladas curvas e dinâmicas e os contrastes de cores complementares de Campo de Trigo com Ciprestes foram aplicados.
Espigões de trigo balançando ao vento e ciprestes erguidos como chamas transmitem o poder vibrante da natureza.

Campo de Trigo com Ciprestes é uma paisagem que Van Gogh pintou repetidas vezes perto do sanatório de Saint-Rémy.
O horizonte de trigo dourado e os ciprestes verticais unem terra e céu.
pt.back:
The curved, dynamic brushstrokes and complementary color contrasts of Wheat Field with Cypresses were applied.
Spikes of wheat swaying in the wind and cypress trees rising like flames convey the vibrant power of nature.

Wheat Field with Cypresses is a landscape that Van Gogh painted repeatedly near the Saint-Rémy sanatorium.
The horizon of golden wheat and the vertical cypresses unite earth and sky.

pt.original:
The gold leaf background and geometric decorative patterns of 〈The Kiss〉 have been applied.
Rectangles on the man's robe contrast with curves and circles on the woman's, creating opposition and harmony.

〈The Kiss〉 is Klimt's masterpiece from his "Golden Phase," inspired by Byzantine mosaics.
The lovers' embrace wrapped in gold holds both the ecstasy of love and the anxiety of its passing.
pt.translated:
O fundo de folhas de ouro e os padrões geométricos decorativos de O Beijo foram aplicados.
Os retângulos nas roupas do homem contrastam com as curvas e círculos nas roupas da mulher, criando oposição e harmonia.

O Beijo foi pintado por Klimt durante seu "período dourado", inspirado nos mosaicos de Bizâncio.
O abraço dos amantes envolvidos em ouro carrega ao mesmo tempo o êxtase do amor e a angústia da efemeridade.
pt.back:
The gold leaf background and decorative geometric patterns of The Kiss were applied.
The rectangles on the man's clothes contrast with the curves and circles on the woman's clothes, creating opposition and harmony.

The Kiss was painted by Klimt during his "golden period", inspired by the mosaics of Byzantium.
The embrace of lovers wrapped in gold carries both the ecstasy of love and the anguish of ephemerality.

pt.original:
The spiral curves and golden decorative patterns of 〈The Tree of Life〉 have been applied.
Triangles, circles, and spirals repeat, creating a rhythmic flow of life's energy.

〈The Tree of Life〉 was created as a dining room mural for the Stoclet Palace in Brussels.
The endlessly branching spiral limbs are a metaphor for birth, death, and the cycle of life.
pt.translated:
As curvas em espiral e os padrões decorativos dourados de A Árvore da Vida foram aplicados.
Triângulos, círculos e espirais se repetem, e a energia da vida flui em ritmo.

A Árvore da Vida é um mural que Klimt criou para a sala de jantar do Palácio Stoclet em Bruxelas.
Os galhos em espiral que se estendem infinitamente são uma metáfora para o nascimento, a morte e o ciclo da vida.
pt.back:
The spiral curves and golden decorative patterns of The Tree of Life have been applied.
Triangles, circles and spirals are repeated, and the energy of life flows in rhythm.

The Tree of Life is a mural that Klimt created for the dining room of the Stoclet Palace in Brussels.
The infinitely extending spiral branches are a metaphor for birth, death and the cycle of life.

pt.original:
The gold leaf decoration and jewel-like sensual colors of 〈Judith〉 have been applied.
Transparent veils and gold necklaces create tension between the sacred and the sensual.

〈Judith〉 is Klimt's reinterpretation of the biblical heroine as a sensual woman.
Half-closed eyes and a provocative smile completely overturn the piety of traditional religious painting.
pt.translated:
A decoração em ouro e as cores sensuais como joias de Judite foram aplicadas.
O véu transparente e o colar dourado criam uma tensão entre o sagrado e o sensual.

Judite é a obra em que Klimt reinterpretou a heroína bíblica como uma mulher sensual.
Os olhos semicerrados e o sorriso provocante invertem a solenidade da pintura religiosa tradicional.
pt.back:
The gold decoration and sensual colors like Judith's jewels were applied.
The transparent veil and gold necklace create a tension between the sacred and the sensual.

Judith is the work in which Klimt reinterpreted the biblical heroine as a sensual woman.
The half-closed eyes and provocative smile invert the solemnity of traditional religious painting.

pt.original:
The undulating curves and distorted forms of 〈The Scream〉 have been applied.
A blood-red sky and rippling background contrast with the straight railing, creating extreme anxiety.

〈The Scream〉 captures the terror Munch felt when the sky turned blood-red during a walk.
The open-mouthed figure's cry is expressed not as sound but as a tremor passing through all existence.
pt.translated:
As curvas ondulantes e as formas distorcidas de O Grito foram aplicadas.
O céu ensanguentado e a paisagem oscilante contrastam com a linha reta do parapeito, criando angústia intensa.

O Grito foi pintado por Munch depois de sentir terror quando o céu ficou vermelho durante um passeio.
O grito da figura de boca aberta não é um som, mas uma vibração que atravessa toda a existência.
pt.back:
The undulating curves and distorted shapes of The Scream were applied.
The bloody sky and the oscillating landscape contrast with the straight line of the parapet, creating intense anguish.

The Scream was painted by Munch after he felt terror when the sky turned red during a walk.
The scream of the open-mouthed figure is not a sound, but a vibration that runs through all of existence.

pt.original:
The red halo and undulating sensual curves of 〈Madonna〉 have been applied.
At the boundary between open and closed eyes, life and death merge into one.

〈Madonna〉 is Munch's reinterpretation of the Virgin Mary as a moment where sensuality and death intersect.
Closed eyes, parted lips, and a red halo reveal human desire rather than holiness.
pt.translated:
O halo vermelho e as curvas ondulantes sensuais de Madonna foram aplicados.
Na fronteira entre os olhos abertos e fechados, vida e morte se unem.

Madonna é a obra em que Munch reinterpretou a Virgem como um momento em que sensualidade e morte se cruzam.
Os olhos fechados, os lábios entreabertos e o halo vermelho revelam não a santidade, mas o desejo humano.
pt.back:
Madonna's red halo and sensual undulating curves were applied.
At the border between open and closed eyes, life and death come together.

Madonna is the work in which Munch reinterpreted the Virgin as a moment when sensuality and death intersect.
The closed eyes, parted lips and red halo reveal not holiness, but human desire.

pt.original:
The symbolic contrast of light and dark colors in 〈The Dance of Life〉 has been applied.
With the central woman in a red dress as axis, innocence and despair are placed on either side.

〈The Dance of Life〉 is Munch's autobiographical work compressing memories of love and heartbreak into one canvas.
Light and dark colors alternate on a single canvas, capturing both the beginning and end of love.
pt.translated:
Os contrastes simbólicos entre cores claras e escuras de A Dança da Vida foram aplicados.
A mulher de vestido vermelho no centro é o pivô; inocência e desespero se distribuem pelos lados.

A Dança da Vida é uma obra autobiográfica em que Munch comprimiu memórias de amor e decepção numa só imagem.
Cores claras e escuras se cruzam na mesma cena, capturando ao mesmo tempo o início e o fim do amor.
pt.back:
The symbolic contrasts between light and dark colors in The Dance of Life have been applied.
The woman in the red dress in the center is the pivot; innocence and despair are distributed on the sides.

The Dance of Life is an autobiographical work in which Munch compressed memories of love and disappointment into a single image.
Light and dark colors intersect in the same scene, capturing both the beginning and the end of love.

pt.original:
The bold color plane division that bisects the face in 〈The Green Stripe〉 has been applied.
Pink, yellow, and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring natural color.

〈The Green Stripe〉 is a portrait Matisse painted of his wife Amélie, drawing cries of "Wild beasts!" at the 1905 Salon.
A single green stripe down the center of the face divides light and shadow through color alone.
pt.translated:
A divisão ousada com planos de cor que atravessam o rosto de A Faixa Verde foi aplicada.
Rosa, amarelo e verde coexistem na pele, ignorando completamente as cores da realidade.

A Faixa Verde é um retrato da esposa de Matisse, Amélie, descrito no Salão de 1905 com "Feras!".
Uma linha verde no centro do rosto separa luz e sombra apenas com cor.
pt.back:
The bold division with planes of color that cross the face of The Green Ribbon has been applied.
Pink, yellow and green coexist on the skin, completely ignoring the colors of reality.

The Green Strip is a portrait of Matisse's wife Amélie, described in the 1905 Salon with "Beasts!".
A green line in the center of the face separates light and shadow with only color.

pt.original:
The intense color planes and decorative flat composition of 〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 have been applied.
The boundary between figure and background dissolves, with color itself creating space.

〈Woman in a Purple Coat〉 is a work where Matisse conveyed emotion through simplified forms and pure color.
The vast color plane of the purple coat dominates the canvas, merging with the background patterns as one.
pt.translated:
Os planos de cor densos e a composição plana decorativa de Mulher com Casaco Roxo foram aplicados.
As fronteiras entre a figura e o fundo se dissolvem, e a própria cor cria o espaço.

Mulher com Casaco Roxo é uma obra em que Matisse transmitiu emoções com formas simplificadas e cor pura.
O grande plano de cor do casaco roxo domina a composição e se funde com os padrões do fundo.
pt.back:
The dense color planes and decorative flat composition of Woman in Purple Coat have been applied.
The boundaries between the figure and the background dissolve, and the color itself creates the space.

Woman in a Purple Coat is a work in which Matisse conveyed emotions with simplified forms and pure color.
The large plane of color of the purple coat dominates the composition and merges with the patterns of the background.

pt.original:
The flat color plane composition that defies perspective in 〈The Red Room〉 has been applied.
The red wall and red table merge into one as arabesque patterns blanket the entire space.

〈The Red Room〉 was originally painted in blue, but Matisse repainted it red just before completion.
Even the view through the window appears as flat decoration, erasing the boundary between inside and out.
pt.translated:
A composição plana de cor que ignora as leis da perspectiva de O Quarto Vermelho foi aplicada.
A parede e a mesa, ambas vermelhas, se fundem, e a arabesca cobre toda a superfície.

O Quarto Vermelho foi pintado originalmente em azul por Matisse, mas pintado de vermelho antes de ser concluído.
Até a vista da janela parece uma decoração plana, apagando os limites entre interior e exterior.
pt.back:
The flat color composition that ignores the laws of perspective in The Red Room has been applied.
The wall and the table, both red, merge, and the arabesque covers the entire surface.

The Red Room was originally painted blue by Matisse, but painted red before it was completed.
Even the view from the window looks like a flat decoration, erasing the boundaries between interior and exterior.

pt.original:
The rough brushstrokes and unnatural skin colors of 〈Portrait of André Derain〉 have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin, with bold primary colors, render the subject in a wild, Fauvist manner.

〈Portrait of André Derain〉 is a portrait of friendship Matisse painted of his fellow artist André Derain.
The two painters exchanged portraits of each other at the same time, sharing their Fauvist experiments together.
pt.translated:
As pinceladas brutas e os tons de pele irreais do Retrato de André Derain foram aplicados.
Sombras verdes e pele laranja com cores primárias intensas retratam a figura num estilo "fauve".

O Retrato de André Derain é um retrato de amizade que Matisse pintou de seu colega pintor André Derain.
Os pintores trocaram retratos no mesmo período, partilhando experiências do fauvismo.
pt.back:
The raw brushstrokes and unreal skin tones of the Portrait of André Derain have been applied.
Green shadows and orange skin with intense primary colors portray the figure in a "fauve" style.

The Portrait of André Derain is a friendship portrait that Matisse painted of his fellow painter André Derain.
The painters exchanged portraits during the same period, sharing experiences of Fauvism.

pt.original:
The dreamlike colors and overlapping images of 〈Lovers with Flowers〉 have been applied.
Pink, cobalt blue, and violet blend together, erasing the boundary between dream and reality.

〈Lovers with Flowers〉 is a work Chagall painted of his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife.
A vivid bouquet of red and white flowers glows against a deep green and navy background, expressing the warmth of love.
pt.translated:
As cores oníricas e as imagens sobrepostas como sonhos de Amantes com Flores foram aplicadas.
Rosa, azul cobalto e violeta se fundem, apagando os limites entre sonho e realidade.

Amantes com Flores é uma obra em que Chagall verteu seu amor por Bella, sua musa e esposa por toda a vida, sobre a tela.
Os amantes sobrepostos como um sonho sobre um fundo real expressam o êxtase do amor.
pt.back:
The dreamlike colors and overlapping dreamlike images of Lovers with Flowers were applied.
Pink, cobalt blue and violet merge, blurring the boundaries between dream and reality.

Lovers with Flowers is a work in which Chagall poured his love for Bella, his lifelong muse and wife, onto the canvas.
The lovers superimposed like a dream on a real background express the ecstasy of love.

pt.original:
The dreamlike contrast of red bouquets and blue night sky in 〈La Mariée〉 has been applied.
Animals and figures blend like apparitions, unfolding a festive fantasy.

〈La Mariée〉 captures the rooftops and church spires of Chagall's hometown Vitebsk as a landscape of memory.
The bride holding a red bouquet, overlaid with the village scene, holds both nostalgia and joy.
pt.translated:
O contraste onírico entre o buquê vermelho e o céu noturno azul de A Noiva foi aplicado.
Animais e figuras se fundem como espectros, espalhando uma fantasia celebratória.

A Noiva captura os telhados e minaretes de Vitebsk, cidade natal de Chagall, como uma cena da memória.
A noiva com seu buquê vermelho sobreposto à cena da aldeia carrega nostalgia e alegria ao mesmo tempo.
pt.back:
The dreamlike contrast between the red bouquet and the blue night sky in The Bride has been applied.
Animals and figures merge like spectres, spreading a celebratory fantasy.

The Bride captures the rooftops and minarets of Vitebsk, Chagall's hometown, as a scene from memory.
The bride with her red bouquet superimposed on the village scene carries nostalgia and joy at the same time.

pt.original:
The multiple perspectives and transparently overlapping images of 〈I and the Village〉 have been applied.
People, animals, and village scenery intermingle dreamlike, defying scale and proportion.

〈I and the Village〉 was painted in Paris as Chagall longed for his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, capturing an endless yearning for home.
pt.translated:
As perspectivas múltiplas e as imagens transparentes sobrepostas de Eu e a Aldeia foram aplicadas.
Seres humanos, animais e a cena da aldeia se misturam como num sonho, ignorando proporções e tamanhos.

Eu e a Aldeia foi pintada por Chagall em Paris, com saudades de sua cidade natal, Vitebsk.
Realidade e memória se fundem numa só tela, carregando uma nostalgia interminável pela terra natal.
pt.back:
The multiple perspectives and overlapping transparent images of Me and the Village have been applied.
Humans, animals and the village scene blend together as if in a dream, ignoring proportions and sizes.

Me and the Village was painted by Chagall in Paris, missing his hometown of Vitebsk.
Reality and memory merge on a single canvas, carrying an endless nostalgia for his homeland.

pt.original:
The simultaneous front and side view of 〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 has been applied.
Intense primary color planes divide the face geometrically, creating Cubist tension.

〈Portrait of Dora Maar〉 was painted by Picasso of his lover and fellow photographer Dora Maar.
The seated woman's front and side coexist in one face, revealing the complexity within.
pt.translated:
A expressão simultânea do rosto e do perfil do Retrato de Dora Maar foi aplicada.
Planos de cores primárias intensas dividem geometricamente o rosto, criando tensão cubista.

O Retrato de Dora Maar foi pintado por Picasso para sua amante e fotógrafa Dora Maar.
O rosto e o perfil da mulher sentada coexistem num único rosto, revelando a complexidade do interior.
pt.back:
The simultaneous expression of the face and profile of the Portrait of Dora Maar has been applied.
Planes of intense primary colors geometrically divide the face, creating cubist tension.

The Portrait of Dora Maar was painted by Picasso for his lover and photographer Dora Maar.
The face and profile of the seated woman coexist in a single face, revealing the complexity of the interior.

pt.original:
The intense frontal gaze and Mexican folk colors of 〈Me and My Parrots〉 have been applied.
Vivid reds, greens, and blues fill the canvas alongside tropical foliage.

〈Me and My Parrots〉 is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots are messengers of love in Mexican folklore, and faithful companions in Frida's solitude.
pt.translated:
O olhar direto intenso e as cores do folclore mexicano de Eu e Meus Papagaios foram aplicados.
Vermelho, verde e azul vibrantes preenchem a tela com plantas tropicais.

Eu e Meus Papagaios é um autorretrato em que Frida está cercada de quatro papagaios.
Os papagaios no folclore mexicano são mensageiros do amor, companheiras fiéis na solidão de Frida.
pt.back:
The intense direct look and colors of Mexican folklore from Me and My Parrots were applied.
Vibrant reds, greens and blues fill the canvas with tropical plants.

Me and My Parrots is a self-portrait in which Frida is surrounded by four parrots.
Parrots in Mexican folklore are messengers of love, faithful companions in Frida's solitude.

pt.original:
The frontal gaze and lush tropical background of 〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 have been applied.
Amid dark green leaves, her intense eyes and connected eyebrows reveal her identity.

〈Self-Portrait with Monkeys〉 is a self-portrait Frida painted with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were surrogates for the children she could never have.
pt.translated:
O olhar direto e o fundo tropical exuberante de Autorretrato com Macacos foram aplicados.
Entre folhas verde-escuras, seu olhar penetrante e sobrancelha única revelam sua identidade.

Autorretrato com Macacos foi pintado por Frida com seus macacos de estimação.
Na mitologia mexicana, os macacos simbolizam o desejo, mas para Frida eram substitutos dos filhos que não pôde ter.
pt.back:
The direct gaze and lush tropical background of Self-Portrait with Monkeys were applied.
Among dark green leaves, his penetrating gaze and unique eyebrow reveal his identity.

Self-Portrait with Monkeys was painted by Frida with her pet monkeys.
In Mexican mythology, monkeys symbolize desire, but for Frida they were substitutes for the children she couldn't have.

pt.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of 〈In the Car〉 have been applied.
Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill the canvas like a comic book panel.

〈In the Car〉 was created by enlarging a panel from the comic Girls' Romances onto canvas.
Lichtenstein deliberately removed the speech bubble from the original, leaving only the power of the image instead of narrative.
pt.translated:
Os pontos Ben-Day e os contornos pretos espessos de No Carro foram aplicados.
Apenas cores primárias puras — vermelho, azul e amarelo — preenchem a tela como um quadro de quadrinhos.

No Carro foi criado por Lichtenstein ampliando um quadro da revista de quadrinhos Girls' Romances sobre a tela.
Deliberadamente omitiu o balão de fala do original, deixando apenas o poder da imagem.
pt.back:
The Ben-Day dots and thick black outlines of In the Car have been applied.
Only pure primary colors - red, blue and yellow - fill the canvas like a comic book frame.

In the Car was created by Lichtenstein by enlarging a picture from the comic Girls' Romances onto canvas.
He deliberately omitted the speech balloon from the original, leaving only the power of the image.

pt.original:
The Ben-Day dots and comic-book narrative of speech bubbles in 〈M-Maybe〉 have been applied.
Primary colors fill flatly within thick outlines, recreating the texture of a printed page.

〈M-Maybe〉 was a work Lichtenstein kept in his personal collection, which sold for $30,000—five times the going rate.
It is now displayed as a centerpiece of the Pop Art collection at Museum Ludwig in Cologne, Germany.
pt.translated:
Os pontos Ben-Day e a narrativa do balão de quadrinhos de Talvez foram aplicados.
As cores primárias preenchem planos dentro de contornos espessos, recriando a textura da página impressa.

Talvez é uma obra que Lichtenstein guardou em sua coleção particular e vendeu por 30.000 dólares — cinco vezes o preço de mercado.
Hoje é exposta como peça central da coleção de pop art do Museu Ludwig em Colônia, Alemanha.
pt.back:
The Ben-Day dots and the narrative of Maybe's comic strip were applied.
Primary colors fill planes within thick outlines, recreating the texture of the printed page.

Maybe is a work that Lichtenstein kept in his private collection and sold for 30,000 dollars - five times the market price.
Today it is exhibited as the centerpiece of the pop art collection at the Ludwig Museum in Cologne, Germany.

pt.original:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of 〈Forget It!〉 have been applied.
Thick black outlines fix the form like a comic panel, compressing emotion dramatically.

〈Forget It!〉 is an early masterwork created just after Lichtenstein's first solo show in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran the headline: "Is He the Worst Artist in the U.S.?"—about him.
pt.translated:
Os pontos Ben-Day e os contrastes intensos de cores primárias de Esqueça! foram aplicados.
Os contornos pretos espessos fixam a forma como um quadro de quadrinhos, comprimindo as emoções dramaticamente.

Esqueça! é uma obra inicial criada após a primeira exposição individual de Lichtenstein em 1962.
No mesmo ano, a revista Life publicou uma manchete: "É ele o pior artista da América?" — referindo-se a ele.
pt.back:
The Ben-Day dots and intense primary color contrasts of Forget It! were applied.
The thick black outlines fix the form like a comic book frame, compressing the emotions dramatically.

Forget It! is an early work created after Lichtenstein's first solo exhibition in 1962.
That same year, Life magazine ran a headline: "Is he America's worst artist?" referring to him. - referring to him.

pt.original:
The Ben-Day dots and flat color plane composition of 〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 have been applied.
Red, blue, and yellow separate cleanly within black outlines, as crisp as a printed page.

〈Ohhh...Alright...〉 is a 1964 work Lichtenstein borrowed from issue #88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for $42.6 million at Christie's in 2010, overturning the value of what he once called "used canvas."
pt.translated:
Os pontos Ben-Day e a composição de cor plana de Ahh... Tudo Bem... foram aplicados.
Vermelho, azul e amarelo se separam claramente dentro de linhas pretas, tão nítidos quanto uma página impressa.

Ahh... Tudo Bem... é uma obra de 1964 tirada do número 88 de Secret Hearts.
Foi vendida por 42,6 milhões de dólares na Christie's em 2010, invertendo o valor do que chamava de "tela usada".
pt.back:
The Ben-Day dots and the flat color composition of Ahh... It's OK... have been applied.
Red, blue and yellow separate clearly within black lines, as sharp as a printed page.

Ahh... All Right... is a 1964 artwork taken from issue 88 of Secret Hearts.
It sold for 42.6 million dollars at Christie's in 2010, reversing the value of what it called a "used canvas".

pt.original:
The Ben-Day dots and thick outlines creating flat expression in 〈Still Life〉 have been applied.
Primary color planes simplify objects in a comic-like manner, recreating the texture of print.

〈Still Life〉 is part of a 1970s series in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional genre as homage to Picasso.
"There's no mood in my still lifes—it's just lemons and grapefruits," he said.
pt.translated:
Os pontos Ben-Day e os contornos espessos da expressão plana de Natureza Morta foram aplicados.
Os planos de cores primárias simplificam os objetos num estilo de quadrinhos, recriando a textura da impressão.

Natureza Morta é parte de uma série dos anos 1970 em que Lichtenstein reinterpretou um tema tradicional em homenagem a Picasso.
"Na minha natureza morta não há atmosfera — só limões e toranjas", disse ele.
pt.back:
The Ben-Day dots and thick contours of Still Life's flat expression were applied.
The planes of primary colors simplify the objects in a comic book style, recreating the texture of the print.

Still Life is part of a series from the 1970s in which Lichtenstein reinterpreted a traditional theme in homage to Picasso.
"In my still lifes there is no atmosphere - only lemons and grapefruits," he said.

pt.translated: Estilo Van Gogh
pt.back: Van Gogh style

pt.original:
Van Gogh's thick impasto technique and swirling brushstrokes have been applied.
The intense contrast of yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

Van Gogh left over 40 self-portraits, confronting his inner self most honestly through them.
The eyes gazing directly forward show unwavering will even amid suffering.
pt.translated:
A técnica do impasto espesso e as pinceladas em espiral de Van Gogh foram aplicadas.
O contraste intenso entre amarelo e azul revela as emoções interiores.

Deixou mais de 40 autorretratos, encarando seu mundo interior com total honestidade.
O olhar direto mostra uma vontade inabalável mesmo em meio ao sofrimento.
pt.back:
The technique of thick impasto and Van Gogh's spiral brushstrokes were applied.
The intense contrast between yellow and blue reveals inner emotions.

He left more than 40 self-portraits, facing his inner world with total honesty.
The direct gaze shows an unwavering will even in the midst of suffering.

pt.translated: Estilo Klimt
pt.back: Klimt style

pt.original:
Klimt's real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics have been applied.
Rectangles on men's clothing contrast with curves and circles on women's, expressing both opposition and harmony.

Klimt's spiral curves and ornate decoration wrap the figure in a mystical golden world.
Within this decoration, sensuality and divinity coexist.
pt.translated:
Folhas de ouro reais e padrões geométricos inspirados em mosaicos bizantinos foram aplicados.
Os retângulos nas roupas do homem contrastam com as curvas e círculos nas roupas da mulher, expressando oposição e harmonia.

As curvas em espiral e a decoração suntuosa envolvem a figura num mundo dourado e misterioso.
Nessa decoração, o sensual e o sagrado coexistem.
pt.back:
Real gold leaf and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics were applied.
The rectangles on the men's clothes contrast with the curves and circles on the women's clothes, expressing opposition and harmony.

The spiral curves and sumptuous decoration envelop the figure in a golden and mysterious world.
In this decoration, the sensual and the sacred coexist.

pt.translated: Estilo Munch
pt.back: Munch style

pt.original:
Munch's swirling curves, distorted forms, and intense colors have been applied.
Blood-red skies and wavering landscapes visualize inner emotions.

Munch wrote in his diary that while walking by the Oslo fjord, he "heard an enormous scream pass through nature."
Before his paintings, the colors themselves become vibrations of emotion.
pt.translated:
As curvas giratórias, as formas distorcidas e as cores intensas foram aplicadas.
Céus ensanguentados e paisagens oscilantes transformam as emoções interiores em imagens visíveis.

Escreveu em seu diário que durante um passeio "ouviu um grito enorme atravessar a natureza".
Diante de seus quadros, as próprias cores se transformam em vibrações emocionais que te alcançam.
pt.back:
Swirling curves, distorted shapes and intense colors were applied.
Bloody skies and oscillating landscapes transform inner emotions into visible images.

He wrote in his diary that during a walk he "heard a huge scream go through nature".
In front of his paintings, the colors themselves are transformed into emotional vibrations that reach out to you.

pt.translated: Estilo Matisse
pt.back: Matisse style

pt.original:
Matisse's simplified forms and bold primary colors have been applied.
Rather than anatomical accuracy, focus is on the energy of movement, with color directly conveying emotion.

Just as Matisse erased the boundary between table and wall in The Red Room, he the boundary between table and wall, he chose harmony of color planes over perspective.
Before his canvas, colors sing out.
pt.translated:
As formas simplificadas e as cores primárias ousadas foram aplicadas.
Em vez de precisão anatômica, a energia do movimento; a cor transmite emoções diretamente.

Assim como em O Quarto Vermelho, onde apagou os limites entre mesa e parede, escolheu a harmonia de planos de cor em vez da perspectiva.
Diante de seu quadro, as cores cantam como uma canção.
pt.back:
Simplified shapes and bold primary colors were applied.
Instead of anatomical precision, the energy of movement; color conveys emotions directly.

Just as in The Red Room, where he erased the boundaries between table and wall, he chose the harmony of color planes over perspective.
In front of his painting, the colors sing like a song.

pt.translated: Estilo Chagall
pt.back: Chagall style

pt.original:
Chagall's dreamlike atmosphere of pink, cobalt blue, and jewel-like colors has been applied.
Bouquets, lovers, and hometown scenes blend together like memories, lifting the weight of reality.

Chagall composed his canvases with the logic of emotion rather than perspective or anatomy.
In his paintings, time and space halt before love, and the world fills with dreamlike light.
pt.translated:
Atmosferas oníricas de rosa, azul cobalto e cores como joias foram aplicadas.
Buquês, amantes e paisagens da terra natal se fundem como memórias, libertas do peso da realidade.

Construiu suas composições com a lógica da emoção, não da perspectiva ou da anatomia.
Em seus quadros, o tempo e o espaço param diante do amor, e o mundo se enche de uma luz como sonho.
pt.back:
Dreamlike atmospheres of pink, cobalt blue and jewel-like colors were applied.
Bouquets, lovers and hometown landscapes merge like memories, freed from the weight of reality.

He built his compositions with the logic of emotion, not perspective or anatomy.
In her paintings, time and space stand still in the face of love, and the world is filled with a dream-like light.

pt.translated: Estilo Picasso
pt.back: Picasso style

pt.original:
Picasso's Cubist techniques that deconstruct subjects into geometric planes and show front and side simultaneously have been applied.
Like Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple viewpoints coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, Picasso conveyed the terror of war immediately. This destructive deconstruction was itself creation.
Five centuries of perspective rules shattered on his canvas.
pt.translated:
Técnicas cubistas que desconstroem objetos em planos geométricos e mostram rosto e perfil ao mesmo tempo foram aplicadas.
Como no Retrato de Dora Maar, múltiplos pontos de vista coexistem num só rosto.

Em Guernica, apenas em preto e branco transmitiu o horror da guerra diretamente; essa desconstrução devastadora era em si uma criação.
As regras da perspectiva de quinhentos anos se despedaçaram em sua tela.
pt.back:
Cubist techniques that deconstruct objects into geometric planes and show face and profile at the same time were applied.
As in Portrait of Dora Maar, multiple points of view coexist in a single face.

In Guernica, only black and white conveyed the horror of war directly; this devastating deconstruction was itself a creation.
The five-hundred-year-old rules of perspective shattered on his canvas.

pt.translated: Estilo Frida Kahlo
pt.back: Frida Kahlo style

pt.original:
Frida's traditional Mexican colors, tropical foliage, and communion with animals have been applied.
In Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird, thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Unable to have children, Frida treated her parrots and monkeys as family.
In her self-portraits, animals are not mere backgrounds but companions offering comfort amid suffering.
pt.translated:
As cores tradicionais mexicanas, as folhas tropicais e a convivência com animais foram aplicadas.
No Autorretrato com Colar de Espinhos e Beija-Flor, os espinhos simbolizam a dor e o beija-flor morto representa o amor perdido.

Frida, que não pôde ter filhos, tratava seus papagaios e macacos como família.
Em seus autorretratos, os animais não são apenas fundo, mas companheiros que oferecem consolo em meio ao sofrimento.
pt.back:
Traditional Mexican colors, tropical leaves and living with animals were applied.
In Self-Portrait with Necklace of Thorns and Hummingbird, the thorns symbolize pain and the dead hummingbird represents lost love.

Frida, who was unable to have children, treated her parrots and monkeys like family.
In her self-portraits, the animals are not just background, but companions who offer consolation in the midst of suffering.

pt.translated: Estilo Lichtenstein
pt.back: Lichtenstein style

pt.original:
Lichtenstein's Pop art techniques using Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines, and primary colors have been applied.
Like the glamorous couple In the Car, every image becomes a comic book panel.

Lichtenstein's dramatic expressions and speech bubbles of comic heroines, everyday moments transform into dramatic scenes.
Emotion exploding within mechanical dots and lines—that is Lichtenstein's paradox.
pt.translated:
Técnicas pop art com pontos Ben-Day de quadrinhos impressos, contornos pretos espessos e cores primárias foram aplicadas.
Como o elegante casal em No Carro, cada imagem se transforma num quadro de quadrinhos.

Como as expressões dramáticas das heroínas de quadrinhos e os balões de fala, momentos cotidianos se tornam cenas dramáticas.
Emoções explodem a partir de pontos e linhas mecânicos — este é o paradoxo de Lichtenstein.
pt.back:
Pop art techniques with Ben-Day dots from printed comics, thick black outlines and primary colors were applied.
Like the elegant couple in In the Car, each image is transformed into a comic strip.

Like the dramatic expressions of comic book heroines and speech balloons, everyday moments become dramatic scenes.
Emotions explode from mechanical points and lines - this is Lichtenstein's paradox.

th.translated: วินเซนต์ ฟาน โกะห์ (Vincent van Gogh, 1853–1890)
th.back: Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890)

th.translated: โพสต์อิมเพรสชันนิสม์ · เนเธอร์แลนด์
th.back: Post-Impressionism · Netherlands

th.translated: ความเป็นอมตะที่บานสะพรั่งกลางความทุกข์
th.back: Immortality that blooms amidst suffering

th.translated: วินเซนต์ ฟาน โกะห์ (Vincent van Gogh, 1853–1890)
th.back: Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890)

th.translated: คืนแห่งดวงดาว · ทานตะวัน · ภาพเหมือนตนเอง
th.back: Night of the Stars · Sunflower · Self-Portrait

th.translated: ความหลงใหลจากปลายพู่กันที่หมุนวน
th.back: The Passion from the Swirling Brushstroke

th.translated: คืนแห่งดวงดาว (The Starry Night)
th.back: The Starry Night

th.original: The night sky painted in swirls
th.translated: ย้อมท้องฟ้ายามค่ำด้วยลวดลายหมุนวน
th.back: Dye the night sky with swirling patterns.

th.translated: คาเฟ่เทอร์เรซยามค่ำคืน (Café Terrace at Night)
th.back: Café Terrace at Night

th.original: A warm glow of yellow under starlight
th.translated: คืนสีเหลืองที่สว่างใต้แสงดาว
th.back: A bright yellow night under the stars

th.translated: ทานตะวัน (Sunflowers)
th.back: Sunflowers

th.translated: ดอกไม้แห่งดวงอาทิตย์ สีแห่งความหลงใหล
th.back: The flower of the sun, the color of passion.

th.translated: ภาพเหมือนตนเอง (Self-Portrait, 1889)
th.back: Self-Portrait (Self-Portrait, 1889)

th.original: Gazing into the mirror of the soul
th.translated: จ้องมองวิญญาณในกระจก
th.back: Staring at the soul in the mirror

th.translated: ทุ่งข้าวสาลีกับต้นสน (Wheat Field with Cypresses)
th.back: Wheat Field with Cypresses

th.original: Golden fields dancing in the wind
th.translated: แผ่นดินสีทองแห่งสายลม
th.back: The Golden Land of the Wind

th.translated: กุสตาฟ คลิมท์ (Gustav Klimt, 1862–1918)
th.back: Gustav Klimt (1862–1918)

th.translated: อาร์ต นูโว · ออสเตรีย
th.back: Art Nouveau · Austria

th.translated: ความฝันสีทองแห่งเวียนนาปลายยุค
th.back: The Golden Dream of Vienna in the Late Era

th.translated: กุสตาฟ คลิมท์ (Gustav Klimt, 1862–1918)
th.back: Gustav Klimt (1862–1918)

th.original: The Kiss · Judith · The Tree of Life
th.translated: จูบ · จูดิธ · ต้นไม้แห่งชีวิต
th.back: Kiss · Judith · Tree of Life

th.translated: โลกแห่งประสาทสัมผัสสีทอง
th.back: The Golden Sensory World

th.translated: จูบ (The Kiss)
th.back: The Kiss

th.original: An eternal kiss dissolved in gold
th.translated: จูบนิรันดร์ที่หลอมรวมในทอง
th.back: A kiss of eternity, fused in gold.

th.translated: ต้นไม้แห่งชีวิต (The Tree of Life)
th.back: The Tree of Life

th.original: Golden branches singing the song of life
th.translated: บทเพลงแห่งชีวิตจากกิ่งทองที่เหยียดยาว
th.back: The Song of Life from the Lengthening Golden Branch

th.translated: จูดิธ I (Judith I)
th.back: Judith I

th.original: Between the sacred and sensual, golden allure
th.translated: การล่อลวงสีทองระหว่างความศักดิ์สิทธิ์และกามารมณ์
th.back: The Golden Temptation Between Sacred and Sensual

th.translated: เอดวาร์ด มุงก์ (Edvard Munch, 1863–1944)
th.back: Edvard Munch (1863–1944)

th.translated: เอ็กซ์เพรสชันนิสม์ · นอร์เวย์
th.back: Expressionism · Norway

th.original: An artist of the soul, born from loss
th.translated: จิตรกรแห่งวิญญาณที่เกิดจากการสูญเสีย
th.back: The Painter of Souls Born from Loss

th.translated: เอดวาร์ด มุงก์ (Edvard Munch, 1863–1944)
th.back: Edvard Munch (1863–1944)

th.original: The Scream · Madonna · The Dance of Life
th.translated: เสียงร้อง · มาดอนนา · การเต้นรำแห่งชีวิต
th.back: The Voice · Madonna · The Dance of Life

th.translated: วาดเสียงร้องจากภายใน
th.back: Draw the sound from within.

th.translated: เสียงร้อง (The Scream)
th.back: The Scream

th.original: A soul's cry beneath a blood-red sky
th.translated: เสียงร้องแห่งวิญญาณดังก้องท้องฟ้าสีเลือด
th.back: The cry of the soul echoed across the blood-red sky.

th.translated: มาดอนนา (Madonna)
th.back: Madonna

th.original: A mysterious figure between life and death
th.translated: หญิงลึกลับที่ยืนอยู่ระหว่างชีวิตและความตาย
th.back: The mysterious woman standing between life and death.

th.translated: การเต้นรำแห่งชีวิต (The Dance of Life)
th.back: The Dance of Life

th.original: A waltz of love and loss
th.translated: บอลรูมแห่งชีวิตที่ถักทอจากรักและการสูญเสีย
th.back: The ballroom of life, woven from love and loss.

th.translated: อองรี มาติส (Henri Matisse, 1869–1954)
th.back: Henri Matisse (1869–1954)

th.translated: โฟวิสม์ · ฝรั่งเศส
th.back: Fauvism · France

th.translated: ขับร้องความสุขด้วยสีสัน
th.back: Sing happiness with colors.

th.translated: อองรี มาติส (Henri Matisse, 1869–1954)
th.back: Henri Matisse (1869–1954)

th.original: The Dance · The Red Room · The Green Stripe
th.translated: การเต้นรำ · ห้องสีแดง · เส้นสีเขียว
th.back: Dancing · Red Room · Green Line

th.translated: นักมายากลแห่งสีสัน
th.back: The Magician of Colors

th.translated: เส้นสีเขียว (The Green Stripe)
th.back: The Green Stripe

th.original: A single green line that declared a color revolution
th.translated: ประกาศการปฏิวัติสีด้วยเส้นสีเขียวเส้นเดียว
th.back: Announcement of a Color Revolution with a Single Green Line

th.translated: หญิงในเสื้อโค้ตสีม่วง (Woman in a Purple Coat)
th.back: Woman in a Purple Coat

th.translated: ท่าทางสง่างามที่โอบล้อมด้วยสีสันสดใส
th.back: A graceful demeanor surrounded by vibrant colors.

th.translated: ห้องสีแดง (The Red Room)
th.back: The Red Room

th.original: A feast of decoration ruled by red
th.translated: งานเลี้ยงตกแต่งที่สีแดงครอบครอง
th.back: A party decorated entirely in red.

th.translated: ภาพเหมือนอ็องเดร เดอแร็ง (Portrait of André Derain)
th.back: Portrait of André Derain

th.original: A Fauvist colleague painted in the colors of the wild
th.translated: ภาพเหมือนมิตรสหายด้วยสีแห่งโฟว์
th.back: Portraits of Friends in the Colors of Four

th.translated: มาร์ก ชาร์กาล (Marc Chagall, 1887–1985)
th.back: Marc Chagall (1887–1985)

th.translated: เซอร์เรียลิสม์ · รัสเซีย/ฝรั่งเศส
th.back: Surrealism · Russian/French

th.original: A painter of 100 years of love
th.translated: จิตรกรผู้วาดความรักร้อยปี
th.back: The Painter Who Captured a Century of Love

th.translated: มาร์ก ชาร์กาล (Marc Chagall, 1887–1985)
th.back: Marc Chagall (1887–1985)

th.original: Lovers with Flowers · I and the Village · La Mariée
th.translated: คู่รักกับดอกไม้ · ฉันกับหมู่บ้าน · เจ้าสาว
th.back: Lovers and Flowers · Me and the Village · The Bride

th.translated: กวีแห่งความรักและความฝัน
th.back: Poet of Love and Dreams

th.translated: คู่รักกับดอกไม้ (Lovers with Flowers)
th.back: Lovers with Flowers

th.original: Love glowing warmly within a bouquet
th.translated: ความรักที่เปล่งประกายอบอุ่นในช่อดอกไม้
th.back: A bouquet of flowers radiating warm love.

th.translated: เจ้าสาว (La Mariée)
th.back: The Bride (La Mariée)

th.original: A bride floating between dream and reality
th.translated: เจ้าสาวชุดแดงที่ล่องลอยระหว่างฝันและความจริง
th.back: The bride in red floats between dream and reality.

th.translated: ฉันกับหมู่บ้าน (I and the Village)
th.back: I and the Village

th.original: Memories of home drifting like a dream
th.translated: ความทรงจำบ้านเกิดที่ไหลผ่านดั่งความฝัน
th.back: Memories of my hometown flow like a dream.

th.translated: ปาโบล ปิกัสโซ (Pablo Picasso, 1881–1973)
th.back: Pablo Picasso (1881–1973)

th.translated: คิวบิสม์ · สเปน
